RTL8852D
Offset Bit Access INI Symbol Description Efuse Map ANAPAR
18002000h R_AX_UART_RBR
[31:8] R 0 RSVD RSVD
[7:0] R 0 B_AX_RBR Receive Buffer Register
18002000h R_AX_UART_THR
[31:8] R 0 RSVD RSVD
[7:0] W 0 B_AX_THR Transmit Holding Register
18002000h R_AX_UART_DLL
[31:8] R 0 RSVD RSVD
[7:0] R/W 0 B_AX_DLL Divisor Latch (Low)
18002004h R_AX_UART_DLH
[31:8] R 0 RSVD RSVD
[7:0] R/W 0 B_AX_DLH Divisor Latch (High)
18002004h R_AX_UART_IER
[31:8] R 0 RSVD RSVD
[7] R/W 0 B_AX_IER_PTIME Programmable THRE Interrupt Mode Enable
[6:4] R 0 RSVD RSVD
[3] R/W 0 B_AX_IER_EDSSI Enable Modem Status Interrupt
[2] R/W 0 B_AX_IER_ELSI Enable Receiver Line Status Interrupt
[1] R/W 0 B_AX_IER_ETBEI Enable Transmit Holding Register Empty Interrupt
[0] R/W 0 B_AX_IER_ERBFI Enable Received Data Available Interrupt
18002008h R_AX_UART_IIR
[31:8] R 0 RSVD RSVD
[7:6] R 0 B_AX_FIFOSE FIFOs Enabled. This is used to indicate whether the FIFOs are enabled or disabled.
• 00 = disabled
• 11 = enabled
[5:4] R 0 RSVD RSVD
[3:0] R 1 B_AX_IID Interrupt ID. This indicates the highest priority pending interrupt which can be one of the following types:
• 0000 = modem status
• 0001 = no interrupt pending
• 0010 = THR empty
• 0100 = received data available
• 0110 = receiver line status
• 0111 = busy detect
• 1100 = character timeout
18002008h R_AX_UART_FCR
[31:8] R 0 RSVD RSVD
[7:6] W 0 B_AX_RT RCVR Trigger.
• 00 = 1 character in the FIFO
• 01 = FIFO ¼ full
• 10 = FIFO ½ full
• 11 = FIFO 2 less than full
[5:4] W 0 B_AX_TET TX Empty Trigger.
• 00 = FIFO empty
• 01 = 2 characters in the FIFO
• 10 = FIFO ¼ full
• 11 = FIFO ½ full
[3] W 0 B_AX_DMAM DMA Mode.
• 0 = mode 0
• 1 = mode 1
[2] W 0 B_AX_XFIFOR XMIT FIFO Reset
[1] W 0 B_AX_RFIFOR RCVR FIFO Reset
[0] W 0 B_AX_FIFOE FIFO Enable
1800200Ch R_AX_UART_LCR
[31:8] R 0 RSVD RSVD
[7] R/W 0 B_AX_DLAB Divisor Latch Access Bit
[6] R/W 0 B_AX_BC Break Control Bit
[5] R 0 RSVD RSVD
[4] R/W 0 B_AX_EPS Even Parity Select
[3] R/W 0 B_AX_PEN Parity Enable
• 0 = parity disabled
• 1 = parity enabled
[2] R/W 0 B_AX_STOP Number of stop bits
• 0 = 1 stop bit
• 1 = 1.5 stop bits when DLS (LCR[1:0]) is zero, else 2 stop bit
[1:0] R/W 0 B_AX_DLS Data Length Select
• 00 = 5 bits
• 01 = 6 bits
• 10 = 7 bits
• 11 = 8 bits
18002010h R_AX_UART_MCR
[31:7] R 0 RSVD RSVD
[6] R/W 0 B_AX_SIRE SIR Mode Enable
• 0 = IrDA SIR Mode disabled
• 1 = IrDA SIR Mode enabled
[5] R/W 0 B_AX_AFCE Auto Flow Control Enable
• 0 = Auto Flow Control Mode disabled
• 1 = Auto Flow Control Mode enabled
[4] R/W 0 B_AX_LB LoopBack Bit
[3] R/W 0 B_AX_OUT2 OUT2. This is used to directly control the user-designated Output2 (out2_n) output.
The value written to this location is inverted and driven out on out2_n, that is:
• 0 = out2_n de-asserted (logic 1)
• 1 = out2_n asserted (logic 0)
[2] R/W 0 B_AX_OUT1 OUT1. This is used to directly control the user-designated Output1 (out1_n) output.
The value written to this location is inverted and driven out on out1_n, that is:
• 0 = out1_n de-asserted (logic 1)
• 1 = out1_n asserted (logic 0)
[1] R/W 0 B_AX_RTS Request to Send
[0] R/W 0 B_AX_DTR Data Terminal Ready. This is used to directly control the Data Terminal Ready (dtr_n) output. The value written to this location is inverted and driven out on dtr_n, that is:
• 0 = dtr_n de-asserted (logic 1)
• 1 = dtr_n asserted (logic 0)
18002014h R_AX_UART_LSR
[31:8] R 0 RSVD RSVD
[7] R 0 B_AX_RFE Receiver FIFO Error bit
• 0 = no error in RX FIFO
• 1 = error in RX FIFO
[6] R 1 B_AX_TEMT Transmitter Empty bit
[5] R 1 B_AX_THRE Transmit Holding Register
[4] R 0 B_AX_BI Break Interrupt bit
[3] R 0 B_AX_FE Framing Error bit
• 0 = no framing error
• 1 = framing error
[2] R 0 B_AX_PE Parity Error bit
• 0 = no parity error
• 1 = parity error
[1] R 0 B_AX_OE Overrun error bit
• 0 = no overrun error
• 1 = overrun error
[0] R 0 B_AX_DR Data Ready bit
• 0 = no data ready
• 1 = data ready
18002018h R_AX_UART_MSR
[31:8] R 0 RSVD RSVD
[7] R 0 B_AX_DCD Data Carrier Detect. This is used to indicate the current state of the modem control line dcd_n.
[6] R 0 B_AX_RI Ring Indicator.This is used to indicate the current state of the modem control line ri_n.
[5] R 0 B_AX_DSR Data Set Ready. This is used to indicate the current state of the modem control line dsr_n.
[4] R 0 B_AX_CTS Clear to Send. This is used to indicate the current state of the modem control line cts_n.
[3] R 0 B_AX_DDCD Delta Data Carrier Detect. This is used to indicate that the modem control line dcd_n has changed since the last time the MSR was read.
[2] R 0 B_AX_TERI Trailing Edge of Ring Indicator. This is used to indicate that a change on the input ri_n (from an active-low to an inactive-high state) has occurred since the last time the MSR was read.
[1] R 0 B_AX_DDSR Delta Data Set Ready. This is used to indicate that the modem control line dsr_n has changed since the last time the MSR was read.
[0] R 0 B_AX_DCTS Delta Clear to Send. This is used to indicate that the modem control line cts_n has changed since the last time the MSR was read.
1800201Ch R_AX_UART_SCR
[31:8] R 0 RSVD RSVD
[7:0] R/W 0 B_AX_SCR This register is for programmers to use as a temporary storage space.
18002020h R_AX_UART_LPDLL
[31:8] R 0 RSVD RSVD
[7:0] R/W 0 B_AX_LPDLL
18002024h R_AX_UART_LPDLH
[31:8] R 0 RSVD RSVD
[7:0] R/W 0 B_AX_LPDLH
18002070h R_AX_UART_FAR
[31:1] R 0 RSVD RSVD
[0] R/W 0 B_AX_FAR • 0 = FIFO access mode disabled
• 1 = FIFO access mode enabled
18002074h R_AX_UART_TFR
[31:8] R 0 RSVD RSVD
[7:0] R 0 B_AX_TFR Transmit FIFO Read. These bits are only valid when FIFO access mode is enabled (FAR[0] is set to one).
18002078h R_AX_UART_RFW
[31:10] R 0 RSVD RSVD
[9] W 0 B_AX_RFFE Receive FIFO Framing Error. These bits are only valid when FIFO access mode is enabled (FAR[0] is set to one).
[8] W 0 B_AX_RFPE Receive FIFO Parity Error. These bits are only valid when FIFO access mode is enabled (FAR[0] is set to one).
[7:0] W 0 B_AX_RFWD Receive FIFO Write Data. These bits are only valid when FIFO access mode is enabled (FAR[0] is set to one).
1800207Ch R_AX_UART_USR
[31:5] R 0 RSVD RSVD
[4] R 0 B_AX_RFF Receive FIFO Full. This bit is only valid when FIFO_STAT == YES. This is used to indicate that the receive FIFO is completely full.
• 0 = Receive FIFO not full
• 1 = Receive FIFO Full
[3] R 0 B_AX_RFNE Receive FIFO Not Empty. This bit is only valid when FIFO_STAT == YES. This is used to indicate that the receive FIFO contains one or more entries.
• 0 = Receive FIFO is empty
• 1 = Receive FIFO is not empty
[2] R 0 B_AX_TFE Transmit FIFO Empty. This bit is only valid when FIFO_STAT == YES. This is used to indicate that the transmit FIFO is completely empty.
• 0 = Transmit FIFO is not empty
• 1 = Transmit FIFO is empty
[1] R 0 B_AX_TFNF Transmit FIFO Not Full. This bit is only valid when FIFO_STAT == YES. This is used to indicate that the transmit FIFO in not full.
• 0 = Transmit FIFO is full
• 1 = Transmit FIFO is not full
[0] R 0 B_AX_BUSY UART Busy. This bit is valid only when UART_16550_COMPATIBLE == NO and indicates that a serial transfer is in progress; when cleared, indicates that the DW_apb_uart is idle or inactive.
• 0 = DW_apb_uart is idle or inactive
• 1 = DW_apb_uart is busy (actively transferring data)
18002080h R_AX_UART_TFL
[31:0] R 0 B_AX_TFL Transmit FIFO Level. This is indicates the number of data entries in the transmit FIFO.
18002084h R_AX_UART_RFL
[31:0] R 0 B_AX_RFL Receive FIFO Level. This is indicates the number of data entries in the receive FIFO.
18002088h R_AX_UART_SRR
[31:3] R 0 RSVD RSVD
[2] W 0 B_AX_XFR XMIT FIFO Reset.
[1] W 0 B_AX_RFR RCVR FIFO Reset.
[0] W 0 B_AX_UR UART Reset.
180020A4h R_AX_UART_HTX
[31:1] R 0 RSVD RSVD
[0] R/W 0 B_AX_HTX This register is use to halt transmissions for testing, so that the transmit FIFO can be filled by the master when FIFOs are implemented and enabled.
• 0 = Halt TX disabled
• 1 = Halt TX enabled
180020A8h R_AX_UART_DMASA
[31:1] R 0 RSVD RSVD
[0] W 0 B_AX_DMASA This register is use to perform a DMA software acknowledge if a transfer needs to be terminated due to an error condition.
180020F4h R_AX_UART_CPR
[31:24] R 0 RSVD RSVD
[23:16] R 0 B_AX_FIFO_MODE 0x00 = 0
0x01 = 16
0x02 = 32
to
0x80 = 2048
0x81- 0xff = reserved
[15:14] R 0 RSVD RSVD
[13] R 0 B_AX_DMA_EXTRA 0 = FALSE
1 = TRUE
[12] R 0 B_AX_UART_ADD_ENCODED_PARAMS 0 = FALSE
1 = TRUE
[11] R 0 B_AX_SHADOW 0 = FALSE
1 = TRUE
[10] R 0 B_AX_FIFO_STAT 0 = FALSE
1 = TRUE
[9] R 0 B_AX_FIFO_ACCESS 0 = FALSE
1 = TRUE
[8] R 0 B_AX_ADDITIONAL_FEAT 0 = FALSE
1 = TRUE
[7] R 0 B_AX_SIR_LP_MODE 0 = FALSE
1 = TRUE
[6] R 0 B_AX_SIR_MODE 0 = FALSE
1 = TRUE
[5] R 0 B_AX_THRE_MODE 0 = FALSE
1 = TRUE
[4] R 0 B_AX_AFCE_MODE 0 = FALSE
1 = TRUE
[3:2] R 0 RSVD RSVD
[1:0] R 0 B_AX_APB_DATA_WIDTH 00 = 8 bits
01 = 16 bits
10 = 32 bits
11 = reserved
180020F8h R_AX_UART_UCV
[31:0] R 0 B_AX_UCV ASCII value for each number in the version, followed by *. For example 32_30_31_2A represents the version 2.01*
180020FCh R_AX_UART_CTR
[31:0] R 0x0 B_AX_PID This register contains the peripherals identification code.
18003000h R_AX_MAILBOX_WIFI2BT_DATA_L
[31:0] R/W 0 B_AX_MBOX_WIFI2BT_DATA_L Wifi to BT Maibox Data[31:0]
18003004h R_AX_MAILBOX_WIFI2BT_DATA_H
[31:0] R/W 0 B_AX_MBOX_WIFI2BT_DATA_H Wifi to BT Maibox Data[63:32]
18003008h R_AX_MAILBOX_WIFI2BT_READY
[31:8] $R/W 0 RSVD RSVD
[7] $R/W 0 B_AX_MBOX_OUT_ABORT Mailbox OUT abort
[6:1] $R/W 0 RSVD RSVD
[0] $R/W 0 B_AX_MBOX_ACK_WIFI2BT Mailbox ACK Wifi2BT
0: After BT receive mailbox msg, BT set 0 to ack WIFI
1: After WIFI fliled mailbox msg, WIFI set 1 to notify BT.
18003010h R_AX_MAILBOX_BT2WIFI_DATA_L
[31:0] $R/W 0 B_AX_MBOX_BT2WIFI_DATA_L BT to Wifi Maibox Data[31:0]
18003014h R_AX_MAILBOX_BT2WIFI_DATA_H
[31:0] R 0 B_AX_MBOX_BT2WIFI_DATA_H BT to Wifi Maibox Data[63:32]
18003018h R_AX_MAILBOX_CTRL
[31:1] $RW 0 RSVD
[0] $W1C 0 B_AX_MBOX_ACK_BT2WIFI Mailbox ACK BT2WIFI
18003020h R_AX_EXC_JUMP_ADDR
[31:0] R/W 0x20000100 B_AX_WLCPU_EXC_JUMP_ADDR When an exception occurs, the instruction address changes to this location ( for BEV = 1)
18003024h R_AX_CPU_BOOT_ADDR
[31:0] R/W 0x20000000 B_AX_WLCPU_BOOT_ADDR This address is WLCPU booting address when boot from ROM.
18003028h R_AX_TR9_MSMMUPFN
[31:0] R/W 0x76005412 B_AX_TR9_MSMMUPFN TR9 Msmmupfn
MSMMUPFN: SMMUFPN provide a simple mechanism for virtual address (VA) to physical address (PA) translation, that PA = {PFN[ VA[31:29]], VA[28:0]}
 Reset value is determined by input port cfg_msmmupfn.
1800302Ch R_AX_TR9_TIMER_BASE
[31:0] R/W 0x76005212 B_AX_TR9_TIMER_BASE TR9 timer_base
TIMER_BASE: Internal Timer, it is from primary input cfg_timer_base, 1KB range from TIMER_BASE will be reserved in core.

TIMER_BASE (MTIME): Low 32 bits of 48-bits timer
- Reset = 0, assert MTINT when {timeh, time} >= {timecmph, timecmp}
TIMER_BASE + 0x4: High 16 bits of 48-bits timer
- Reset = 0
TIMER_BASE + 0x8: Low 32 bits of 48-bits timer compare
- Reset = 0xffffffff
TIMER_BASE + 0xC : High 16 bits of 48-bits timer compare
- Reset = 0xffff
TIMER_BASE + 0x10 (MSIP): 1 bit Machine Software Interrupt
- Reset = 0, write 1 to asset MSINT, write 0 to deassert.
18003030h R_AX_CPU_IDMEM_TO_CNT
[31:16] $R/W 0xFA B_AX_CPU_DMEM_TO_CNT_TH When Platform SRAM_Ctrl want to access DMEM and not avariable, a internal counter will increase. After the counter reach this timeout count threshold, SRAM_Ctrl issues the request to make CPU stop accessing DMEM so that SRAM_Ctrl can access DMEM.
Unit: cpu clock cycle
[15:0] $R/W 0xFA B_AX_CPU_IMEM_TO_CNT_TH When Platform SRAM_Ctrl want to access IMEM and not avariable, a internal counter will increase. After the counter reach this timeout count threshold, SRAM_Ctrl issues the request to make CPU stop accessing IMEM so that SRAM_Ctrl can access IMEM.
Unit: cpu clock cycle
[31:0] $R/W 0x8aaa0000 RSVE for ECO
18003038h R_AX_TR9_TIMER_CLK
[31] R/W 0x76005212 B_AX_TR9_TIMER_CLK_EN TR9 timer clock enable
[30:0] $R/W 0 RSVD RSVD
1800303Ch R_AX_APB_HANG_ADDR
[31:0] R/W 0 B_AX_APB_HANG_ADDR When error ind(B_AX_APB_HANG_IND) = 0, update the IO address to this register.
When error ind(B_AX_APB_HANG_IND) != 0, no update the IO address, but no impact on resolving IO register stuck
18003040h R_AX_WDT_CTRL
[31] R/W 0 B_AX_WDT_EN WDT enable bit.
[30] $R/W 0 rsvd
[29] R/W 0 B_AX_WDT_OPT_RESET_PLATFORM_EN When WDT_EN =1 & WDT_CDC count down to zero.
1: Leve2_rstb (CPU, Platform and peripheral) will be reset and released. //No Support, Do not set to 1
0: Leve2_rstb (CPU, Platform and peripheral) won’t be reset
[28] $R/W 0 RSVD RSVD
[27] $R/W 0 RSVD RSVD
[26] $R/W 0 RSVD RSVD
[25] $R/W 0 RSVD RSVD
[24:17] $R/W 0 RSVD RSVD
[16] W1C 0 B_AX_WDT_CLR When this bit is set to 1, WDT_CDC will be set to the value of WDT_COUNT and start to count down.
[15:0] R/W 0x1900 B_AX_WDT_COUNT Set this filed to decide the WDT countdown initial value
Unit: 2560 clock cycle multiplied by MAC clock duration time.
if MAC clock = 80MHz, the unit is 2560x(1/80MHz)=32us
18003044h R_AX_WDT_STATUS
[31:9] $R/W 0 rsvd
[8] R/W1C 0 B_AX_FS_WDT_INT Watchdog Reset interrupt
[7:1]] $R/W 0 rsvd
[0] R/W 0 B_AX_FS_WDT_INT_MSK Enable Watchdog Reset interrupt source
18003048h R_AX_WDT_CDC
[31:16] $R/W 0 rsvd
[15:0] RO 0x1900 B_AX_WDT_CDC WDT Countdown Counter
If WDT_EN = 1, set WDT_CLR = 1 to trigger that WDT_CDC will be set to the value of WDT_COUNT and start to count down. When WDT_CDC countdown to 0, WDT_INT will be triggered to host/fw. CPU, Platform, and peripheral could be reset by option.
1800304Ch R_AX_INT0_CTRL_IND
[31:3] $R/W 0 rsvd
[2:0] RO 0 B_AX_FWD0_INT_IND This bit will be set to 1 when R_AX_FWD(n)_ISR != 0. This bit will be auto clear to 0 when R_AX_FWD0_ISR = 0.


BIT0: n = 0. This bit indicate R_AX_FWD0_ISR status.
18003050h R_AX_INT1_CTRL_IND
[31:0] $W1C 0 B_AX_FWC_INT_IND This bit will be set to 1 when (R_AX_FWC(n)_IMR & R_AX_FWC(n)_ISR) != 0. This bit will be auto clear to 0 when R_AX_FWC(n)_ISR = 0.

BIT31~BIT27: rsvd
BIT26: This bit indicate R_AX_FWC1A_ISR status.
BIT25: This bit indicate R_AX_FWC19_ISR status.
BIT24: This bit indicate R_AX_FWC18_ISR status.
BIT23: This bit indicate R_AX_FWC17_ISR status.
BIT22: This bit indicate R_AX_FWC16_ISR status.
BIT21: This bit indicate R_AX_FWC15_ISR status.
BIT20: This bit indicate R_AX_FWC14_ISR status.
BIT19: This bit indicate R_AX_FWC13_ISR status.
BIT18: This bit indicate R_AX_FWC12_ISR status.
BIT17: This bit indicate R_AX_FWC11_ISR status.
BIT16: This bit indicate R_AX_FWC10_ISR status.

BIT15~BIT11: rsvd
BIT10: This bit indicate R_AX_FWC0A_ISR status.
BIT9: This bit indicate R_AX_FWC09_ISR status.
BIT8: This bit indicate R_AX_FWC08_ISR status.
BIT7: This bit indicate R_AX_FWC07_ISR status.
BIT6: This bit indicate R_AX_FWC06_ISR status.
BIT5: This bit indicate R_AX_FWC05_ISR status.
BIT4: This bit indicate R_AX_FWC04_ISR status.
BIT3: This bit indicate R_AX_FWC03_ISR status.
BIT2: This bit indicate R_AX_FWC02_ISR status.
BIT1: This bit indicate R_AX_FWC01_ISR status.
BIT0: This bit indicate R_AX_FWC00_ISR status.
18003054h R_AX_INT2_CTRL_IND
[31:3] $R/W 0 rsvd
[2:0] RO 0 B_AX_FWD_INT_IND This bit will be set to 1 when R_AX_FWD(n)_ISR != 0. This bit will be auto clear to 0 when R_AX_FWD(n)_ISR = 0.

BIT2: n = 2. This bit indicate R_AX_FWD2_ISR status.
BIT1: n = 1. This bit indicate R_AX_FWD1_ISR status.
BIT0: NA
18003058h R_AX_INT3_CTRL_IND
[31:2] $R/W 0 rsvd
[1:0] RO 0 B_AX_FWS_INT_IND_V1 This bit will be set to 1 when R_AX_FWS(n)_ISR != 0. This bit will be auto clear to 0 when R_AX_FWS(n)_ISR = 0.

BIT1: n = 1. This bit indicate R_AX_FWS1_ISR status.
BIT0: n = 0. This bit indicate R_AX_FWS0_ISR status.
1800305Ch R_AX_INT4_CTRL_IND
[31:3] $R/W 0 rsvd
[2:0] RO 0 B_AX_FWDA_INT_IND This bit will be set to 1 when each IP interrupt status != 0. This bit will be auto clear to 0 when interrupt status = 0.

BIT2: This bit indicate IP_SEC interrupt status. (1800C000[24], 1800C010[4], 1800D000[24])
BIT1: NA
BIT0: This bit indicate IDDMA interrupt status.(180080E8h)
18003060h R_AX_INT5_CTRL_IND
[31:29] W1C 0 B_AX_SUB_SYS_ERR_IND This bit will be set to 1 when each Sub_system error flag != 0. This bit will be auto clear to 0 when each Sub_system error flag = 0. The Sub_Module error flags will be put in self domain. W1C this field also make Sub_Module error flags cleared.

BIT31: This bit indicate Sub_system CMAC1 error status
BIT30: This bit indicate Sub_system CMAC0 error status
BIT29: This bit indicate Sub_system DMAC error status
[28] W1C 0 B_AX_APB_HANG_IND Once bus gets stuck, HW will trigger an interrupt with FWISR bit. And FW can find out the IO stuck address by checking R_AX_APB_HANG_ADDR. This backdoor is enabled in AON reg 0x1D0[15].
[27] RO 0 B_AX_SUB_BB_ERR_IND_HIGH0 This bit will be set to 1 when BB error interrupt status != 0. This bit will be auto clear to 0 when interrupt status = 0. (High priority BB Band 0)
[26] RO 0 B_AX_SUB_BB_ERR_IND_LOW0 This bit will be set to 1 when BB error interrupt status != 0. This bit will be auto clear to 0 when interrupt status = 0. (Low priority BB Band 0)
[25] RO 0 B_AX_SUB_BB_ERR_IND_HIGH1 This bit will be set to 1 when BB error interrupt status != 0. This bit will be auto clear to 0 when interrupt status = 0. (High priority BB Band 1)
[24] RO 0 B_AX_SUB_BB_ERR_IND_LOW1 This bit will be set to 1 when BB error interrupt status != 0. This bit will be auto clear to 0 when interrupt status = 0. (Low priority BB Band 1)
[23] W1C 0 B_AX_AHB_HANG_IND Once bus gets stuck, HW will trigger an interrupt with FWISR bit. And FW can find out the IO stuck address by checking R_AX_AHB_HANG_ADDR. This backdoor is enabled in AON reg 0x1D0[23].
[22] W1C 0 B_AX_HCI_ILLEGAL_CMD_IND ISR of hci mater illegal cmd interrupt
[21:0] R/W 0 rsvd
18003064h R_AX_INT6_CTRL_IND
[31:1] $R/W 0 rsvd
[0] RO 0 B_AX_RXI300_INT_IND This bit will be set to 1 when each IP interrupt status != 0. This bit will be auto clear to 0 when interrupt status = 0.

BIT0: This bit indicate rxi300 bus error interrupt status.(0x18005030)
18003068h R_AX_INT7_CTRL_IND
[31:1] $R/W 0 rsvd
[0] RO 0 B_AX_PAXIDMA_INT_IND This bit will be set to 1 when each IP interrupt status != 0. This bit will be auto clear to 0 when interrupt status = 0.

BIT0: This bit indicate AXIDMA interrupt status.(18006904h)
1800306Ch R_AX_INT8_CTRL_IND
[31:2] $R/W 0 rsvd
[1:0] RO 0 B_AX_FWP_INT_IND_V1 This bit will be set to 1 when each IP interrupt status != 0. This bit will be auto clear to 0 when interrupt status = 0.

BIT1: This bit indicate SPIC interrupt status. (0x18004030[11:0])
BIT0: This bit indicate UART interrupt status. (0x18002008[7:0])
18003070h R_AX_SUB_BB_ERR_IMR
[31:28] $R/W 0 rsvd
[27] R/W 0 B_AX_SUB_BB_ERR_IMR_HIGH0 This bit set 1 indicated B_AX_SUB_BB_ERR_ISR_HIGH0 interrupt enable. (High priority BB Band 0)
[26] R/W 0 B_AX_SUB_BB_ERR_IMR_LOW0 This bit set 1 indicated B_AX_SUB_BB_ERR_IMR_LOW0 interrupt enable. (Low priority BB Band 0)
[25] R/W 0 B_AX_SUB_BB_ERR_IMR_HIGH1 This bit set 1 indicated B_AX_SUB_BB_ERR_IMR_HIGH1 interrupt enable. (High priority BB Band 1)
[24] R/W 0 B_AX_SUB_BB_ERR_IMR_LOW1 This bit set 1 indicated B_AX_SUB_BB_ERR_IMR_LOW1 interrupt enable. (Low priority BB Band 1)
[23:0] $R/W 0 rsvd
18003074h R_AX_SW_ISR_CTRL
[31] R/W 0 B_AX_SW_ISR_EN SW ISR enable bit.
[30] $R/W 0 RSVD RSVD
[29] $R/W 0 RSVD RSVD
[28] $R/W 0 RSVD RSVD
[27] $R/W 0 RSVD RSVD
[26] $R/W 0 RSVD RSVD
[25] $R/W 0 RSVD RSVD
[24:17] $R/W 0 RSVD RSVD
[16] W1C 0 B_AX_SW_ISR_CLR When this bit is set to 1, SW_ISR_CDC will be set to the value of SW_ISR_COUNT and start to count down.
[15:0] R/W 0x1900 B_AX_SW_ISR_COUNT Set this filed to decide the SW_ISR countdown initial value
Unit: depends on CPU clock, if CPU clock is 240MHz, the unit is 1/240MHz.
18003078h R_AX_SW_ISR_STATUS
[31:9] $R/W 0 RSVD
[8] R/W1C 0 B_AX_FS_SW_ISR_INT Software interrupt
[7:1]] $R/W 0 RSVD
[0] R/W 0 B_AX_FS_SW_ISR_MSK Enable software interrupt source
1800307Ch R_AX_SW_ISR_CDC
[31:16] $R/W 0 rsvd
[15:0] RO 0x1900 B_AX_SW_ISR_CDC SW ISR Countdown Counter
If B_AX_SW_ISR_EN = 1, set SW_ISR_CLR = 1 to trigger that SW_ISR_CDC will be set to the value of SW_ISR_COUNT and start to count down. When SW_ISR_CDC countdown to 0, SW_ISR_INT will be triggered to CPU.
18003080h R_AX_APB_AHB
[31:30] $R/W 0 RSVD RSVD
[29] RW 1 B_AX_AHB_FIXHANG_IMR Setting this bit to 0 masks AHB hang interrupt
[28] RW 1 B_AX_APB_FIXHANG_IMR Setting this bit to 0 masks APB hang interrupt
[27] RW 1 B_AX_HCI_ILLEGAL_CMD_IMR IMR of hci mater illegal cmd interrupt
[26:24] $R/W 0 RSVD RSVD
[23:16] $R/W 0 RSVD RSVD
[15:10] $R/W 0 RSVD RSVD
[9:0] RW 3FF B_AX_APB_SLAVE_EN apb slave enable
(9: WLAN_VIR, 8: RVSD, 7:HCI_DBG, 6:IPSEC, 5:IDDMA, 4:HIOE, 3:TOPOFF, 2: P_AXIDMA, 1:CMAC, 0:DMAC)
[31:0] $R/W 0xFFFF0000 RSVD RSVD for ECO
1800308Ch R_AX_AHB_HANG_ADDR
[31:0] RO 0 B_AX_AHB_HANG_ADDR AHB hang address
1800309Ch R_AX_PHYREG_SET_V1
[31:11] $R/W 0 Rsvd
[10:9] R/W 1 B_AX_PHYREG_SET_F PHYREG access cycle
Enlarge multiplier of access cycles.
F = 0: access 80M cycles = (Y+N+X) * 0.5
F = 1: access 80M cycles = (Y+N+X) * 1
F = 2: access 80M cycles = (Y+N+X) * 2
F = 3: access 80M cycles = (Y+N+X) * 4
[8:6] R/W 2 B_AX_PHYREG_VALUE_X Counter value for X
[5:3] R/W 3 B_AX_PHYREG_VALUE_N Counter value for N
[2:0] R/W 4 B_AX_PHYREG_VALUE_Y Counter value for Y
180030A0h R_AX_BIST_MCU_CTRL1
[31:16] RO 0 RSVD RSVD
[15] RW 0 B_AX_BIST_TMCK_W Wlmcu bist tmck
[14] RW 0 B_AX_BIST_DYN_READ_EN Wlmcu bist dyn read enable
[13] RW 0 B_AX_BIST_LOOP_MODE Wlmcu bist loop mode
[12] RW 0 B_AX_BIST_LVDRF_CLKDIS Wlmcu bist lvdrf clk disable
[11:8] RW 0 B_AX_BIST_ROM_MISR_SEL Wlmcu bist rom misr output select
[7:4] $R/W 0 RSVD RSVD
[3] RW 0 B_AX_BIST_DRF_RESUME Wlmcu bist drf test resume
[2] RW 0 B_AX_BIST_DRF_MODE Wlmcu bist drf mode
[1] RW 0 B_AX_BIST_MODE Wlmcu bist bist mode
[0] RW 0 B_AX_BIST_RESET_ALL Wlmcu bist reset all
180030A4h R_AX_BIST_MCU_CTRL2
[31:30] RW 0 B_AX_WLMCU_MEM_RMV_FABDBG Wlmcu read margin ctrl FABDBG
[29] RW 0 B_AX_WLMCU_RMV_SIGN Wlmcu read margin ctrl SIGN
[28] RO 0 RSVD RSVD
[27] RW 0 B_AX_WLMCU_RMV_2PRF Wlmcu read margin ctrl for 2PRF
[26] RW 0 B_AX_WLMCU_RMV_1PRF Wlmcu read margin ctrl for 1PRF
[25] RW 0 B_AX_WLMCU_RMV_1PSR Wlmcu read margin ctrl for 1PSR
[24] RW 0 B_AX_WLMCU_RMV_ROM Wlmcu read margin ctrl for ROM
[23:8] RO 0 RSVD RSVD
[7:4] RW 0 B_AX_WLMCU_READ_MARGIN_EN Wlmcu read margin enable for 2PRF,1PRF,1PSR,ROM
[3:0] RO 0 RSVD RSVD
180030A8h R_AX_BIST_MCU_RESET
[31:27] RO 0 RSVD RSVD
[26:0] RW 0 B_AX_WLMCU_MBIST_RESET Wlmcu bist reset
180030ACh R_AX_BIST_MCU_DONE
[31:27] RO 0 RSVD RSVD
[26:0] RW 0 B_AX_WLMCU_MBIST_DONE Wlmcu bist done
180030B0h R_AX_BIST_MCU_FAIL
[31:27] RO 0 RSVD RSVD
[26:0] RW 0 B_AX_WLMCU_MBIST_FAIL Wlmcu bist fail
180030B4h R_AX_BIST_MCU_DRF_PAUSE
[31:27] RO 0 RSVD RSVD
[26:0] RW 0 B_AX_WLMCU_MBIST_DRF_PAUSE Wlmcu bist drf pause
180030B8h R_AX_BIST_MCU_ROM_MISR_OUT
[31:0] RO 0 B_AX_WLMCU_MBIST_ROM_MISR_OUT Wlmcu bist rom misr out
18004000h R_AX_SPIC_CTRLR0
[31] R/W 0 B_AX_SPIC_USER_MODE User mode bit. Enable to enter user mode. Disable to enter auto mode. It can not be changed while SPIC is busy (0x28 [0] BUSY).

1: User mode. User can push data to FIFO and set SSIENR to 1. SPIC won't accept auto read/write command.

0: Auto mode. User can't push data to FIFO and set SSIENR to 1. SPIC will accept auto read/write command.
[30] R/W 0 B_AX_SPIC_UAR User mode bit auto reset. Enable to auto reset USER_MODE to 0 after current user mode transaction is over (SPIC_EN reset to 0).
[29:28] N/A 0 RSVD RSVD
[27:23] R/W ATCK_MTIMES B_AX_SPIC_CK_MTIMES Indicate the check times. If Flash is always busy in auto-check times, it causes ACEIR interrupt. The delay time (cycles) is related to bus_clk.
The cycles of the parameter = (CK_MTIMES << (11+ATCK_BIT_EXTEND).). User should ensure the timeout > Write time of Flash
The timeout time(ns) =
2*BAUDR*(1/SPIC_freq)(8/CH) (CK_MTIMES << (11+ATCK_BIT_EXTEND).)
EXAMPLE1:
Flash Program time= 1ms, BAUDR=1, CH=1 (SPI) and (1/SPIC_freq) = 10ns. ATCK_MTIMES should be to set 4.
EXAMPLE2:
SPIC_freq = 200MHz, BAUDR = 1, CH =4 (QSPI), and ATCK_BIT_EXTEND = 2, the maximum auto_check time should be
= 2*1*(1/200MHz)(8/4) (5'b11111<<13)
= 2*1*(1/200MHz)(8/4) (18'b11_1110_0000_0000_0000)
= 2*1*(1/200MHz)(8/4) (18'h3_e000)
= 2*1*(1/200MHz)(8/4) (253952)
= 5079040 ns
= 5.07 ms

Suggestion: If ACEIR interrupt occurs, the user should check the flash status by the software itself.
Note: Before SVN 8369 Version, CK_MTIMES is fixed to shift 10bit; The formula become2*BAUDR*(1/SPIC_freq)(8/CH) (CK_MTIMES << (10))
[22] R/W 0 B_AX_SPIC_FAST_RD Indicate to use fast read command in user mode. If setting to 1, SPIC would use FBAUDR to generate spi _sclk.
[21:20] R/W 0 B_AX_SPIC_CMD_CH Indicate channel number of command phase in transmitting or receiving data. Command phase is usually used to send SPI command.
0: single channel,
1: dual channels,
2: quad channels,
3: octal channels.
[19:18] R/W 0 B_AX_SPIC_DATA_CH Indicate channel number of data phase in transmitting or receiving data. Data phase is used to send data after address phase.
0: single channel,
1: dual channels,
2: quad channels,
3: octal channels.
[17:16] R/W 0 B_AX_SPIC_ADDR_CH Indicate channel number of address phase after command phase. Addr phase is used to send address or data Addr phase is between one byte command and a data phase. The number of bytes is determined by the ADDR_LENGTH 0: single channel,
1: dual channels,
2: quad channels,
3: octal channels.
[15:13] R/W 0 B_AX_SPIC_DDR_EN_V1 Indicate the DDR mode in CMD_CH/DATA_CH/ADDR_CH.
CTRLR0[15]: CMD_CH (always 2-Byte CMD type)
CTRLR0[14]: DATA_CH
CTRLR0[13]: ADDR_CH
0: Disable; 1: Enable.
[12:11] N/A 0 RSVD RSVD
[10] R/W 0 B_AX_SPIC_GCLK_DIS Set to disable gated clock of icg cell
[9:8] R/W 0 B_AX_SPIC_TMOD Indicate transfer mode.
2b 00: transmit mode, others (or 2 b11): receive mode
[7] R/W SCPOL_DEF B_AX_SPIC_SCPOL Indicate serial clock polarity. I t is used to select the polarity of the inactive serial clock.
0: inactive state of serial clock is low
1: inactive state of serial clock is high
[6] R/W SCPH_DEF B_AX_SPIC_SCPH Indicate serial clock phase. The serial clock phase selects the relationship the serial clock with the slave select signal.
0: serial clock toggles in middle of first data bit
1: serial clock toggles at start of first data bit
[5] R/W 0 B_AX_SPIC_SIPOL_EN Set to enable SIPOL
[4:0] R/W 0x1F B_AX_SPIC_SIPOL While SIPOL_EN = 1 :
SPI_CS is inactive state(SPI_CS ==1): spi_sin[3:0] = SIPOL[3:0]
SPI_CS is active(SPI_CS ==0): spi_sin[3:2] = SIPOL[3:2] (single or dual channel)
(SIPOL[4]: reserved for spi_in[7:4])
18004000h R_AX_SPIC_DR
[31:0] R/W 0 B_AX_SPIC_DR It is a data buffer with 8-bit width FIFO. If accessing in word data byte, it would read/write 4 entries of FIFO.
18004000h R_AX_SPIC_DM_DR
[31:2] N/A 0 RSVD RSVD
[1] W 0 B_AX_SPIC_DATA_EN_DR It is a data buffer for spi_data_en signal in user mode. The output value of spi_data_en is the data that has been pushed to DM_FIFO [1]. If DM_FIFO is empty, the output value of spi_data_en will be 0.
[0] W 0 B_AX_SPIC_DATA_MASK_DR It is a data buffer for spi_dm signal in user mode. The output value of spi_dm is the data that has been pushed to DM_FIFO [0]. If DM_FIFO is empty, the output value of spi_data_en will be (~DM_ACT).
18004000h R_AX_SPIC_ST_DR
[31:8] N/A 0 RSVD RSVD
[7:0] R 0 B_AX_SPIC_ST_DR It is a data buffer with 8-bit width FIFO.
18004004h R_AX_SPIC_RX_NDF
[31:16] N/A 0 RSVD RSVD
[15:0] R/W 0 B_AX_SPIC_RX_NDF Indicate a number of data frames (Unit: Byte). When executing receives operation in user mode, SPIC receives data continuously until data frames are equal to RX_NDF.
18004008h R_AX_SPIC_SSIENR
[31:5] N/A 0 RSVD RSVD
[4] W 0 B_AX_SPIC_PGM_RST_TEST_EN For PGM RST Test only. Set this bit will generate a pulse as a warm reset signal and run PGM RST flow.
[3:2] N/A 0 RSVD RSVD
[1] R/W 0 B_AX_SPIC_ATCK_CMD Set to enable ATCK_CMD implementation. After setting SPIC would not accept any command until checking Flash is not busy or checking time out. (Use in User Mode, especially for Erase/Program to check status automatically)
If CTRLR2.SEQ_SET setting, this function needs to disable.
[0] R/W 0 B_AX_SPIC_SPIC_FUNC_EN Set to enable SPIC, else to disable SPIC.
18004010h R_AX_SPIC_SER
[31:1] N/A 0 RSVD RSVD
[0] R/W 0x1 B_AX_SPIC_SER Each bit in the register corresponds to one SPI Flash. In user mode user program the register to select target flash
0: Not selected
1: selected
18004014h R_AX_SPIC_BAUDR
[31:12] N/A 0 RSVD RSVD
[11:0] R/W See Description B_AX_SPIC_SCKDV Define spi_sclk divider value. The frequency of spi_sclk is derived from:
The frequency of spi_sclk = The frequency of bus_clk / (2*SCKDV)
About Init:
Check SPIC frequency in configure form * *: (spic frequency) / 32 ex: spic freq=120 MHz, SCKDV= ⌊120/32⌋ = ⌊3.75⌋ =3
18004018h R_AX_SPIC_TXFTLR
[31:7] N/A 0 RSVD RSVD
[6:0] R/W 0 B_AX_SPIC_TFT Transmit FIFO threshold. To Control FIFO entries of valid data in normal transfer except in receiving mode. When a number of FIFO entry is equal or smaller than TFT, SPIC triggers a spi_txeir interrupt.
Ex: If TXFTLR = 5'h1,
Set spi_txeir when FIFO entries <= TFT (0x1)
1800401Ch R_AX_SPIC_RXFTLR
[31:7] N/A 0 RSVD RSVD
[6:0] R/W 6 B_AX_SPIC_RFT Receive FIFO threshold. To Control FIFO entries of valid data in receiving mode When a number of FIFO entry is greater than RFT, SPIC triggers a spi_rxfir interrupt.
18004020h R_AX_SPIC_TXFLR
[31:8] N/A 0 RSVD RSVD
[7:0] R 0 B_AX_SPIC_TXFLR Transmit FIFO level. Indicate the FIFO entry level of valid data in normal mode except in receiving data. (or as FIFO_FLR in any mode)
18004024h R_AX_SPIC_RXFLR
[31:8] N/A 0 RSVD RSVD
[7:0] R 0 B_AX_SPIC_RXFLR Receive FIFO level. Indicate the FIFO entries of valid data in receiv ing data.
18004028h R_AX_SPIC_SR
[31:9] N/A 0 RSVD RSVD
[8] R/W 0 B_AX_SPIC_ATWR_RDSR_N Boot Finish. Set if count waiting cycles Boot Delay Count for SPI Flash becomes a stable state after power on (or system reset).
1: Boot Finish
Note: Auto_mode would be blocked until boot finish. User_mode is allowed with SSIENR inactive before boot finish.
[7] R 0 B_AX_SPIC_BOOT_FIN (Not Yet Ready)
Boot Finish. Set if count waiting cycles (Boot Delay Count) for SPI Flash becomes a stable state after power on (or system reset). 1: Boot Finish Note: Auto_mode would be blocked until boot finish. User_mode is allowed with SSIENR inactive before boot finish.
[6] R 0 B_AX_SPIC_DCOL Data Collision, or in Transmitting Status. 1: Status shows that SPIC is transmitting spi_flash_cmd/ spi_flash_addr/spi_flash_data to SPI Flash. Suggest not reading DR during this transmitting state. (Check this status can avoid reading wrong data and cause SPIC error.)
[5] R 0 B_AX_SPIC_TXE Transmission error. Set if FIFO is empty and starting to transmit data to SPI Flash. This bit is cleared when read.
[4] R 0 B_AX_SPIC_RFF Receive FIFO full. Set if FIFO is full in receiving mode. This bit is cleared when read.
[3] R 0 B_AX_SPIC_RFNE Receive FIFO is not empty. Set if FIFO is not empty in receiving mode. This bit is cleared when read.
[2] R 1 B_AX_SPIC_TFE Transmit FIFO is empty. Set if FIFO is empty in transmit mode, else it is cleared when it has data in FIFO.
[1] R 1 B_AX_SPIC_TFNF Transmit FIFO is not full. Set if FIFO is not full in transmit mode, else it is cleared when FIFO is full
[0] R 0 B_AX_SPIC_BUSY SPIC busy flag. Set if SPIC is still transmitting to or receiving data from SPI Flash, or TX_FIFO/RX_FIFO are not empty.
1800402Ch R_AX_SPIC_IMR
[31:17] R 0 RSVD RSVD
[16] R/W 0 B_AX_SPIC_NWEIM NAND Flash write error interrupt mask.
1: spi_nweir_r is not masked.
0: spi_nweir_r is masked.
[15] R/W 0 B_AX_SPIC_STFIM Status FIFO full interrupt mask.
1: spi_stfir is not masked.
0: spi_stfir is masked.
[14] R/W 0 B_AX_SPIC_STOIM Status FIFO overflow interrupt masked.
1: spi_stoir_r is not masked.
0: spi_stoir_r is masked.
[13] R/W 0 B_AX_SPIC_STUIM Status FIFO underflow interrupt masked.
1: spi_stuir_r is not masked.
0: spi_stuir_r is masked.
[12] R/W 0 B_AX_SPIC_DREIM DR timeout error interrupt mask.
1: spi_dreir_r is not masked.
0: spi_dreir_r is masked.
[11] R/W 0 B_AX_SPIC_ACSIM Auto-check Flash Status raw interrupt mask.
1: spi_acsir_r is not masked.
0: spi_acsis_r is masked.
[10] R/W 0 B_AX_SPIC_TFSIM Transmit finish interrupt mask.
1: spi_tfsir_r is not masked.
0: spi_tfsir_r is masked.
[9] R/W 0 B_AX_SPIC_USEIM User_mode error interrupt mask.
1: spi_useir_r is not masked.
0: spi_useir_r is masked.
[8] R/W 1 B_AX_SPIC_ACEIM Auto-check Timeout Error Interrupt Mask.
0 : spi_aceir_r interrupt is masked
1 : spi_aceir_r interrupt is not masked
[7] R/W 1 B_AX_SPIC_BYEIM The Byte-Enable error interrupt mask.
1: spi_byeir_r is not masked.
0: spi_byeir_r is masked.
[6] R/W 1 B_AX_SPIC_WBEIM Write Burst Error Interrupt Mask.
0 : spi_wbier_r interrupt is masked
1 : spi_wbier_r interrupt is not masked
[5] R/W 1 B_AX_SPIC_FSEIM FIFO Size Error Interrupt Mask.
0 : spi_fseir_r interrupt is masked
1 : spi_fseir_r interrupt is not masked
[4] R/W 0 B_AX_SPIC_RXFIM Receive FIFO Full Interrupt Mask
0 : spi_rxfir_r interrupt is masked
1 : spi_rxfir_r interrupt is not masked
[3] R/W 1 B_AX_SPIC_RXOIM Receive FIFO Overflow Interrupt Mask
0 : spi_rxoir_r interrupt is masked
1 : spi_rxoir_r interrupt is not masked
[2] R/W 1 B_AX_SPIC_RXUIM Receive FIFO Underflow Interrupt Mask
0 : spi_rxuir_r interrupt is masked
1 : spi_rxuir_r interrupt is not masked
[1] R/W 1 B_AX_SPIC_TXOIM Transmit FIFO Overflow Interrupt Mask
0 : spi_txoir_r interrupt is masked
1 : spi_txoir_r interrupt is not masked
[0] R/W 0 B_AX_SPIC_TXEIM Transmit FIFO Empty Interrupt Mask
0 : spi_txeir_r interrupt is masked
1 : spi_txeir_r interrupt is not masked
18004030h R_AX_SPIC_ISR
[31:17] N/A 0 RSVD
[16] R 0 B_AX_SPIC_NWEIS NAND Flash write error interrupt status raw interrupt status after masking.
1: spi_nweir_r is active after masking.
0: spi_nweir_r is not active after masking.
[15] R 0 B_AX_SPIC_STFIS Status FIFO full raw interrupt status after masking.
1: spi_stfir_r is active after masking.
0: spi_stfir_r is not active after masking.
[14] R 0 B_AX_SPIC_STOIS Status FIFO overflow interrupt status after masking.
1: spi_stoir_r is active after masking.
0: spi_stoir_r is not active after masking.
[13] R 0 B_AX_SPIC_STUIS Status FIFO underflows interrupt status after masking.
1: spi_stuir_r is active after masking.
0: spi_stuir_r is not active after masking.
[12] R 0 B_AX_SPIC_DREIS DR timeout error status after masking.
1: spi_dreir_r is active after masking.
0: spi_dreir_r is not active after masking.
[11] R 0 B_AX_SPIC_ACSIS Auto-check Flash Status after masking.
1: spi_aceir_r is active after masking.
0: spi_aceir_r is not active after masking.
[10] R 0 B_AX_SPIC_TFSIS Transmit finish interrupt status after masking.
1: spi_tfsir_r is active after masking.
0: spi_tfsir_r is not active after masking.
[9] R 0 B_AX_SPIC_USEIS User mode error status after masking.
1: spi_useir_r is active after masking.
0: spi_useir_r is not active after masking.
[8] R 0 B_AX_SPIC_ACEIS Auto-check timeout error status after masking.
1: spi_aceir_r is active after masking.
0: spi_aceir_r is not active after masking.
[7] R 0 B_AX_SPIC_BYEIS The byte-Enable error interrupts status after masking.
1: spi_byeir_r is active after masking.
0: spi_byeir_r is not active after masking.
[6] R 0 B_AX_SPIC_WBEIS Write burst error interrupt status after masking.
1: spi_wbeir_r is active after masking.
0: spi_wbeir_r is not active after masking.
[5] R 0 B_AX_SPIC_FSEIS FIFO size error interrupts status after masking.
1: spi_fesir_r is active after masking.
0: spi_fesir_r is not active after masking.
[4] R 0 B_AX_SPIC_RXFIS Receive FIFO full interrupt status after masking
1: spi_rxfir is active after masking.
0: spi_rxfir is not active after masking.
[3] R 0 B_AX_SPIC_RXOIS Receive FIFO overflow interrupt status after masking.
1: spi_rxoir_r is active after masking.
0: spi_rxoir_r is not active after masking.
[2] R 0 B_AX_SPIC_RXUIS Receive FIFO underflows interrupt status after masking.
1: spi_rxuir_r is active after masking.
0: spi_rxuir_r is not active after masking.
[1] R 0 B_AX_SPIC_TXOIS Transmit FIFO overflow raw interrupt status after masking.
1: spi_txoir_r is active after masking.
0: spi_txoir_r is not active after masking.
[0] R 0 B_AX_SPIC_TXEIS Transmit FIFO empty raw interrupt status after masking.
1: spi_txeir is active after masking.
0: spi_txeir is not active after masking.
18004034h R_AX_SPIC_RISR
[31:17] NA 0 RSVD RSVD
[16] R 0 B_AX_SPIC_NWEIR NAND Flash write error interrupt status raw interrupt status prior to masking.
1: spi_nweir_r is active prior to masking.
0: spi_nweir_r is not active prior to masking.
[15] R 0 B_AX_SPIC_STFIR Status FIFO full raw interrupt status prior to masking
1: spi_stfir is active prior to masking.
0: spi_stfir is not active prior to masking.
[14] R 0 B_AX_SPIC_STOIR Status FIFO overflows raw interrupt status prior to masking.
1: spi_stoir_r is active prior to masking.
0: spi_stoir_r is not active prior to masking.
[13] R 0 B_AX_SPIC_STUIR Status FIFO underflows raw interrupt status prior to masking.
1: spi_stuir_r is active prior to masking.
0: spi_stuir_r is not active prior to masking.
[12] R 0 B_AX_SPIC_DREIR DR Timeout error status raw interrupt status prior to masking
1: spi_dreir_r is active prior to masking.
0: spi_dreir_r is not active prior to masking.

1: spi_aceir_r is active prior to masking.

[7] R 0 B_AX_SPIC_BYEIR The Byte-Enable error interrupt status raw interrupt status prior to mask.
1: spi_byeir_r is active prior to masking.
0: spi_byeir_r is not active prior to masking.
[6] R 0 B_AX_SPIC_WBEIR Write burst error interrupt status raw interrupt status prior to masking.
1: spi_wbeir_r is active prior to masking.
0: spi_wbeir_r is not active prior to masking.
[5] R 0 B_AX_SPIC_FSEIR FIFO size error interrupt status raw interrupt status prior to masking.
1: spi_fseir_r is active prior to masking.
0: spi_fseir _r is not active prior to masking.

18004038h R_AX_SPIC_TXOICR
[31:1] N/A 0 RSVD RSVD
[0] R/W 0 B_AX_SPIC_TXOICR When reading/writing the register, spi_rxoir_r would be cleared.
1800403Ch R_AX_SPIC_RXOICR
[31:1] N/A 0 RSVD RSVD
[0] R/W 0 B_AX_SPIC_RXOICR When reading/writing the register, spi_rxoir_r would be cleared.
18004040h R_AX_SPIC_RXUICR
[31:1] N/A 0 RSVD RSVD
[0] R/W 0 B_AX_SPIC_RXUICR When reading/writing the register, spi_rxuir_r would be cleared.
18004044h R_AX_SPIC_MSTICR
[31:1] N/A 0 RSVD RSVD
[0] R/W 0 B_AX_SPIC_MSTICR When reading/writing the register, spi_mstir_r would be cleared.
18004048h R_AX_SPIC_ICR
[31:1] N/A 0 RSVD RSVD
[0] R/W 0 B_AX_SPIC_ICR When reading/writing the register, all interrupt would be cleared.
1800404Ch R_AX_SPIC_DMACR
[31:2] N/A 0 RSVD RSVD
[1] R/W 0 B_AX_SPIC_TX_DMAC_EN Transmit DMA Enable. This bit enables/disables the transmit FIFO DMA channel.
0 = Transmit DMA disabled
1 = Transmit DMA enabled
[0] R/W 0 B_AX_SPIC_RX_DMAC_EN Receive DMA Enable. This bit enables/disables the receive FIFO DMA channel
0 = Receive DMA disabled
1 = Receive DMA enabled
18004050h R_AX_SPIC_DMATDLR
[31:7] N/A 0 RSVD RSVD
[6:0] R/W 0 B_AX_SPIC_DMATDL Transmit Data Level. This bit field controls the level at which a DMA request is made by the transmit logic. It is equal to the watermark level; that is, the dma_tx_req signal is generated when the number of valid data entries in the transmit FIFO is equal to or below this field value, and TDMAE = 1. Refer to 2.4.3 Handshaking Interface Operation for more details.
18004054h R_AX_SPIC_DMARDLR
[31:7] N/A 0 RSVD RSVD
[6:0] R/W 0 B_AX_SPIC_DMARDL Receive Data Level. This bit field controls the level at which a DMA request is made by the receive logic. The watermark level = DMARDL+1; that is, dma_rx_req is generated when the number of valid data entries in the receive FIFO is equal to or above this field value + 1, and RDMAE=1. Refer to 2.4.3 Handshaking Interface Operation for more details.
18004058h R_AX_SPIC_IDR
[31:0] R {16'h CR_version, 16'hFF01}
e.g., if there are two SPICs in a chip, the ID should be 0x0203_FF01 for #1 SPIC v2.3 and 0x0203_FF02 for #2 SPIC v2.3		 B_AX_SPIC_IDCODE Contain the decimal value of SPIC version.
1800405Ch R_AX_SPIC_VERSION
[31:0] R {16'd SVN_NUM, 16'd Encryptd_Date}
SVN_NUM: version of SPIC rtl design
Encrypted_Date: generated date		 B_AX_SPIC_SPIC_VERSION Contain the decimal value of SPIC version.
(After 201612: SVN_NUM changes to Git Counter
180040E0h R_AX_SPIC_READ_FAST_SINGLE
[31:16] N/A 0 RSVD RSVD
[15:0] R/W 0x0B0B B_AX_SPIC_FRD_CMD Indicate SPI Flash command value of fast read command. The baud rate is used with FBAUDR.
FRD_CMD[15:8] is for RD_OCTA_IO_CMD_2nd_BYTE (only available when configuration of DDR_EN is defined)
FRD_CMD[7:0] is for RD_OCTA_IO_CMD_1st_BYTE
180040E4h R_AX_SPIC_READ_DUAL_DATA
[31:8] N/A 0 RSVD RSVD
[7:0] R/W 0x3B B_AX_SPIC_RD_DUAL_O_CMD Indicate SPI Flash command value of dual data read command.
180040E8h R_AX_SPIC_READ_DUAL_ADDR_DATA
[31:8] N/A 0 RSVD RSVD
[7:0] R/W 0xBB B_AX_SPIC_RD_DUAL_IO_CMD Indicate SPI Flash command value of dual address and data read command.
180040ECh R_AX_SPIC_READ_QUAD_DATA
[31:8] N/A 0 RSVD
[7:0] R/W 0x6B B_AX_SPIC_RD_QUAD_O_CMD Indicate SPI Flash command value of quad data read command.
180040F0h R_AX_SPIC_READ_QUAD_ADDR_DATA
[31:24] N/A 0xFF B_AX_SPIC_EXIT_PRM_CMD Exit High Performance Read Mode commend.
[23:16] R/W 0xA5 B_AX_SPIC_PRM_VALUE High Performance Read Mode Value.
[15:8] N/A 0 B_AX_SPIC_EXIT_PRM_DUM_LEN If Exit PRM commend is more than 1 byte, use EXIT_PRM_DUM_LEN to extend the commend. It is referenced by bus_clk.

Ex. In qpi mode (4-4-4), FFFFFFFFh data cycle should be issued (4-byte data in 8 spi cycles). EXIT_PRM_CMD only push 1 byte (2 spi cycles), so use EXIT_PRM_DUM_LEN to extend 6 spi cycles.
EXIT_PRM_DUM_LEN = 6 (spi_cycle) * (2 * baurd_rate)
[7:0] R/W 0xEB B_AX_SPIC_RD_QUAD_IO_CMD Indicate SPI Flash command value of quad address and data read command.
180040F4h R_AX_SPIC_WRITE_SINGLE
[31:16] N/A 0 RSVD RSVD
[15:0] R/W 0x0202 B_AX_SPIC_WR_CMD Indicate SPI Flash command value of write command.
WR_CMD[15:8] is for WR_OCTA_IO_CMD_2nd_BYTE (only available when configuration of DDR_EN is defined)

WR_CMD[7:0] is for WR_OCTA_IO_CMD_1st_BYTE
180040F8h R_AX_SPIC_WRITE_DUAL_DATA
[31:8] N/A 0 RSVD RSVD
[7:0] R/W 0xA2 B_AX_SPIC_WR_DUAL_I_CMD Indicate SPI Flash command value of dual data write command.
180040FCh R_AX_SPIC_WRITE_DUAL_ADDR_DATA
[31:8] N/A 0 RSVD RSVD
[7:0] R/W 0 B_AX_SPIC_WR_DUAL_II_CMD Indicate SPI Flash command value of dual address and data write command.
18004100h R_AX_SPIC_WRITE_QUAD_DATA
[31:8] N/A 0 RSVD RSVD
[7:0] R/W 0x32 B_AX_SPIC_WR_QUAD_I_CMD Indicate SPI Flash command value of quad data write command.
18004104h R_AX_SPIC_WRITE_QUAD_ADDR_DATA
[31:8] N/A 0 RSVD RSVD
[7:0] R/W 0x38 B_AX_SPIC_WR_QUAD_II_CMD Indicate SPI Flash command value of quad data write command.
18004108h R_AX_SPIC_WRITE_ENABLE
[31:16] N/A 0 RSVD RSVD
[15:8] R/W 0x06 B_AX_SPIC_WR_EN_CMD_2ND_BYTE Indicate SPI Flash command value of 2nd byte of write enable
[7:0] R/W 0x06 B_AX_SPIC_WR_EN_CMD_1ST_BYTE Indicate SPI Flash command value of 1st byte of write enable.
1800410Ch R_AX_SPIC_READ_STATUS
[31] R/W 0 B_AX_SPIC_INTERVAL_EN_V1 Set to enable INTERVAL_ODD.
[30] R/W 0 B_AX_SPIC_INTERVAL_ODD Indicate SPI Flash read status with 2-Byte status but checking only odd byte or even byte.
0: even byte (2, 4, 6, …); 1: odd byte (1, 3, 5, …);
[29:28] R/W 0x1 B_AX_SPIC_ST_CMD_LEN Indicate the number of bytes in read status command.
ST_CMD_LEN can be 1~3.
[27:26] R/W 0 B_AX_SPIC_ST_CMD_CH Indicate channel number of read status command in transmitting and receiving data.
0: single channel, 1: dual channels, 2: quad channels, 3: octal channel.
[25] R/W 0 B_AX_SPIC_ST_CMD_DDR_EN Indicate the DDR mode in ST_CMD_CH.
[24] N/A 0 RSVD RSVD
[23:16] R/W 0 B_AX_SPIC_RD_ST_CMD_3RD_BYTE Indicate SPI Flash command value of 3rd byte of read status.
[15:8] R/W 0x05 B_AX_SPIC_RD_ST_CMD_2ND_BYTE Indicate SPI Flash command value of 2nd byte of read status.
[7:0] R/W 0x05 B_AX_SPIC_RD_ST_CMD_1ST_BYTE Indicate SPI Flash command value of 1st byte of read status.
18004110h R_AX_SPIC_CTRLR2
[31:15] N/A 0 RSVD
[14] R/W 0 B_AX_SPIC_DIS_DM_CA Set to disable spi_dm_oe_n (let the device to drive DM) when SPIC pushing CMD and ADDR for both user mode and auto mode.
[13] R/W 1 B_AX_SPIC_FULL_WR Set to enable full write in auto mode.
If FULL_WR = 0, SPIC will use spi_dm to do the partial write.
[12] R/W 1 B_AX_SPIC_DM_ACT DM is active high or low.
If DM is active high (DM=1 means "don't write"), DM_ACT should be set to 1.
[11:8] R/W RX_FIFO_ABW B_AX_SPIC_RX_FIFO_ENTRY If using SPIC_HAS_DMA (NO_MERGE_FIFO) SPIC, this field indicates the valid entry of RX FIFO. It is an index of 2 and it should be equal or smaller than RX_FIFO_ABW. The user can modify it to profile with a different entry.
Ex: if RX_FIFO entry is 32, RX_FIFO_ENTYR <=5.
[7:4] R/W FIFO_ABW B_AX_SPIC_TX_FIFO_ENTRY If using SPIC_HAS_DMA (NO_MERGE_FIFO) SPIC, this field indicates the valid entry of TX_FIFO. If using MERGE_FIFO SPIC, there is only one FIFO is in the SPIC design, and this field indicates the valid entry of the FIFO. It is an index of 2 and it should be equal or smaller than FIFO_ABW. The user can modify it to profile with a different entry.
Ex: if FIFO entry is 32, FIFO_ENTYR <=5.
[3] N/A 0 RSVD RSVD
[2] R/W 0 B_AX_SPIC_WPN_DNUM Indicate the WPn input pin of SPI Flash is connected to spi_sout[2]or [3].
If setting 0, it connects to spi_sdata[2]. Otherwise, it connects to spi_sdata[3].
[1] R/W 0 B_AX_SPIC_WPN_SET To implement write protect function. spi_wen_out and the bit of spi_sout which connects to WPN would be initial to 0.
WPN_SET: If setting 1 is protected. Otherwise, is not protected.(Configuration Form is using "WPN_SET_n": If setting 1 is NOT protected. Otherwise, is protected.)
[0] R/W 1 B_AX_SPIC_SO_DNUM Indicate SO input pin of SPI Flash is connected to spi_sout[0]or [1]. It refers to SPI Flash and also the system connection with SPI_Flash (see 6.1.3)
If setting 0, it connects to spi_sout[0] (single-channel connection).
Otherwise, it connects to spi_sout[1] (multi-channels connection).
(Default is configured to 1to support multi-channel connection)
18004114h R_AX_SPIC_FBAUDR
[31:12] N/A 0 RSVD RSVD
[11:0] R/W 1 B_AX_SPIC_FSCKDV Indicate the divider of Fast read command when FAST_RD is setting in user mode or using fast read command in auto mode. The frequency of spi_sclk is derived from:
Frequency of spi_sclk = Frequency of bus_clk / (2*FSCKDV).
18004118h R_AX_SPIC_USER_LENGTH
[31:20] N/A 0 RSVD RSVD
[19:16] R/W 0x3 B_AX_SPIC_USER_ADDR_LENGTH Indicate number of bytes in address phase (between command phase and write/read phase) in user mode.
If it is set to 4, it will transmit 4-byte Address to support 4-byte address mode in SPI Flash.
[15:14] N/A 0 RSVD RSVD
[13:12] R/W 0 B_AX_SPIC_USER_CMD_LENGHT Indicate number of bytes in command phase in user mode.
USER_CMD_LENGHT can be 0~3.
[11:0] R/W 0 B_AX_SPIC_USER_RD_DUMMY_LENGTH Indicate delay cycles for receiving data in user mode (USER_MODE == 1). It is referenced by bus_clk.
1800411Ch R_AX_SPIC_AUTO_LENGTH
[31:28] N/A 0 RSVD RSVD
[27:20] R/W 0 B_AX_SPIC_RDSR_DUMMY_LENGTH Indicate delay cycles for receiving data after Read Status Register (RDSR) command. (auto write or user mode with auto check)
It is referenced by bus_clk.
[19:16] R/W FLASH_ADDR_BYTE B_AX_SPIC_AUTO_ADDR_LENGTH Indicate number of bytes address in read/write command in auto mode.
AUTO_ADDR_LENGTH should be 1, 2, 3, 4 bytes.
If it is set to 4, it will transmit 4-byte Address to support 4-byte address mode in SPI Flash.
*Note: PRM in auto mode should set VALID_CMD[11] (PRM_EN) with correct AUTO_ADDR_LENGTH.
[15:12] R/W 0 B_AX_SPIC_IN_PHYSICAL_CYC This indicates how many SPIC CLK (bus_clk) cycles from pad to internal SPIC.
[11:0] R/W DUMMY_CYCLE B_AX_SPIC_RD_DUMMY_LENGTH Indicate delay cycles for receiving data. It is referenced by bus_clk.
*Note: Don't include SPIC CLK (bus_clk) cycles from pad to internal SPIC
18004120h R_AX_SPIC_VALID_CMD
[31:16] N/A 0 RSVD RSVD
[15] R/W 0 B_AX_SPIC_SEQ_WR_EN Set (1) to enable read sequential transaction write function in auto mode. If two bus write transactions are sequential (address is consecutive), SPIC can access second transaction data without sending Read CMD/ADDR/DUMMY.
Once disable (0) this bit field, spi_csn will inactive immediately and also disable this function.
Notes: Run SEQ_WR_EN after SPIC boot finish.
[14] R/W 0x1 B_AX_SPIC_SEQ_RD_EN Set (1) to enable read sequential transaction read function in auto mode. If two bus read transactions are sequential (address is consecutive), SPIC can access second transaction data without sending Read CMD/ADDR/DUMMY.
Once disable (0) this bit field, spi_csn will inactive immediately and also disable this function.
Notes: Run SEQ_RD_EN after SPIC boot finish.
[13] R/W 0 B_AX_SPIC_DUM_EN Enable to push one dummy byte (DUM_BYTE_VAL) after pushing address to Flash in auto read. If PRM_EN is active, it will push PRM_Value instead of DUM_BYTE_VAL.

If RD_DUMMY_LENGTH = 0, it won't push the dummy byte.
[12] R/W 0 B_AX_SPIC_CTRLR0_CH Set (1) to use CTRLR0 CMD_CH/DATA_CH/ADDR_CH and DDR_EN field in Auto mode; Otherwise, SPIC will decode according to which VALID_CMD you choose in auto mode.

Suggestion while use this bit field:
Read Flash mode: (1-4-8), (4-4-8), (1-4D-8D), (4-4D-8D) with using RD_QUAD_IO[7:0]
Read Flash mode (with 2-Byte CMD): (1-8-8), (8-8-8), (8D-8D-8D), (4D-4D-4D) with using RD_FAST_SINGLE_IO[15:0] (always using FBAUD)
Write Flash mode: (1-4-8), (4-4-8) with using WR_QUAD_IO[7:0]
Write Flash mode (with 2-Byte CMD): (1-8-8), (8-8-8), (8D-8D-8D) with using WR_SINGLE_IO[15:0]
[11] R/W 0 B_AX_SPIC_PRM_EN Set to enable SPIC performance read mode in Auto Mode. RXI-312 SPIC will auto exit performance read mode before auto write or enter to user mode.
Enter PRM Flow:

Set CR_VALID_CMD[11] (PRM_EN) with CR_VALID_CMD[4] (RD_QUAD_IO) or with (CR_VALID_CMD[0] and CR_VALID_CMD[12])
Set CR_READ_QUAD_ADDR_DATA[23:16] (PRM_VALUE) and [7:0] (RD_QUAD_IO_CMD)
Set correct Dummy Cycle, and Valid Command then access in Auto Mode address range
If PRM_EN, at 1st time Auto Rd, it will access with CMD, and store this condition. At 2nd Auto Rd or later, it will access without CMD but with Address and Mode Value. Our SPIC will check PRM_VALUE (CR_READ_QUAD_ADDR_DATA [23:16]), if PRM_VALUE ==0x00 (not a valid PRM value), and PRM_EN is set, SPIC will always access SPI Flash with (CMD+ADDR+MODE+DUMMY+…) format.
[10] R 0 B_AX_SPIC_RM_WEN Remove write enable command in auto write.
[9] R/W 0 B_AX_SPIC_RM_RDSR Remove read status register in auto write.
[8] R/W VALID_CMD_DEF[8] B_AX_SPIC_WR_QUAD_II Indicate quad address/data write is a valid command to execute. (known as (1-4-4))
[7] R/W VALID_CMD_DEF[7] B_AX_SPIC_WR_QUAD_I Indicate quad data write is a valid command to execute. (known as (1-1-4))
[6] R/W VALID_CMD_DEF[6] B_AX_SPIC_WR_DUAL_II Indicate dual address/data write is a valid command to execute. (known as (1-2-2))
[5] R/W VALID_CMD_DEF[5] B_AX_SPIC_WR_DUAL_I Indicate dual data write is a valid command to execute. (known as (1-1-2))
[4] R/W VALID_CMD_DEF[4] B_AX_SPIC_RD_QUAD_IO Indicate quad address/data read is a valid command to execute. (known as (1-4-4))
[3] R/W VALID_CMD_DEF[3] B_AX_SPIC_RD_QUAD_O Indicate quad data read is a valid command to execute. (known as (1-1-4))
[2] R/W VALID_CMD_DEF[2] B_AX_SPIC_RD_DUAL_IO Indicate dual address/data read is a valid command to execute. (known as (1-2-2))
[1] R/W VALID_CMD_DEF[1] B_AX_SPIC_RD_DUAL_I Indicate dual data read is a valid command to execute. (known as (1-1-2))
[0] R/W VALID_CMD_DEF[0] B_AX_SPIC_FRD_SINGLE Indicate fast read command is a valid command to execute. (known as (1-1-1))
18004124h R_AX_SPIC_FLASH_SIZE
[31:4] N/A 0 RSVD RSVD
[3:0] R/W FLASH_MEM_SIZE - 12 B_AX_SPIC_FLASH_SIZE_V1 Indicate the size of flash size to select the target SPI Flash in auto mode. The number is an exponent of 2 (Flash size) -12(The smallest flash size is 4kB).
Ex: If flash size is 4MB (22-bit), set FLASH_SIZE = 10 (22-12).
18004128h R_AX_SPIC_FLUSH_FIFO
[31:3] R 0 RSVD RSVD
[2] W 0 B_AX_SPIC_FLUSH_ST_FIFO Clear all data in the ST_FIFO when writing to FLUSH_ST_FIFO register.
[1] W 0 B_AX_SPIC_FLUSH_DR_FIFO Clear all data in the TX_FIFO and RX_FIFO when writing to FLUSH_DR_FIFOregister.
[0] W 0 B_AX_SPIC_FLUSH_ALL Clear all data in all FIFO (include TX_FIFO, RX_FIFO and ST_FIFO) and reset all FSM when writing to FLUSH_ALL register.
1800412Ch R_AX_SPIC_DUM_BYTE
[31:8] RN/A 0 RSVD RSVD
[7:0] R/W 0 B_AX_SPIC_DUM_BYTE_VAL Push dummy byte value.
18004130h R_AX_SPIC_TX_NDF
[31:24] N/A 0 RSVD RSVD
[23:0] R/W 0 B_AX_SPIC_TX_NDF Indicate a number of data frames (Unit: Byte). After transmitting command and address, SPIC transmits data continuously until data frames are equal to TX_NDF. TX_NDF can set larger than TX_FIFO size.
18004134h R_AX_SPIC_DEVICE_INFO
[31:13] N/A 0 RSVD RSVD
[12] R/W 0 B_AX_SPIC_DATA_UNIT_2B Set (1) when the SPI device stores 2-byte data for each address.
[11] R/W 0 B_AX_SPIC_JEDEC_P2CMF Set (1) when the SPI cmd is JEDEC Profile 2.0 Command Modifier Formats. (6-byte command and address)
[10] R/W 0 B_AX_SPIC_PSRAM Set (1) when the SPI device is PSRAM. PSRAM will disable WEN and RDSR in auto write.
[9] R/W 0 B_AX_SPIC_NAND_FLASH Set (1) when the SPI device is NAND Flash. (not support yet)
[8] R/W 1 B_AX_SPIC_NOR_FLASH Set (1) when the SPI device is NOR Flash.
[7:6] N/A 0 RSVD RSVD
[5] R/W 0 B_AX_SPIC_RD_PAGE_EN Enable SPIC to chop the burst read command across page boundaries in auto mode.
[4] R/W 1 B_AX_SPIC_WR_PAGE_EN Enable SPIC to chop the burst write command across page boundaries in auto mode.
[3:0] R/W 0x08 B_AX_SPIC_PAGE_SIZE The page size of the SPI device (byte) = 2^(PAGE_SIZE)
eg. If the page size of the SPI Flash is 256-byte. PAGE_SIZE should be set to 8.
18004138h R_AX_SPIC_TPR0
[31:24] R/W 0 B_AX_SPIC_CS_TCEM Set to chop auto cmd when CSN low pulse width = (CS_TCEM*32) bus_clk. If set CS_TCEM = 0 will disable this function.
ex. PSRAM tCEM = 4us, SPIC frequency = 100Mhz.
CS_TCEM = ((4000 ns/10 ns)/32) - BYTE_DELAY = 12 (0xc) - BYTE_DELAY

If ((16*baud_rate)/data_channel) ≦ 32. BYTE_DELAY = 0.
If ((16*baud_rate)/data_channel) > 32. BYTE_DELAY = (((16*baud_rate)/data_channel) - 32)/32.
ex. If baud_rate = 3, data_channel = 1.
BYTE_DELAY = (((16*3)/1) - 32)/32 = 16/32 = 0.5
Therefore, BYTE_DELAY should be 1 in this example.
[23:16] R/W 0x10 B_AX_SPIC_CS_SEQ_TIMEOUT The timeout setting of auto command after sequential read command. If set CS_SEQ_TIMEOUT = 0x00 will disable this function.
If set CS_SEQ_TIMEOUT > 0x00, SPIC will exit sequential transaction read and inactive CS when there is no auto read/write command for (CS_SEQ_TIMEOUT *4) bus_clocks.
[15:12] R/W 0 B_AX_SPIC_CS_ACTIVE_HOLD_V1 For Flash chip select active hold time after SCLK rising edge (refer to tSLCH/CHSH, tCSS/tCSH)
*Note: Wait most 4 bus_clk cycles before CS inactive. (Implement CS_ACTIVE_Hold timing only, CS_ACTIVE_Setup timing using D-PHY to shift)
[11:6] R/W CS_H_WR_DUM B_AX_SPIC_CS_H_WR_DUM_LEN Dummy cycle between sending write command to SPI Flash. Using the dummy cycles to avoid the timing violation of CS high time.
[5:0] R/W CS_H_RD_DUM B_AX_SPIC_CS_H_RD_DUM_LEN Dummy cycle between sending read command to SPI Flash. Using the dummy cycles to avoid the timing violation of CS high time.
1800413Ch R_AX_SPIC_AUTO_LENGTH2
[31:12] N/A 0 RSVD RSVD
[11:0] R/W 0 B_AX_SPIC_WR_DUMMY_LENGTH Indicate delay cycles for transmitting data in auto mode. It is referenced by bus_clk.
180041C0h R_AX_SPIC_STFLR
[31:8] N/A 0 RSVD RSVD
[7:0] R 0 B_AX_SPIC_STFLR Status FIFO level. Indicate the FIFO entries of valid data in status data.
180041D0h R_AX_SPIC_PAGE_READ
[31:20] N/A 0 RSVD RSVD
[19:18] R/W 0x3 B_AX_SPIC_PAGE_RD_ADDR_LEN Indicate number of bytes in address phase in page read command.
[17:16] R/W 0 B_AX_SPIC_PAGE_RD_CH Indicate channel number of command and address phase in transmitting page read command.
0: single channel, 1: dual channels, 2: quad channels, 3: octal channel.
[15:8] N/A 0 RSVD RSVD
[7:0] R/W 0x13 B_AX_SPIC_PAGE_RD_CMD Indicate SPI Flash command value of page read command
18005000h R_AX_RXI300_NAME
[31:0] R 0x52300 B_AX_RXI300_NAME The design name of RXI-300.
18005004h R_AX_RXI300_VER
[31:0] R 0x300 B_AX_RXI300_VER The design version of RXI-300.
18005008h R_AX_RXI300_REV
[31:0] R 3 B_AX_RXI300_REV The design reversion of RXI-300.
1800500Ch R_AX_RXI300_INST
[31:0] R 0 B_AX_RXI300_INST The design instance number of RXI-300.
18005010h R_AX_RXI300_IMPL_Y
[31:0] R 0x2020 B_AX_RXI300_IMPL_Y The implementatin year of RXI-300. Reset value depends on implementation date.
18005014h R_AX_RXI300_IMPL_D
[31:0] R 11241543 B_AX_RXI300_IMPL_D The implementatin date of RXI-300. Reset value depends on implementation date.
18005018h R_AX_RXI300_DEV
[31:0] R 0x00521439 B_AX_RXI300_DEV The project numberof RXI-300.
1800501Ch R_AX_RXI300_PRO_NUM
[31:0] R 8852C B_AX_RXI300_PRO_NUM The developer of RXI-300.
18005200h R_AX_RXI300_ELR_0_PLD0
[31:24] R 0 B_AX_ERR_BSTINDEX Burst index of the error request.
[23:16] R 0 B_AX_ERR_BSTLEN Burst Length of the error request.
[15:14] R 0 RSVD RSVD
[13:11] R 0 B_AX_ERR_BSTTYPE BurstSeq of the error request.
[10:8] R 0 B_AX_ERR_CMD Cmd of the error request.
3’b010 (read)
3’b001 (write)
[7:0] R 0 B_AX_ERR_SRC Error source of the error request.
8’d1 (default slave)
8’d2 (bbrf_admap)
8’d3 (apb_adap)
8’d4 (wpa_adap)
18005204h R_AX_RXI300_ELR_0_PLD1
[31:23] R 0 B_AX_ERR_MREQINFO Request info of the error request.
[22:19] R 0 RSVD RSVD
[18:16] R 0 B_AX_ERR_SIZE Size of the error request.
3’b000 (1 Bytes)
3’b001 (2 Bytes)
3’b010 (4 Bytes)
[15:0] R 0 B_AX_ERR_BYTEEN Byteen of the error request.
18005208h R_AX_RXI300_ELR_0_ID
[31:0] R 0 B_AX_ERR_ID Tagid of the error request.
7’b000???? (TR9)
7’b0000000 (SIC)
7’b0010000 (IDDMA)
7’b100???? (AXIDMA)
7’b1010000 (HCI)
7’b110???? (HIOE)
7’b111???? (IPSec)
1800520Ch R_AX_RXI300_ELR_0_ADR0
[31:0] R 0 B_AX_ERR_ADR0 Address[31:0] of the error request.
18005210h R_AX_RXI300_ELR_0_ADR1
[31:0] R 0 B_AX_ERR_ADR1 Address[63:32] of the error request.
18005230h R_AX_RXI300_ELR_0_CODE
[31:8] R 0 RSVD RSVD
[7:0] R 0 B_AX_ELR_CODE The error code of error request, if it’s 0, indicates that there hasn’t error request.
8’h01 (address-hole)
8’h21 (apb/bbrf adapter timeout)
8’h22 (wpa_adapter timeout)
1800523Ch R_AX_RXI300_ELR_0_INTR_CLR
[31:1] R 0 RSVD RSVD
[0] W 0 B_AX_ELR_INTR_CLR Error log interrupt clear. Writing 1’b1 to the register will trigger a 1-cycle pulse interrupt clear. It has to write 1’b1 to this register every time when the error exception occurs. Note that this register is write-only, the return value of reading this register is meaningless.
18005408h R_AX_RXI300_TIME_MON_V1
[31:27] R 0 RSVD RSVD
[26] R/W 1 B_AX_RXI300_TM_BBRF_RST Reset bbrf_adapter module after timeout (active LOW)
[25] R/W 1 B_AX_RXI300_TM_APB_RST Reset apb_adapter module after timeout (active LOW)
[24] R/W 1 B_AX_RXI300_TM_wpa_RST Reset wpa_adapter module after timeout (active LOW)
[23:19] R 0 RSVD RSVD
[18] R/W 0 B_AX_RXI300_TM_AXI_BBRF_EN bbrf_adapter timeout monitor enable (active
HIGH)
[17] R/W 0 B_AX_RXI300_TM_AXI_APB_EN apb_adapter timeout monitor enable (active HIGH)
[16] R/W 0 B_AX_RXI300_TM_WPA_EN wpa_adapter timeout monitor enable (active HIGH)
[15:12] R 0 RSVD RSVD
[11:8] R/W 0 B_AX_RXI300_TM_GRADE grade
[7:0] R/W 0 B_AX_RXI300_TM_TRSH threshold
18600000h R_AX_SYS_ISO_CTRL
[15:14] R/W 0 B_AX_PWC_EV2EF Power Cut for OTP VDD 0.9V and VDD2 3.3V, 1: Power valid; 0: Power invalid.
[15]: For Big Power cut control, [14] for Small Power cut control
[13] R/W 0 B_AX_PA33V_EN Enable PCIE PHY analog 3.3V power cut, 1: Power valdi, 0: Power invalid
[12] R/W 0 B_AX_PA12V_EN Enable PCIE PHY analog 1.2V power cut, 1: Power valdi, 0: Power invalid
[11] R/W 0 B_AX_UA33V_EN Enable USB PHY analog 3.3V power cut, 1: Power valdi, 0: Power invalid
[10] R/W 0 B_AX_UA12V_EN Enable USB PHY analog 1.2V power cut, 1: Power valdi, 0: Power invalid
[9] R/W 1 B_AX_ISO_RFDIO RF Digital I/O to Power On, 1: isolation; 0: attach (no used)
[8] R/W 1 B_AX_ISO_EB2CORE OTP cell output to Power On, 1: isolation; 0: attach
[7] R/W 1 B_AX_ISO_DIOE Extra Digital I/O to Power On, 1: isolation; 0: attach (no used)
[6] R/W 1 B_AX_ISO_WLPON2PP WL PON to Power On, 1: isolation; 0: attach
[5] R/W 1 B_AX_ISO_IP2MAC_WA2PP Analog IPs to Digital 1.2V, 1: isolation; 0: attach
Isolate signal from WLAN AFE, 1: isolation, 0: attach
[4] R/W 1 B_AX_ISO_PD2CORE PCIe Digital 1.2V to Power On/Core 1.2V, 1: isolation; 0: attach (no used)
[3] R/W 1 B_AX_ISO_PA2PCIE PCIe Analog 1.2V to Digital 1.2V, 1: isolation; 0: attach
(PCIe Analog 1.2V and 3.3V are almost turned on at the same time)
[2] R/W 1 B_AX_ISO_UD2CORE USB Digital 1.2V to Power On/Core 1.2V, 1: isolation; 0: attach
[1] R/W 1 B_AX_ISO_UA2USB USB Analog 1.2V to Digital 1.2V, 1: isolation; 0: attach
(USB Analog 1.2V and 3.3V are almost turned on at the same time)
[0] R/W 1 B_AX_ISO_WD2PP MACTOP/BB Digital to Power On, 1: isolation; 0: attach
18600002h R_AX_SYS_FUNC_EN
[15] R/W 0 B_AX_FEN_MREGEN MAC I/O Registers Enable
[14] R/W 1 B_AX_FEN_HWPDN 0 : force All analog blocks shutdown, 1 : keep Analog Blocks alive
[13] R/W 0 RSVD RSVD
[12] R/W 0 B_AX_FEN_ELDR Enable EEPROM Loader (Loader POR)
[11] R/W 0 B_AX_FEN_DCORE Enable Core Digital (MACTOP POR)
[10] R/W 0 RSVD RSVD
[9] R/W 1 B_AX_FEN_DIOE Extra Debug I/O PAD Enable
[8] R/W 0 B_AX_FEN_PCIED Enable PCIe eMAC
[7] R/W 0 B_AX_FEN_PPLL Enable PCIe PHY_PLL
[6] R/W 0 B_AX_FEN_PCIEA Enable PCIe PHY
[5] R/W 0 RSVD RSVD
[4] R/W 0 B_AX_FEN_USBD Enable USB_SIE
[3] R/W 0 B_AX_FEN_UPLL Enable USB PHY_PLL
[2] R/W 0 B_AX_FEN_USBA Enable USB PHY
[1] R/W 0 B_AX_FEN_BB_GLB_RSTn When this bit is set to “0”, whole BB S0 is reset. When this bit is set, BB is enabled.
[0] R/W 0 B_AX_FEN_BBRSTB When this bit is set to “0”, S0 CCK and OFDM are disabled, and clock are gated. Otherwise, CCK and OFDM are enabled.
18600004h R_AX_SYS_PW_CTRL
[31] R/W 0 B_AX_SOP_ASWRM Suspend option for analog SWR operates in PWM mode.
1: PWM mode, 0: PFM mode
[30] R/W 1 B_AX_SOP_EASWR Suspend option for analog SWR operation
1. SWR enable, 0: SWR disable
[29] R/W 0 B_AX_SOP_PWMM_DSWR Suspend option for D-SWR operates in PWM mode.
1: PWM mode, 0: PFM mode
[28] R/W 1 B_AX_SOP_EDSWR Suspend option for D-SWR operation
1. SWR enable, 0: SWR disable
[27] R/W 0 B_AX_SOP_ACKF Suspend option to switch analog Clock Mode to 12Mhz (Host could suspend by gating, Power Modes by FSM).
1: keep 12M (Fast Clock), 0: 500K(Slow Clock)
[26] R/W 0 B_AX_SOP_ERCK Suspend option to enable SYMON register clock (12M/500KHz) (USB could suspend by gating, Power Modes by FSM)
1: clock on, 0: clock off
[25] R/W 0 B_AX_SOP_ANA_CLK_Division_2 1 : suspend option ANA_CLK 除 2
0 : suspend option ANA_CLK
[24] R/W 0 B_AX_SOP_EXTL Suspend option not to turn off XTAL
1: Enable XTAL, 0: Disable XTAL
[23] R/W 0 B_AX_SOP_OFF_CAPC_EN Suspend option for Enable to cut CA33PC_EN & CA10PC_EN as PCIE assert L1.OFF/L2 in PCIE suspend state
(for SUS 要不要關APHY power cut)
0 : disable to cut CA33PC_EN & CA10PC_EN
1 : enable to cut CA33PC_EN & CA10PC_EN
[22] R/W 0 B_AX_XTAL_OFF_A_DIE A-Die跟著Xtal開關
0: Disable , 1: Enable
[21] R/W 0 B_AX_ROP_SWPR Resume option to skip wait Power Ready.
1: power up no delay, 0: wait power stable
[20] R/W 0 B_AX_DIS_HW_LPLDM DIS_HW_LPLDM: Disable HW automatically change LPLDO to normal/sleep mode for power saving (B-CUT ECO)
0: Enable, 1: Disable
[19] R/W 0 B_AX_DIS_HW_LPURLDO DIS_HW_ULDO: Disable HW automatically change UART_LDO to normal/sleep mode for power saving
0: Enable, 1: Disable
[18] R/W 0 B_AX_DIS_WLBT_PDNSUSEN_SOPC Disable WLBT PDNSUSEN SOPC
為了避免sympmc在跑LPS or SUS的時候
因LPS/SUS的condition過短, 造成選擇LPS/SUS option時誤動作的問題
[17] RO 0 B_AX_RDY_SYSPWR Power ready from LPS, ROF, suspend mode. Valid in normal version.
[16] R/W 0 B_AX_EN_WLON Enable WLON circuit reset. Before enable APFM_ONMAC, driver shall release this bit first.
[15] R/W 0 B_AX_APDM_HPDN Enable WLAN Power Down operation
[14] R/W 0 B_AX_PSUS_OFF_CAPC_EN Enable to cut CA33PC_EN & CA10PC_EN as PCIE assert L1.OFF/L2
( for 單純side-band控制APHY power cut )
0 : disable to cut CA33PC_EN & CA10PC_EN
1 : enable to cut CA33PC_EN & CA10PC_EN
[13] R/W 0 RSVD RSVD
[12] R/W 0 B_AX_AFSM_PCIE_SUS_EN When this bit is set, PCIE suspends via HW FSM control flow.
[11] R/W 0 B_AX_AFSM_WLSUS_EN 1: Host Suspend through FSM operation; 0: Host Suspend by gated
[10] R/W 0 B_AX_APFM_SWLPS Auto Power On State Machine to enter SW LPS mode
[9] R/W 0 B_AX_APFM_OFFMAC Auto FSM to Turn On, include clock, isolation, power control for MAC only (auto set by ICFG, and clear when Power Ready)
[8] R/W 0 B_AX_APFN_ONMAC Auto FSM to Turn On, include clock, isolation, power control for MAC only (auto set by ICFG, and clear when Power Ready)
[7] R/W 1 B_AX_CHIP_PDN_EN Chip powen down enable
0: disable
1: enable
[6] R/W 0 B_AX_RDY_MACDIS Disable MAC power Ready Output for Test Mode
[5] R/W 0 RSVD RSVD
[4] R/W 0 B_AX_SW_AFE_MODE Trigger HW State machine to switch AFE mode. The mode is dependent on BIT_AFE_BCN.
It switches to beacon mode if BIT_AFE_BCN =1.
It switches to normal mode if BIT_AFE_BCN = 0.
1: Switch AFE mode, HW clear to zero when finished.
[3] R/W 0 B_AX_PFM_WOWL Wake On Lan indicator for H/W FSM
[2] R/W 0 RSVD RSVD
[1] RO 0 B_AX_WL_HCI_ALD Inform WLAN HCI Region load done
[0] R/W 0 B_AX_EFUSE_LDALL 1: SW enable HWSM to autoload all
0: SW enable HWSM to autoload excluding Efuse 0x00~60h
18600008h R_AX_SYS_CLK_CTRL
[15] R/W 0 B_AX_CPU_IDMEM_CLK_EN MCU IDMEM Clock Enable, 1: Enable, 0: Disable
[14] R/W 0 B_AX_CPU_CLK_EN MCU Clock Enable, 1: Enable, 0: Disable
[13] R/W 1 B_AX_SYMR_AX_CLK_EN System on register clock enable, 1: Enable, 0: Disable
[12] R/W 0 RSVD RSVD
[11] R/W 0 B_AX_MAC_CLK_EN MAC_CLK_EN MAC Clock Enable, 1: Enable, 0 : Disable
[10:9] R/W 0 RSVD RSVD
[8] R/W 0 B_AX_EXT_32K_EN Enable extern 32K clock
0: disable (internal 32K clock, but the real clock should be set in reg0x5d0[2])
1: enable (external 32K clock, but the real clock should be set in reg0x5d0[2])
[7] R/W 0 B_AX_WL_CLK_TEST WL clock test mode control
[6] R/W 0 RSVD RSVD
[5] R/W 1 B_AX_LOADER_CLK_EN Loader Clock Enable
[4:2] R/W 0 RSVD RSVD
[1] R/W 0 B_AX_ANA_CLK_Division_2 1 : select ANA_CLK 除 2
0 : select ANA_CLK
[0] R/W 0 B_AX_CNTD16V_EN CNTD16V_EN Analog Clock source Divide 16 Mode for Hardware Timer
1860000Ah R_AX_SYS_WL_EFUSE_CTRL
[15] R 0 B_AX_OTP_B_PWC_RPT OTP Big Power Cut OR Result:
WLAN OTP Power Cut 0x00[15] and BT OTP Power cut BT reg 0x33[26] OR result
[14] R 0 B_AX_OTP_S_PWC_RPT OTP Small Power Cut OR Result:
WLAN OTP Power Cut 0x00[14] and BT OTP Power cut BT reg 0x33[25] OR result
[13] R 0 B_AX_OTP_ISO_RPT OTP ISO AND Result:
WLAN OTP ISO 0x00[8] and BT OTP Power cut BT reg 0x33[27] AND result
[12] R 0 B_AX_OTP_Burst_RPT WLBT OTP Burst OR Result:
WLAN OTP Burst mode control 0x38[19] and BT Burst mode control OR result
[11] R 0 B_AX_OTP_AutoLoad_RPT WL OTP Autoload Report:
1: at Autoload process
0: Autoload Finished
control by Loader
[10:7] R/W 0 RSVD RSVD
[6] R 0 B_AX_AUTOLOAD_DIS_A_DIE Disable Autoload from WL eFuse (A-die) (GPIO5)
1 : Disable
0 : Enable
[5] R/W 1 B_AX_AUTOLOAD_SUS 解鎖 0x1C[3] = 0 can write this bit
1: Autoload successful
0: Autoload fail
[4] R 0 B_AX_AUTOLOAD_DIS Disable Autoload from WL OTP (D-die) (EESK)
1 : Disable
0 : Enable
[3:0] R/W 0 RSVD RSVD

[31:16] R/W 0 RSVD RSVD

[13] R/W 0 B_AX_DIS_CLK_REGD_GATE Disable AON PageD register clock gated enable.
[12] R/W 0 B_AX_DIS_CLK_REGC_GATE Disable AON PageC register clock gated enable.
[11] R/W 0 B_AX_DIS_CLK_REGB_GATE Disable AON PageB register clock gated enable.
[10] R/W 0 B_AX_DIS_CLK_REGA_GATE Disable AON PageA register clock gated enable.
[9] R/W 0 B_AX_DIS_CLK_REG9_GATE Disable AON Page9 register clock gated enable.
[8] R/W 0 B_AX_DIS_CLK_REG8_GATE Disable AON Page8 register clock gated enable.
[7] R/W 0 B_AX_DIS_CLK_REG7_GATE Disable AON Page7 register clock gated enable.
[6] R/W 0 B_AX_DIS_CLK_REG6_GATE Disable AON Page6 register clock gated enable.

[4] R/W 0 B_AX_DIS_CLK_REG4_GATE Disable AON Page4 register clock gated enable.
[3] R/W 0 B_AX_DIS_CLK_REG3_GATE Disable AON Page3 register clock gated enable.
[2] R/W 0 B_AX_DIS_CLK_REG2_GATE Disable AON Page2 register clock gated enable.
[1] R/W 0 B_AX_DIS_CLK_REG1_GATE Disable AON Page1 register clock gated enable.

18600010h R_AX_SYS_SWR_CTRL1
[31:12] R/W 0 RSVD RSVD
[11] R/W 0 B_AX_SYM_CTRL_SPSANA_PWMFREQ 1'b1: Control SWR PWM/PFM by register 0x220[6](SWRANA)
1'b1: Control SWR reg_mos_half_L by 0x224[1]
1'b0: SWR PWM/PFM control by WiFi/BT HW
1'b0: Control SWR reg_mos_half_L by WiFi/BT HW
[10] R/W 0 B_AX_SYM_CTRL_SPS_PWMFREQ 1'b1: Control SWR PWM/PFM by register 0x200[6] (SPSLDO),0x220[6](SWRANA)
1'b1: Control SWR reg_mos_half_L by 0x0204[1]/0x0224[1]
1'b0: SWR PWM/PFM control by WiFi/BT HW
1'b0: Control SWR reg_mos_half_L by WiFi/BT HW
[9] R/W 0 B_AX_HW_AUTO_CTRL_EXT_SWR 當使用external SWR提供1.3V, interanl SWR提供1.05V時,透過GPIO6去關掉extenal SWR when set LPS option[28] :
1 : always turn on external SWR
0 : HW auto control external SWR
LPS option[28]會把interna SWR and external SWR都關掉(internal SWR關掉時還會有小LDO開啟),但是external SWR有提供1.3V給PHY,所以不能關掉,所以需要再設這個bit強制開啟external SWR以免掉卡, FW需要根據是否有外掛SWR來控制這個bit
[8] R/W 0 B_AX_use_internal_SWR_and_LDO 當完全不需要使用internal SWR的時候可以關掉 : (之前華為有這個使用case, 當1.3V和1.05V都外掛時,可以從efuse關掉internal SWR和LDO)
(外掛兩顆SWR提供1.3V和1.05V)
1 : disable internal SWR and LDO, use external SWR and LDO
0 : use internal SWR and LDO
[7] R/W 0 B_AX_MAC_ID_EN Reserved for RF_ID selection to be MAC
[6:3] R/W 0 RSVD RSVD
[2] R/W 1 B_AX_OPTION_DIS_XTAL_BG Use in PCIe only :
1 : check reg 0x90[31] value to decide XTAL_BG is disable or not
0 : reg 0x90[31] for PCIe is reserved
[1:0] R/W 0 RSVD RSVD

1860001Ch R_AX_RSV_CTRL
[23:12] RO 0000 B_AX_HR_AX_DBG HREG Access Debug information
[11:10] R/W 0 RSVD Reserved
[9] R/W 0 B_AX_R_SYM_DIS_PCIE_FLR 擋住PCIE FLR Reset
1 : Enabled
0 : Disabled
[8] R/W 0 B_AX_R_EN_HRST_PWRON Enable (PCIE PERST , USB SE0RST) reset power on.
1 : Enabled
0 : Disabled
Write Locked by 0xCC[2]
[7] R/W 0 B_AX_LOCK_ALL_EN 1:Enable ‘Locked All’ setting by Hardware, 0: Hardware Enable ‘Locked All’ denied.
[6] R/W 0 B_AX_R_DIS_PRST Enable SW_RST (PCIE PERST, USB SE0RST) can reset WL_CORE :
(1) Set PCIE PERST and USB SE0RST CAN reset WL_CORE :
Step 1 : Write reg0x1C[5] = 1'b1
Step 2 : Write reg0x1C[6] = 1'b0
Step 3 : Write reg0x1C[5] = 1'b0

(2) Set Set PCIE PERST and USB SE0RST CANNOT reset WL_CORE :
Step1 : Write reg0x1C[5] = 1'b1
Step2 : Write reg0x1C[6] = 1'b1
Step3 : Write reg0x1C[5] = 1'b0
[5] R/W 0 B_AX_WLOCK_1C_BIT6 0x1C[6] Register Write Locked disable . The control sequence reference 0x1C[6] description.
[4] R/W 0 B_AX_WLOCK_40 1:Register Write Locked, 0: Register Accessible by SW/FW (offset:0x0040h~0x0043h)
[3] R/W 0 B_AX_WLOCK_08 1:Register Write Locked, 0: Register Accessible by SW/FW (offset:0x0008h~0x000Bh)
[2] R/W 0 B_AX_WLOCK_04 1:Register Write Locked, 0: Register Accessible by SW/FW (offset:0x0004h~0x0007h)
[1] R/W 0 B_AX_WLOCK_00 1:Register Write Locked, 0: Register Accessible by SW/FW (offset:0x0000h~0x0003h)
[0] R/W 0 B_AX_WLOCK_ALL 1:Register Write Locked, 0: Register Accessible by SW/FW (offset:0x0000h~0x00FFh)
