18603044h R_AX_PCIE_LAT_CTRL
[31:18] RO 0 RSVD RSVD
[17] R/W 1 B_AX_SYS_SUS_L12_EN 1: enable
0: disable
[16] R/W 0 B_AX_MDIO_S_EN MDIO serial mode
1: enable
0: disable
serial mode doesn't support byte enable function
[15] RO 0 RSVD RSVD
[14:12] RO 1 B_AX_LDO_T3_LAT the latency for waiting power source is stable
0: 0us (don't use)
1: 5us
2: 10us
3: 15us
4: 20us
5: 25us
6: 30us
7: 35us
[11] RO 0 RSVD RSVD
[10:8] R/W 1 B_AX_VMAIN_LAT the latency for waiting power source is stable
0: 0us (don't use)
1: 5us
2: 10us
3: 15us
4: 20us
5: 25us
6: 30us
7: 35us
[7:4] R/W 1 B_AX_CLK_REQ_LAT Bit[7:4]: EMAC delay setting
0000 : No delay
0001 : 30us (hw default value)
0010 : 50us
0011 : 80us
0100 : 100us
0101 : 120us
0110 : 150us
0111 : 180us
1000 : 200us
1001 : 300us
1010 : 400us
1011 : 500us
1100 : 1ms
1101 : 3ms
1110 : 5ms
1111 : 10ms
[3:2] RO 0 RSVD
[1] R/W 0 B_AX_CLK_REQ_SEL_OPT the ECO eMAC delay option
0: disable
1: Modified RTK request with additional delay
[0] R/W 0 B_AX_CLK_REQ_SEL the clock removel request for PHY is from
0: DWC original signal
1: RTK request with additional delay

186031D0h R_AX_PCIE_DBG_STATE
[31:11] RO 0 RSVD RSVD
[10:8] R/W 0 B_AX_PCIE_DBG_STATE_MODE [debug state] state mode
0: LTSSM
1: PM current
2: PM master
3: PM slave
3: PM L1s
[7] RO 0 RSVD RSVD
[6:4] R/W 0 B_AX_PCIE_DBG_STATE_GRP select debug group, only 4 units [debug state]
this function is used to setting the maximum continued 3 states want to send a trigger singal
[3] RO 0 RSVD RSVD
[2] R/W 0 B_AX_PCIE_DBG_STATE_MSK [debug state] trigger mask
[1] R/W 0 B_AX_PCIE_DBG_STATE_CLR [debug state] clear tirgger
[0] W1S 0 B_AX_PCIE_DBG_STATE_SET [debug state] wirte 1 to begin setting debug state
186031D4h R_AX_PCIE_DBG_STATE_D
[31:23] RO 0 RSVD RSVD
[22:16] R/W 0 B_AX_PCIE_DBG_STATE_D3 [debug state] the state want to trigger, and to skip this state when bit 6 is 1
[15] RO 0 RSVD RSVD
[14:8] R/W 0 B_AX_PCIE_DBG_STATE_D2 [debug state] the state want to trigger, and to skip this state when bit 6 is 1
[7] RO 0 RSVD RSVD
[6:0] R/W 0 B_AX_PCIE_DBG_STATE_D1 [debug state] the state want to trigger, and to skip this state when bit 6 is 1
186031D8h R_AX_PCIE_DBG_INFO_D
[31:22] RO 0 RSVD RSVD
[21:16] RO 0 B_AX_PCIE_DBG_INFO_D3 [debug state] the state status
[15:14] RO 0 RSVD RSVD
[13:8] RO 0 B_AX_PCIE_DBG_INFO_D2 [debug state] the state status
[7:6] RO 0 RSVD RSVD
[5:0] RO 0 B_AX_PCIE_DBG_INFO_D1 [debug state] the state status
18603330h R_AX_PCIE_MIT_TMR
[31:6] RO 0 RSVD RSVD
[5:4] R/W 0 B_AX_PCIE_MIT_RX_TMR Timer unit for setting Interrupt Migration RX timer.
00: 1 ms, 01: 2 ms, 10: 4 ms, 11: 8 ms
Combine with0x3334[15:8] to obtain time value of RX timer.
ex : this reg. = 0, 0x3334[15:8] = 3, RX timer = 1msx3 = 6 ms
Note: Timeout variation is +- 1 timer unit
[3:2] RO 0 RSVD RSVD
[1:0] R/W 0 B_AX_PCIE_MIT_TX_TMR Timer unit for setting Interrupt Migration TX timer.
00: 1 ms, 01: 2 ms, 10: 4 ms, 11: 8 ms
Combine with0x3334[7:0] to obtain time value of TX timer.
ex : this reg. = 0, 0x3334[7:0] = 3, TX timer = 1msx3 = 6 ms
Note: Timeout variation is +- 1 timer unit
18603334h R_AX_PCIE_MIT_CNT
[31:24] R/W 0 B_AX_PCIE_RX_MIT_CNT When the buffered RX packet number is matched, the interrupt is then issued.
0x00: disable RX counter
[23:16] R/W 0 B_AX_PCIE_TX_MIT_CNT When the buffered TX packet number is matched, the interrupt is then issued.
0x00: disable TX counter
[15:8] R/W 0 B_AX_PCIE_RX_MIT_TMR_CNT Multiple value of RX timer unit.
Combine with 0x3330[5:4] to obtain time value of RX timer.
When the counting time matches or exceeds this setting, the interrupt is then issued.
0x00: disable RX timer
[7:0] R/W 0 B_AX_PCIE_TX_MIT_TMR_CNT Multiple value of TX timer unit.
Combine with 0x3330[1:0] to obtain time value of TX timer.
When the counting time matches or exceeds this setting, the interrupt is then issued.
0x00: disable TX timer
18603338h R_AX_PCIE_MIT_CH_EN
[31:18] RO 0 RSVD RSVD
[17] R/W 0 B_AX_PCIE_MIT_RXP2_EN enable rxq part 2 interrupt mitigation
[16] R/W 0 B_AX_PCIE_MIT_RXP1_EN enable rxq part 1 interrut mitigation
[15:13] RO 0 RSVD RSVD
[12] R/W 0 B_AX_PCIE_MIT_TXCH12_EN enable tx ch12 interrupt mitigation
[11] R/W 0 B_AX_PCIE_MIT_TXCH11_EN enable tx ch11 interrupt mitigation
[10] R/W 0 B_AX_PCIE_MIT_TXCH10_EN enable tx ch10 interrupt mitigation
[9] R/W 0 B_AX_PCIE_MIT_TXCH9_EN enable tx ch9 interrupt mitigation
[8] R/W 0 B_AX_PCIE_MIT_TXCH8_EN enable tx ch8 interrupt mitigation
[7] R/W 0 B_AX_PCIE_MIT_TXCH7_EN enable tx ch7 interrupt mitigation
[6] R/W 0 B_AX_PCIE_MIT_TXCH6_EN enable tx ch6 interrupt mitigation
[5] R/W 0 B_AX_PCIE_MIT_TXCH5_EN enable tx ch5 interrupt mitigation
[4] R/W 0 B_AX_PCIE_MIT_TXCH4_EN enable tx ch4 interrupt mitigation
[3] R/W 0 B_AX_PCIE_MIT_TXCH3_EN enable tx ch3 interrupt mitigation
[2] R/W 0 B_AX_PCIE_MIT_TXCH2_EN enable tx ch2 interrupt mitigation
[1] R/W 0 B_AX_PCIE_MIT_TXCH1_EN enable tx ch1 interrupt mitigation
[0] R/W 0 B_AX_PCIE_MIT_TXCH0_EN enable tx ch0 interrupt mitigation
18603340h R_AX_PCIE_MIT_TX_ISR
[31:13] RO 0 RSVD RSVD
[12] W1C 0 B_AX_PCIE_TX_CH12_ISR Interrupt status for AXI DMA TX CH12 mitigation
[11] W1C 0 B_AX_PCIE_TX_CH11_ISR Interrupt status for AXI DMA TX CH11 mitigation

[9] W1C 0 B_AX_PCIE_TX_CH9_ISR Interrupt status for AXI DMA TX CH9 mitigation

[7] W1C 0 B_AX_PCIE_TX_CH7_ISR Interrupt status for AXI DMA TX CH7 mitigation
[6] W1C 0 B_AX_PCIE_TX_CH6_ISR Interrupt status for AXI DMA TX CH6 mitigation
[5] W1C 0 B_AX_PCIE_TX_CH5_ISR Interrupt status for AXI DMA TX CH5 mitigation
[4] W1C 0 B_AX_PCIE_TX_CH4_ISR Interrupt status for AXI DMA TX CH4 mitigation
[3] W1C 0 B_AX_PCIE_TX_CH3_ISR Interrupt status for AXI DMA TX CH3 mitigation
[2] W1C 0 B_AX_PCIE_TX_CH2_ISR Interrupt status for AXI DMA TX CH2 mitigation
[1] W1C 0 B_AX_PCIE_TX_CH1_ISR Interrupt status for AXI DMA TX CH1 mitigation
[0] W1C 0 B_AX_PCIE_TX_CH0_ISR Interrupt status for AXI DMA TX CH0 mitigation
18603344h R_AX_PCIE_MIT_RX_ISR
[31:2] RO 0 RSVD RSVD
[1] W1C 0 B_AX_PCIE_RX_RXP2_ISR Interrupt status for AXI DMA RX Part2 mitigation
[0] W1C 0 B_AX_PCIE_RX_RXP1_ISR Interrupt status for AXI DMA RX Part1 mitigation
18603348h R_AX_PCIE_MIT_TX_IMR
[31:13] RO 0 RSVD RSVD
[12] W1C 0 B_AX_PCIE_TX_CH12_IMR IMR of interrupt for AXI DMA TX CH12 mitigation
[11] W1C 0 B_AX_PCIE_TX_CH11_IMR IMR of interrupt for AXI DMA TX CH11 mitigation
[10] W1C 0 B_AX_PCIE_TX_CH10_IMR IMR of interrupt for AXI DMA TX CH10 mitigation
[9] W1C 0 B_AX_PCIE_TX_CH9_IMR IMR of interrupt for AXI DMA TX CH9 mitigation
[8] W1C 0 B_AX_PCIE_TX_CH8_IMR IMR of interrupt for AXI DMA TX CH8 mitigation
[7] W1C 0 B_AX_PCIE_TX_CH7_IMR IMR of interrupt for AXI DMA TX CH7 mitigation
[6] W1C 0 B_AX_PCIE_TX_CH6_IMR IMR of interrupt for AXI DMA TX CH6 mitigation
[5] W1C 0 B_AX_PCIE_TX_CH5_IMR IMR of interrupt for AXI DMA TX CH5 mitigation
[4] W1C 0 B_AX_PCIE_TX_CH4_IMR IMR of interrupt for AXI DMA TX CH4 mitigation
[3] W1C 0 B_AX_PCIE_TX_CH3_IMR IMR of interrupt for AXI DMA TX CH3 mitigation
[2] W1C 0 B_AX_PCIE_TX_CH2_IMR IMR of interrupt for AXI DMA TX CH2 mitigation
[1] W1C 0 B_AX_PCIE_TX_CH1_IMR IMR of interrupt for AXI DMA TX CH1 mitigation

1860334Ch R_AX_PCIE_MIT_RX_IMR
[31:2] RO 0 RSVD RSVD
[1] W1C 0 B_AX_PCIE_RX_RXP2_IMR IMR of interrupt for AXI DMA RX Part2 mitigation
[0] W1C 0 B_AX_PCIE_RX_RXP1_IMR IMR of interrupt for AXI DMA RX Part1 mitigation
18604000h R_AX_SDIO_TX_CTRL_V1
[31:16] R/W 0 B_AX_SDIO_INT_TIMEOUT It is SDIO status timeout. The unit is 32usec. The default value is 0xc00 means that timeout is 98304 usec ( 32*0xc00)
Due to HW design, HW takes 0~3 as 0.
[15] R/W1C 0 B_AX_IO_ERR_STATUS If there is any io error due to Clock mismatch, set this flag.
Driver write 1 to clear it
[14] R 0 B_AX_CMD53_W_MIX This bit indicates the tx-packet is divided into two CMD53 (block + byte, byte + byte or block + block mode).

If B_AX_CMD53_TX_FORMAT = 0 or use CMD53 byte mode, this bit indicates CMD53 data length < Aggregation Len_4_BYTE_CNT
If B_AX_CMD53_TX_FORMAT = 1 and use CMD53 block mode, this bit indicates SEQ of a CMD53 Tx = SEQ of the previous CMD53 Tx.
[13] R/W 0 B_AX_CMD53_TX_FORMAT 0: Original format. Treats CMD53-address as data size (unit = 4 Byte)
1: Treats block mode CMD53-address as empty data size (unit = 4 Byte)
data length = block_cnt * block_length – address * 4 (address = dummy length. Unit = 4 byte)

If sets this bit = 1, HW will clear B_AX_CMD53_W_MIX to 0.
[12] R/W 0 B_AX_CMD53_R_TIMEOUT_MASK 1: mask CMD53 data timeout (for debug)
[11:10] R/W 0 B_AX_CMD53_R_TIMEOUT_UNIT Adjust the unit of cmd53 read timeout counter, T: SDIO_CLK
00 = 1T
01 = 2T
10 = 4T
11 = 8T
[9] R/W 0 B_AX_REPLY_ERRCRC_IN_DATA Default: 0
When this bit is set to 1 and there is a CMD53 read issue, fill in incorrect CRC value in the reply Data
[8] R/W 0 B_AX_En_CMD53_Overlap allow new coming cmd53 reset the unfinished cmd53 process
[7] R/W 0 B_AX_REPLY_ERR_IN_R5 Default: 0
When this bit is set to 1 and there is a data sync issue due to Clock mismatch, set ERROR flag(Bit3 and Bit0) in R5 response.
[6] R/W 0 B_AX_R18A_EN When this bit is auto load to 1, Device can accept host request for switch IO voltage to 1.8v
0:disable;
1: Set R18A in Response4		 0x310[6]
[5] R/W 0 B_AX_SDIO_CMD_FORCE_VLD When this bit is auto load to 1, Device can accept any command regardless of card’s current state
0:disable;
1:enable response for CMD in all card states		 0x310[5]
[4] R/W 0 B_AX_INIT_CMD_EN When this bit is set to 1, Device can accept CMD0, CMD3, CMD5 when bus is under command state 0x310[4]
[3] R/W 0 B_AX_RXINT_READ_MASK_DIS 0: RX_REQUEST interrupt will be masked after reading R_AX_SDIO_HISR.
1: RX_REQUEST interrupt will not be masked after reading R_AX_SDIO_HISR.
[2] R/W 0 B_AX_EN_RXDMA_MASK_INT If this bit set to 0, RX_REQUEST interrupt will raised immediately when RX data is ready.
If this bit set to 1 and status information is sent, RX_REQUEST interrupt will not raise. Only If INT_TIME_OUT is 1, interrupt will not be raised until timeout.
[1] R/W 0 B_AX_EN_MASK_TIMER The bit is only valid when EN_RXDMA_MASK_ INT is 1.
If this bit set to 0, timeout is disable.
If this bit set to 1, it enable interrupt timeout.
[0] R/W 0 B_AX_CMD_ERR_STOP_INT_EN If this bit set to 1, then SDIO receive illegal command or wrong CRC will stop send interrupt to host.
18604004h R_AX_SDIO_CTRL_V1
[31:28] R/W 0x03 B_AX_SDIO_DRV_TYPE_D Driver Type D mapping to PAD driving.
Bit0 = 2E , bit1 = 3E , bit2 = 4E , bit3 = 5E
Note: workable if support Driver Type D (CCCR 0x15[2] = 1)		 0x315[7:4]
[27:24] R/W 0x05 B_AX_SDIO_DRV_TYPE_C Driver Type C mapping to PAD driving.
Bit0 = 2E , bit1 = 3E , bit2 = 4E , bit3 = 5E
Note: workable if support Driver Type C (CCCR 0x15[1] = 1)		 0x315[3:0]
[23:20] R/W 0x07 B_AX_SDIO_DRV_TYPE_B Driver Type B mapping to PAD driving.
Bit0 = 2E , bit1 = 3E , bit2 = 4E , bit3 = 5E		 0x314[7:4]
[19:16] R/W 0x0F B_AX_SDIO_DRV_TYPE_A Driver Type A mapping to PAD driving.
Bit0 = 2E , bit1 = 3E , bit2 = 4E , bit3 = 5E
Note: workable if support Driver Type A (CCCR 0x15[0] = 1)		 0x314[3:0]
[15:9] R 0 RSVD
[8] R/W 0 B_AX_SIG_OUT_PH This bit is used to invert the SDCLK phase for SDR50 and SDR104 mode.
0 : Does not invert the SDCLK.
1 : To invert the SDCLK (push/pull signals 0.5T earlier).
Autoload from Efuse 0x123[6].		 0x313[0]
[7:4] R 0 B_AX_CMD11_SEQ_END_DELAY The time duration of device to push CMD/DAT lines high after receiving SD_CLK again.
Unit: 1-T SD_CLK.		 0x312[7:4]
[3:1] R 0 B_AX_CMD11_SEQ_SAMPLE_INTERVAL Sampling interval of ANA_CLK for detecting SD_CLK.
Unit: 64-T ANA_CLK.		 0x312[3:1]
[0] R 0 B_AX_CMD11_SEQ_EN If this bit set to 1, enable cmd11 signal voltage switch sequence function.
If this bit set to 0, only a R1 response will be send to respond to CMD11.		 0x312[0]
18604008h R_AX_SDIO_MONITOR_V1
[31:24] R 0 RSVD
[23:0] R 0 B_AX_tsf_freerun_cnt_reg
1860400Ch R_AX_SDIO_MONITOR_2_V1
[31:24] R 0 RSVD
[23] R/W 0 B_AX_CMD53_WT_EN 1: enable, 0: disable
If getting CMD53 write CMD, Start counting SDIO clock (from 0) when response end
Stop counting when CMD53 DATA start
The SDIO clock count is reported to B_AX_SDIO_CLK_CNT
Only can use CMD52 to write
[22:21] R 0 B_AX_SDIO_CLK_MONITOR 1: 200T MAC_CLK
2: 2000T MAC_CLK
3: 360000T MAC_CLK
Start counting SDIO clock (from 0) when this reg be set to non-zero value
Stop counting when timeout (200T, 2000T or 360000T) and reset this reg to 0
The SDIO clock count is reported to B_AX_SDIO_CLK_CNT
Write, polling 0 ready
Only can use CMD52 to write
[20:0] R 0 B_AX_SDIO_CLK_CNT SDIO clock count
18604010h R_AX_SDIO_CTRL_2_V1
[31:2] R 0 RSVD
[1] R/W 1 B_AX_SDIO_CLK_SMT SD_CLK Schmitt trigger setting:
1: enable Schmitt trigger of SD_CLK, input Pad latch high/low follow SD SPEC.
0: disable Schmitt trigger of SD_CLK, input Pad latch high/low follow 50% signal level.
autoload from efuse 0x173[1]		 0x316[1]
[0] R/W 0 B_AX_SDIO_DATA_SMT SD_DATA/SD_CMD Schmitt trigger setting:
1: enable Schmitt trigger of SD_DATA/SD_CMD, input Pad latch high/low follow SD SPEC.
0: disable Schmitt trigger of SD_DATA/SD_CMD, input Pad latch high/low follow 50% signal level.
autoload from efuse 0x173[0]		 0x316[0]
18604014h R_AX_SDIO_MONITOR_3_V1
[31:0] R/W 0 B_AX_mac_thr
18604018h R_AX_SDIO_LOCAL_REG_V1
[31:8] RO 30'h00000000 RSVD
[7:6] RW 1'b0 B_AX_rx_len_up_mode 在rx_async_FIFO read count大於等於rx_len_up_mode的情況下才會update rx_agg_length和發送rxdma_req。
2'b00: 0
2'b01: rx_fifo_depth/2
2'b10: rx_fifo_depth/4
2'b11: rx_fifo_depth
[5] RW 1'b0 B_AX_bypass_rx_int 0: sdio rx bypass read rx int source(0x4104) when sdio rx agg length break threshold
1: sdio need to read rx int source(0x4104) when sdio rx agg length bread threshold
[4] RO 1'b0 B_AX__mac_is_active
[3] RW 1'b1 B_AX_sdio_ioreg_rst
[2] RW 1'b0 B_AX_disable_0x800
[1] RW 1'b0 B_AX_en_cmd53_rx_mix_mode
[0] RW 1'b0 B_AX_en_rx_len_cmd_ok
1860401Ch R_AX_RSVD0
[31:16] RO 32'h00000000 RSVD
[15:0] RW 16'h0 B_AX_cmd11_timeout_threshold
18604030h R_AX_SDIO_HTSFR_INFO_V1
[31:16] R 0 B_AX_HTSFT2
[15:0] R 0 B_AX_HTSFT1
18604034h R_AX_SDIO_TX_IOREG_INFO
[31:3] RO 30'h00000000 RSVD
[2] RW 1'b0 B_AX_ioreg_w_busy_en
[1] RW 1'b0 B_AX_tx_trigger_delay
[0] RO 1'b0 RSVD
18604038h R_AX_RSVD1
[31:16] RO 16'h0 RSVD
[15:0] RO 16'h0 B_AX_hcpwm2_sdio_sync
18604040h R_AX_SDIO_INDIRECT_ADDR_V1
[31] W1C 0 B_AX_INDIRECT_RDY Polling to 1, it indicates the indirect access R/W is ready.
Write 1 Clear.
[30:0] R/W 0 B_AX_INDIRECT_ADDR When access HPON register using high-speed sdio clock, data timeout may occur, so indirect access will be used. This register sets the indirect access Ioreg Addr bit
18604044h R_AX_SDIO_INDIRECT_DATA_V1
[31:0] R/W 0 B_AX_INDIRECT_DATA When INDIRECT_ACCESS_READY flag is set to 1, host can read this register for required data. Here the read data has been rotated if INDIRECT_ADDR[1:0] != 2’b00 (non-four byte alignment)
e.g., when INDIRECT_ADDR[1:0] = 2’b01, the four-byte read data is ORIG_RDATA[31:0], INDIRECT_RDATA = {ORIG_RDATA[7:0], ORIG_RDATA[31:8]}
18604048h R_AX_SDIO_INDIRECT_CTRL_V1
[31:4] R 0 RSVD
[3] R/W 0 B_AX_INDIRECT_REG_R When host write this bit to 1, the indirect read request will be issued. This bit is always read as 0
[2] R/W 0 B_AX_INDIRECT_REG_W When host write this bit to 1, the indirect write request will be issued. This bit is always read as 0
[1:0] R/W 0 B_AX_INDIRECT_REG_SIZE INDIRECT_SIZE=0: Access one byte
INDIRECT_SIZE=1: Access two bytes
INDIRECT_SIZE=2: Access four bytes
Only indirect writing need to set this field.
18604070h R_AX_SDIO_TRX_STAT_V1
[31:15] R 0 RSVD
[14:12] R 0 B_AX_TX_DMA_WRAP_STAT_V1 0: idle
[11:8] R 0 B_AX_TX_DATA_STAGE_STAT_V1 0: idle
[7:4] R 0 B_AX_RX_DMA_WRAP_STAT_V1 0: idle
[3:0] R 0 B_AX_RX_DATA_STAGE_STAT_V1 0: idle
18604080h R_AX_SDIO_HRPWM1_V1
[31:16] R/W 0 B_AX_HRPWM
[15:0] R 0 RSVD
18604084h R_AX_SDIO_BUS_CTRL_V1
[31:24] R 0 RSVD
[23] R/W 0 B_AX_SDIO_PAD_CLK_S18A_BSS 8852D B cut 新增:
=1時，在1.8V下，sdio clk phase會在cmd52設定完CCCR reg 0x13(BSS)後，sdio clk phase才會由negedge改成posedge。
=0時，在1.8V下，sdio clk phase會在cmd5後，就會由negedge改成posedge。		 0x418[7]
[22] R 0 B_AX_r_cccr_ehs SDIO data output phase change immediately once CCCR offset 0x13 bus speed mode register value is rewritten
1: Rising edge , 0: Falling edge
[21] R 1 B_AX_ee_spi_clk_option 0x318[5]
[20] R/W 1 B_AX_INTR_CTRL This bit is written only by auto load, if load to 1, when device is parsing CMD, no interrupt will be issued during this time interval; otherwise, device can still issue interrupt to host even when CMD line is busy 0x318[4]
[19] R/W 0 B_AX_SDIO_VOLTAGE This bit is written only by auto load, it indicates the card’s working voltage, i.e., 3.3v or 1.8v 0x318[3]
[18] R/W 0 B_AX_BYPASS_INIT When this bit is auto load to 1, HW will by pass SDIO initial stage, and enter standby mode directly 0x318[2]
[17] R 0 B_AX_HCI_RESUME_RDY 1: WL in Active state, all power and clock are enabled and driver may normally operate in active mode.
0: WL in suspend mode. WL clock power are turned off.
[16] R/W 0 B_AX_HCI_SUS_REQ 0 -> 1: driver indicates to suspend. SYSON will report “0” to HCI_SUS_STS in Suspend mode.
1 -> 0: driver to resume WL function via CMD52 only. SYSON will report “1” to indicate WL resume power and clock ready.
[15] R/W 1 B_AX_fn1_crc16_sync_en
[14] R/W 0 B_AX_cmd53_rdata_early 1: CMD53 read support data start before Response end
若開啟此 function, 0x18[0] 要一起設 1, 不然在 Rx block+byte mode 會回傳重複 data
[13] R/W 0 B_AX_HISR_W_CLR_EN When set to 1, HW will by pass HIMR check to do HISR write 1 clear.
[12] R/W 0 B_AX_INT_MASK_DIS This bit is for sdio_clk >> ana_clk or mac_clk case. Its too late to wait sync ready and SD_D1 must leave interrupt state immediately after writing HIMR to 0.
0: Enable mask interrupt by sdio_clk domain.
1: Disable mask interrupt by sdio_clk domain.
[11] R/W 0 B_AX_PAD_CLK_XHGE_EN When host change bus speed mode, by writing CCCR related register, if this register is set to 1, output data sampling mode(posedge/negedge of SD CLK)will not changed until current CMD process is completed. Otherwise, data sampling mode will change immediately once CCCR register value is rewritten 0x317[3]
[10] R/W 0 B_AX_INTER_CLK_EN When set to 1, SDIO CLK will be gated for power saving if no command nor data is received 0x317[2]
[9] R/W 1 B_AX_EN_RPT_TXCRC When set to 1, device response negative ACK when received data has CRC errors
[8] R/W 1 B_AX_DIS_RXDMA_STS When set to 0, if device is under TXDMA mode, device will always response with positive ACK even if the received data has CRC error
18604088h R_AX_SDIO_RESPONSE_TIMER_V1
[31:24] R 0 RSVD
[23:16] R 0 B_AX_SDIO_CMD_CRC Display the calculated CRC value of the SDIO CMD which is considered CRC ERR
[15:0] R/W 0x0A B_AX_CMDIN_2RESP_TIMER This timer define the timeout interval between command and response, if this timeout interval is reached, the response will be forced to output
18604090h R_AX_SDIO_HSISR_V1
[31:24] R/W 0 B_AX_sdio_resp_flag_en
[23:16] R/W 0 B_AX_sdio_resp_flag
[15:9] R 0 RSVD
[8] R/W 0 B_AX_HISR_MASK SDIO Host system interrupt status mask , write 1 to enable, 0 to mask
[7:2] R 0 RSVD
[1] RW 0 B_AX_DRV_WLAN_INT_CLR Write 1 to clear DRV_WLAN_INT
[0] R 0 B_AX_DRV_WLAN_INT Read only, interrupt status from HISR, mainly including power on , power off , SPS_OCP, GPIO interrupt
18604094h R_AX_SDIO_EXTEND_RBLOK_GAP_V1
[31:6] R 0 RSVD
[5:0] R/W 0 B_AX_Extend_rblock_gap SDIO support adjustable Read Block Gap
SDR104/DDR50: (4+7+ Extend_rblock_gap) * SD_CLK
Others: : (4+3+ Extend_rblock_gap) * SD_CLK
186040C0h R_AX_SDIO_DIOERR_RPT_V1
[31:24] R 0 B_AX_DATA_CRC_ERR_CNT The number of CRC error of SDIO Data Block
[23:16] R 0 B_AX_CMD_CRC_ERR_CNT The number of CRC error of SDIO Command
[15:0] R 0 RSVD
186040C4h R_AX_SDIO_CMD_ERR_CONTENT_L_V1
[31:0] R 0 B_AX_SDIO_CMD_ERR_CONTENT_L Cmd 53: {0xC8,0xC7,0xC6,0xC5,0xC4} = {start bit, direction bit, command index, r/w flag, function number, block mode, OP code, register address, byte/block mode};
Cmd 52: {0xC8,0xC7,0xC6,0xC5,0xC4} = {start bit, direction bit, command index, r/w flag, function number, raw flag, Stuff, register address, stuff, write data};
186040C8h R_AX_SDIO_CMD_ERR_CONTENT_V1
[31:16] R 0 B_AX_SDIO_DATA_CRC Display the CRC value of the SDIO DATA which is considered CRC ERR
[15] R 0 B_AX_FN1_WDATA_TO_FLG CMD53 write FN1, 若沒收到write data, 則
當收到另一個CMD52/CMD53 read/write FN1 register,
觸發timeout event, 設B_AX_WDATA_TO_FLG = 1

Note: 若另一個CMD52是直接read B_AX_WDATA_TO_FLG, 可能無法當下反應此timeout event至R_data, 需要再read一次
[14] R 0 B_AX_FN1_WDATA_LEN_SHORT_FLG CMD53 write FN1, 若有收到write data但length尚不足, 則
當收到另一個CMD52/CMD53 read/write FN1 register,
觸發timeout event, 設B_AX_WDATA_LEN_SHORT_FLG = 1

Note: 若另一個CMD52是直接read B_AX_WDATA_LEN_SHORT_FLG, 可能無法當下反應此timeout event至R_data, 需要再read一次
[13] R 0 RSVD
[12] R 0 B_AX_D3_CRC_ERR Indicate there is crc error on SD_D3
[11] R 0 B_AX_D2_CRC_ERR Indicate there is crc error on SD_D2
[10] R 0 B_AX_D1_CRC_ERR Indicate there is crc error on SD_D1
[9] R 0 B_AX_D0_CRC_ERR Indicate there is crc error on SD_D0
[8] R 0 B_AX_CMD_CRC_ERR Indicate there is crc error on SD_CMD
[7:0] R 0 B_AX_SDIO_CMD_ERR_CONTENT_H Cmd 53: {0xC8,0xC7,0xC6,0xC5,0xC4} = {start bit, direction bit, command index, r/w flag, function number, block mode, OP code, register address, byte/block mode};
Cmd 52: {0xC8,0xC7,0xC6,0xC5,0xC4} = {start bit, direction bit, command index, r/w flag, function number, raw flag, Stuff, register address, stuff, write data};

[1] R/W 0 B_AX_SDIO_AVAL_INT_EN HCI channel available page Indication Interrupt:
This bit is set when LR.AVAL _IND INT is issued due to available page is greater than threshold and AVAL_IND_EN is set to 1.
[0] R/W 0 B_AX_RX_REQUEST_INT_EN This bit is set when one complete RX aggregation packet is received into RXPKTBUF and RX_REQUEST _EN is set to 1.
Only when RXPKTBUF is empty, the bit becomes zero.

[31] R/W 0 RSVD

[3] R 0 B_AX_HD1ISR_IND
[2] R 0 B_AX_HD0ISR_IND
[1] R 0 B_AX_SDIO_AVAL_INT HCI channel available page Indication Interrupt:
This bit is set when LR.AVAL _IND INT is issued due to available page is greater than threshold and B_AX_SDIO_AVAL_INT_EN is set to 1.
Only when all of B_AX_ACH_INTRPT_STAT are 0, the bit becomes zero.
Note: also support W1C
[0] R 0 B_AX_RX_REQUEST_INT This bit is set when one complete RX aggregation packet is received into RXPKTBUF and B_AX_RX_REQUEST_INT_EN is set to 1.
Only when RXPKTBUF is empty, the bit becomes zero.
Note: also support W1C
18604108h R_AX_SDIO_RX_REQ_LEN_V1
[31:18] R 0 RSVD
[17:0] R/W 0 B_AX_RX_REQ_LEN The length of RX Request ready in RXPKTBUF 0. The unit is byte. THE MAX length is 256K-1. This register is real-time mapping from RXDMA

0 : Enable time out check
1 : Disable time out check
[14] $R/W 0 B_AX_CRC_CHK_OPT_V1 crc16 error check optionally
[13] $R/W 0 B_AX_FORCE_PCERST_V1 Force PCE reset
[12] $R/W 0 B_AX_FORCE_TOGL_V1 Force toggle enable
[11] $R/W 0 B_AX_FORCE_TOGLSEL_V1 Force toggle bit select
1: DATA1
0: DATA0
[10] $R/W 0 B_AX_FORCE_PIDSW_V1 Force PID enable
[9] $R/W 0 B_AX_FORCE_PCE_IN_V1 Set this bit to emulate an IN token to SIE
[8] $R/W 0 B_AX_FORCE_PCE_OUT_V1 Set this bit to emulate an OUT token to SIE
[7:0] $R/W 0 B_AX_PID_FORCE_V1 Force PID value for test
18605004h R_AX_USB2_MAC_1_V1
[31] $R/W 0 B_AX_FORCE_PCE_CMD_V1
[30:0] R/W 0 RSVD RSVD
18605008h R_AX_USB2_LINK_PORT_V1
[31:24] R/W 0 RSVD RSVD
[23] R/W 0 B_AX_R_HOST_PWR_CTRL_V1
[22] R/W 0 B_AX_R_USB2_CLR_TXVLD_V1 Set 1 to clear txvalid after detecting a pid token
[21] $R/W 0 B_AX_R_USB2_SE0_V1 Set 1 to force internal USB20 se0 reset
[20] R/W 0 B_AX_HOST_RESUME_EDGE_EN_V1 host_resume enable
[19:16] R/W 0 B_AX_RESUME_SEL_V1 reume chirp K timing
[15:14] R/W 0x2 B_AX_DELAY CHIRP K_V1 Chirp K Latency, timing delay to reply the SE0 reset from host
00: 50us
01: 30us
10: 10us
11: 0us
[13] $R/W 0 B_AX_FORCE_TXVLD1_V1 Force TXVLD = 1'b1
[12] $R/W 0 B_AX_FORCE_TXVLD0_V1 Force TXVLD = 1'b0
[11] $R/W 0 B_AX_DORCE_DAT1_V1 Force TXDAT = 16'Hffff
[10] $R/W 0 B_AX_FORCE_DAT0_V1 Force TXDAT = 16'h0
[9] $R/W 0 B_AX_LS_TEST_V1 Set this bit will change timer of Line State FSM.
[8] $R/W 0 B_AX_LS_CHANGE_V1 Swap line state.
1: LS[0], LS[1]
0: LS[1], LS[0]
[7] $R/W 0 B_AX_FORCE_HS_SW_V1 Force PHY into HS mode. (XCVR=0, TERM=0)
[6] $R/W 0 B_AX_FORCE_FS_SW_V1 Force PHY into FS mode. (XCVR=1, TERM=1)
[5] $R/W 0 B_AX_FORCE_HSXCVR_V1 Force PHY XCVR=0
[4] $R/W 0 B_AX_FORCE_FSXCVR_V1 Force PHY XCVR=1
[3] $R/W 0 B_AX_FORCE_HSTERM_V1 Force PHY TERM=0
[2] $R/W 0 B_AX_FORCE_FSTERM_V1 Force PHY TERM=1
[1] $R/W 0 B_AX_FORCE_NORM_SW_V1 Force OPMODE=00. (Normal)
[0] $R/W 0 B_AX_FORCE_DBSN_V1 Force OPMODE=10. (Disable bit stuffing and NRZI encoding)
18605010h R_AX_USB2_LPM_0_V1
[31:18] R/W 0 RSVD RSVD
[17] R/W 0x1 B_AX_USBPHY_PLL_ALIVE_V1 LPM_PLL_Alive
[16] R/W 0 B_AX_USB_LPM_MAX_EN_V1 0
[15] R/W 0 B_AX_USB_LPM_MIN_EN_V1 Recommended Baseline BESL valid
[14] R/W 0 B_AX_BESL_EN_V1 BESL enable
[13] R/W 0 B_AX_USB_LPM_NYET_EN_V1 Always response to NYET packet
[12] R/W 0x1 B_AX_USB_LPM_MAX_ACK_V1 Response to ACK or NYET if HIRD value should be less than 300us. 0:NYET
1:ACK
[11] R/W 0 B_AX_USB_LPM_EN_V1 Link Power Management(LPM) support
0 : Disable
1 : Enable
While 1: enable the capability and the bcdUSB = 210h, support USB 2.0 extendtion descriptor
Wiile 0: do not support this capability, bcdUSB = 200h, and response STALL for USB 2.0 extendtion descriptor request
[10] R/W 0 B_AX_USB2_SUSB_V1 USB PHY suspend
[9] R/W 0 B_AX_LPM_PLL_ALIVE_V1 USB PHY PLL aive in LPM mode
[8] R/W 0 B_AX_USB_LPS_OUT_V1
[7] R/W 0 RSVD
[6] R/W 0 B_AX_USB_LPM_WAKEUP_EN_V1 LPM remote wakeup enable.
A logic 1 enables remote wakeup from external WAKEUP# pin when suspend
[5] R/W 0 B_AX_NEVER_SUSPEND_V1 Never Suspend. A logic 1 makes SIE never enter suspend when bus idle
[4] R/W 0 B_AX_SUSPND_EN_V1 Suspend Enable. A logic 1 enables suspend from USB root port.
[3] R/W 0 B_AX_WAKEUP_EN_V1 Root Port Resume Enable. A logic 1 enables resume from USB root port.
[2] R/W 0 B_AX_USB_SUS_WAKEUP_EN_V1 Suspend Remote Wakeup Enable
[1] R/W 0 B_AX_RESUME_SND_V1
[0] $R/W 0 B_AX_CONNECT_EN_V1 USB Connection.
If this bit is set, the OPMODE of UTMI will be switched to “Normal” when VBUS is high. If this bit is reset, the OPMODE of UTMI is switched to “Non-Driving” when VBUS is high. When VBUS is low, OPMODE is switched to “Non-Driving”.
18605014h R_AX_USB2_LPM_1_V1
[31:24] R/W 0 RSVD RSVD
[23:20] R/W 0 B_AX_USB_LPM_MAX_V1 Recommended Deep BESL value.Field shall be ignored by system software if Deep BESL valid is a zero.
[19:16] R/W 0x2 B_AX_USB_LPM_MIN_V1 Recommended Baseline BESL value.Field shall be ignored by system software if Baseline BESL valid is a zero.
[15:8] R/W 0 B_AX_R_WAKE_HOST_WT_H_V1 allow wakeup timer[15:8]
[7:0] R/W 0 B_AX_R_WAKE_HOST_WT_L_V1 allow wakeup timer[7:0]
18605018h R_AX_USB2_MACRO_TEST_MODE_V1
[31:15] R/W 0 RSVD RSVD
[14] R/W 0 B_AX_TXRDY_SLB_SEL_V1 Select non pipe TXRDY for self-loop-back test
1: non pipe TXRDY
0: 1 pipe TXRDY
[13] R/W 0 B_AX_SLB_EN_V1 Enable self-loop-back
[12] R/W 0 B_AX_SLB_RST_V1 Reset self-loop-back
[11] R 0 B_AX_SLB_FAIL_V1 Indicate self-loop-back compare fail
[10] R 0 B_AX_SLB_DONE_V1 Indicate self-loop-back done
[9:8] R/W 0 B_AX_SLB_PS1_SW_V1 Select self-loop-back pattern
00 : all zeros
01 : load from SLBSEED
10 : pseudo random pattern
11 : incremental counter
[7:4] R/W 0 RSVD RSVD
[3] R/W 0 B_AX_PHY_LOOP_TEST_V1 Force SIE into Self-loop-back test, RX data will be stored at selected EP buffer.
[2:0] R/W 0 B_AX_USBTMOD_V1 USB Test Mode. Compliance testing method defined by USB Spec 2.0.
000: Normal
001: Test_SE0_NAK
010: Test_J
011: Test_K
100: Test_Packet

1 : Enable
ep0 is dependent on setup token bit7

4’h0: disable watchdog timer
4’h1: 2ms timeout
4’h2: 4ms timeout
…
4’hF: 30ms timeout
[3] R/W 0 B_AX_ON_IOH_HW_AUTO_RST_V1 HW auto reset IO ONREG wrapper enable (response 32’EAEAEAEA to bus in reset period )
1’b1: enable HW auto reset
1’b0: disable HW auto reset
[2] R/W 0 B_AX_ON_IOH_EMPTY_V1 SW reset IO ONREG wrapper enable (response 32’EAEAEAEA to bus in reset period )
1b’1: assert a SW reset pulse, clear by HW
1’b0: no activity
[1] W1C 0 RSVD RSVD
[0] R/W 0 B_AX_ON_IOH_RST_STS_V1 Reset status
1’b1: reset active
1’b0: reset idle

18606000h R_AX_RXAGG_0_V1
[31] R/W 0 B_AX_RXAGG_EN Enable RX Aggregation function
[30] R/W 0 B_AX_RXAGG_DMA_STORE Enable dma agg
[29] R/W 0 B_AX_RXAGG_SW_EN enable SW control RXAGG stop
[28] W 0 B_AX_RXAGG_SW_TRIG SW write 1 to break RXAGG
[27:24] R/W 0 RSVD RSVD
[23:16] R/W 0 B_AX_RXAGG_PKTNUM_TH RXDMA aggregation packet number threshold
[15:8] R/W 0 B_AX_RXAGG_TIMEOUT_TH RXDMA aggregation timeout threshold : unit : 32us(usb), 1us(sdio)
[7:0] R/W 0 B_AX_RXAGG_LEN_TH When RXDMA_AGG_EN is set, then RXDMA will inform host to receive RX packets, when packet page number in RXFF exceeds the threshold set in RXDMA_AGG_PG_TH register.
Unit : 4k for USB aggregation mode 1k for Rx DMA aggregation mode
18606004h R_AX_RXAGG_1_V1
[31:18] R/W 0 RSVD RSVD
[17:16] R/W 0 B_AX_RXAGG_LEN_TH_HIGH_V1 When r_rxagg_en is set, then RXDMA will inform host to receive RX packets, when packet page number in RXFF exceeds the threshold set in r_rxagg_len_th register. Unit : 1k
[15:8] R/W 0 RSVD RSVD
[7:5] R/W 0 B_AX_RXAGG_SML_PKT_SIZE_V1 The parameter of target pkt length during rx agg process, where target pkt length = 64x2^(B_AX_PKTLEN_PARA-1) bytes.
[4:0] R/W 0 B_AX_RXAGG_SML_PKTNUM_TH RXDMA breaks rx agg when the amount of aggregated pkts of length smaller than target pkt length exceeds this threshold.

18607000h R_AX_GT0_CTRL_V1
[31] W1T 0 B_AX_GT_COUNT_EN When write “1” to this bit, GTimer starts counting down. This is a one-bit pulse.
[30] R/W 0 B_AX_GT_MODE GTimer mode
0 = counter mode
1 = timer mode
[29] $R/W 0 B_AX_GT_EN GTimer enable
HW auto clear this enable bit when counter count down to zero in counter mode.
[28] R/W 0 B_AX_GT_SORT_EN GTimer sorting enable.
0 = HW timer sorting does not consider Gtimer
1 = HW timer sorting will consider Gtimer
[27:0] R/W 0 B_AX_GT_DATA GTimer data register. It specifies the time-out duration. Unit:1us
18607004h R_AX_GT0_CNT_V1
[31:28] R/W 0 rsvd
[27:0] $R/W 0 B_AX_GT_CNT GTimer Counter
When B_AX_GT_COUNT_EN=1 & B_AX_GT_DATA=1, B_AX_GT_CNT will be count down from B_AX_GT_DATA. When B_AX_GT_CNT count down to 0, interrupt will be issued.
This field is updated by FW/HIOE in PGLPS restore flow. Else, it is updated by HW.
18607008h R_AX_GT1_CTRL_V1
[31] W1T 0 B_AX_GT_COUNT_EN When write “1” to this bit, GTimer starts counting down. This is a one-bit pulse.
[30] R/W 0 B_AX_GT_MODE GTimer mode
0 = counter mode
1 = timer mode
[29] $R/W 0 B_AX_GT_EN GTimer enable
HW auto clear this enable bit when counter count down to zero in counter mode.
[28] R/W 0 B_AX_GT_SORT_EN GTimer sorting enable.
0 = HW timer sorting does not consider Gtimer
1 = HW timer sorting will consider Gtimer
[27:0] R/W 0 B_AX_GT_DATA GTimer data register. It specifies the time-out duration. Unit:1us

18607024h R_AX_SORT_CTRL_V1
[31:2] R/W 0 RSVD RSVD
[1] R/W 0 B_AX_CMAC1_SORT_EN CMAC1 sorting enable
When B_AX_CMAC1_SORT_EN=1, HW will sort CMAC1 bcn early timers and ps timers.
If CMAC1 is inactive, B_AX_CMAC1_SORT_EN must be set to 0.
[0] R/W 0 B_AX_CMAC0_SORT_EN CMAC0 sorting enable
When B_AX_CMAC0_SORT_EN=1, HW will sort CMAC0 bcn early timers and ps timers.

18607160h R_AX_H2CREG_CTRL_V1
[31:1] R/W 0 rsvd
[0] R/W 0 B_AX_H2CREG_TRIGGER Write 1 to trigger H2C REG interrupt to FW.
(After receiving H2C, FW must write 0 to B_AX_H2C_REG_TRIGGER to let the host know the data has received)
18607164h R_AX_C2HREG_CTRL_V1
[31:1] R/W 0 rsvd
[0] R/W 0 B_AX_C2HREG_TRIGGER Write 1 to trigger C2H REG interrupt to the host.
(After receiving C2H, the host must write 0 to B_AX_C2H_REG_TRIGGER to let FW know the data has received)
18607170h R_AX_CPWM_V1
[31:16] R/W 0 rsvd
[15] R/W 0 B_AX_CPWM_TOGGLE If FW toggle this bit, CPWM HISR will be issued
[14:0] R/W 0 B_AX_CPWM_VAL CPWM value
18607180h R_AX_ANACLK_CAL_CTRL_V1
[31] R/W 0 B_AX_ANACLK_CAL_EN 1: polling CK Calibration enable and indicate busy when calibration is on going
0: calibration is done
[30:26] R/W 0 RSVD RSVD
[25:24] R/W 0 B_AX_ANACLK_SEL 00: caliabration of 12M for 16 cycles
01: calibration of 32K for 8 cycles
10, 11: rsvd
[23:16] R/W 0 RSVD RSVD
[15:0] R/W 0 B_AX_ANACLK_CAL_RPT the calibration value of ANA clock where the calibration clock is MAC clock.
18607190h R_AX_OTP_PARA
[31:6] R/W 0 RSVD
[5:4] R/W 1 B_AX_OTP_PWE_H_PARA OTP PWE high level parameter.
0 : 10us~11us / 1 : 12us~13us / 2 : 14us~15us / 3 : 15us~16us
[3:2] R/W 0 RSVD
[1:0] R/W 1 B_AX_OTP_PWE_L_PARA OTP PWE low level parameter.
0 : 1us~2us / 1 : 2us~3us / 2 : 3us~4us / 3 : 5us~6us

18607800h R_AX_FWD1IMR_V1
[31] R/W 0 RSVD
[30] R/W 0 RSVD
[29] R/W 0 RSVD
[28] R/W 0 RSVD
[27] R/W 0 RSVD

[17] R/W 0 RSVD
[16] R/W 0 B_AX_FS_TM_WAKE_INT_EN Enable the interrupt source that 32K wakeup due to LPS wake timer.
[15] R/W 0 RSVD
[14] R/W 0 RSVD

[12] R/W 0 RSVD

[5] R/W 0 RSVD
[4] R/W 0 RSVD
[3] R/W 0 RSVD
[2] R/W 0 RSVD
[1] R/W 0 B_AX_FS_BT_Mailbox_INT_EN Enable Mailbox INT (BT Mailbox to WIFI)
[0] R/W 0 B_AX_FS_RPWM_INT_EN Enable RPWM interrupt source (wakeup from HWLPS)
18607804h R_AX_FWD1ISR_V1
[31] R/W1C 0 RSVD
[30] R/W1C 0 RSVD
[29] R/W1C 0 RSVD
[28] R/W1C 0 RSVD
[27] R/W1C 0 RSVD
[26] R/W1C 0 RSVD
[25] R/W1C 0 RSVD
[24] R/W1C 0 RSVD
[23] R/W1C 0 RSVD
[22] R/W1C 0 RSVD
[21] R/W1C 0 RSVD
[20] R/W1C 0 RSVD
[19] R/W1C 0 RSVD
[18] R/W1C 0 RSVD
[17] R/W1C 0 RSVD
[16] R/W1C 0 B_AX_FS_TM_WAKE_INT When 32K wakeup due to LPS wake timer, issue this interrupt.
[15] R/W1C 0 RSVD
[14] R/W1C 0 RSVD
[13] R/W1C 0 RSVD
[12] R/W1C 0 RSVD
[11] R/W1C 0 RSVD

[3] R/W1C 0 RSVD
[2] R/W1C 0 RSVD
[1] R/W1C 0 B_AX_FS_BT_Mailbox_INT The interrupt is issued while BT trigger Mailbox (BT Mailbox to WIFI)
[0] R/W1C 0 B_AX_FS_RPWM_INT The interrupt is issued while RPWM toggle bit changed. (wakeup from HWLPS)
18607808h R_AX_FSMIMR_V1
[31] R/W 0 RSVD
[30] R/W 0 RSVD
[29] R/W 0 RSVD
[28] R/W 0 RSVD
[27] R/W 0 RSVD
[26] R/W 0 RSVD
[25] R/W 0 RSVD
[24] R/W 0 RSVD
[23] R/W 0 RSVD
[22] R/W 0 RSVD
[21] R/W 0 RSVD
[20] R/W 0 RSVD
[19] R/W 0 RSVD
[18] R/W 0 RSVD
[17] R/W 0 RSVD
[16] R/W 0 RSVD
[15] R/W 0 RSVD
[14] R/W 0 RSVD
[13] R/W 0 RSVD
[12] R/W 0 RSVD
[11] R/W 0 RSVD
[10] R/W 0 RSVD
[9] R/W 0 RSVD
[8] R/W 0 RSVD
[7] R/W 0 RSVD
[6] R/W 0 B_AX_FSM_RP_END_EVENT_IMR 1: Enable Rx period end event. No wake WCPU.
0: Disable Rx period end event. No wake WCPU.
[5] R/W 0 B_AX_FSM_RX_BCN_TO_CNT_EVENT_IMR 1: Enable Rx bcn timeout counter event wake WCPU
0: Disable Rx bcn timeout counter event wake WCPU
[4] R/W 0 B_AX_FSM_RX_MATCH_EVENT_IMR 1: Enable Rx match event wake WCPU
0: Disable Rx match event wake WCPU
[3] R/W 0 B_AX_FSM_HIOE_ERR_EVENT_IMR 1: Enable HIOE error event wake WCPU
0: Disable HIOE error event wake WCPU
[2] R/W 0 B_AX_FSM_OTHERS_WAKE_EVENT_IMR 1: Enable others wake event wake WCPU
0: Disable others wake event wake WCPU
[1] R/W 0 B_AX_FSM_TIMER_TO_EVENT_IMR 1: Enable other timer timeout event wake WCPU
0: Disable other timer timeout event wake WCPU
[0] R/W 0 rsvd
1860780Ch R_AX_FSMISR_V1
[31] RO 0 RSVD
[30] RO 0 RSVD
[29] RO 0 RSVD
[28] RO 0 RSVD
[27] RO 0 RSVD
[26] RO 0 RSVD
[25] RO 0 RSVD
[24] RO 0 RSVD
[23] RO 0 RSVD
[22] RO 0 RSVD
[21] RO 0 RSVD
[20] RO 0 RSVD
[19] RO 0 RSVD
[18] RO 0 RSVD
[17] RO 0 RSVD
[16] RO 0 RSVD
[15] RO 0 RSVD
[14] RO 0 RSVD
[13] RO 0 RSVD
[12] RO 0 RSVD
[11] RO 0 RSVD
[10] RO 0 RSVD
[9] RO 0 RSVD
[8] RO 0 RSVD
[7] RO 0 RSVD
[6] RO 0 B_AX_FSM_RP_END_EVENT_ISR This event is triggered from (FS_RP_END_INT & FS_RP_END_INT_EN).
When this event happened and IMR = 1, FSM load the register of R_AX_HIOE_STR_ADDR & R_AX_HIOE_END_ADDR to HIOE STR/END address, then load the register of R_AX_BKP_HIOE_CTRL to R_PL_HIOE_CTRL.
[5] RO 0 B_AX_FSM_RX_BCN_TO_CNT_EVENT_ISR This event is triggered from (FS_RXBCN_TO_CNT_INT. & FS_RXBCN_TO_CNT_INT._EN)
When BCN_TO_CNT > BCN_TO_CNT_THD, issue this interrupt to wake WCPU
[4] RO 0 B_AX_FSM_RX_MATCH_EVENT_ISR This event is triggered from
( (FS_WWLAN_INT & FS_WWLAN_INT_EN) | (FS_BCN_HIT_INT & FS_BCN_HIT_INT_EN) )
When Rx packet match wakeup pattern or Rx Bcn hit (IE_HIT/BCAID_HIT/UNIAID_HIT), issue this interrupt to wake WCPU
[3] RO 0 B_AX_FSM_HIOE_ERR_EVENT_ISR This event is triggered from (FS_HIOE_ERR_INT & FS_HIOE_ERR_INT_EN).
When HIOE timeout or format error, issue this interrupt to wake WCPU
[2] RO 0 B_AX_FSM_OTHERS_WAKE_EVENT_ISR When others wake source triggered and imr is enabled, issue this interrupt to wake WCPU
Others wake event include
- All (FWISRs & FWIMRs) in AON domain
- - (FWD1ISR[15:0] & FWD1IMR[15:0]) in PON domain
[1] RO 0 B_AX_FSM_TIMER_TO_EVENT_ISR When others timer timeout triggered and corresponding imr =1, issue this interrupt to wake WCPU.
Timers include
- GTimer
- PSTimer
[0] RO 0 rsvd
18607810h R_AX_TM_BKP_RES_CTRL_V1
[31:14] R/W 0 RSVD
[13:12] RO 0 B_AX_SORTING_ROLE_SEL_V1 After sorting done, this field will be updated.
2’b00 for Rsvd
2’b01 for CMAC0
2’b10 for CMAC1
2’b11 for GTimer
[11:8] RO 0 B_AX_SORTING_ROLE_ST_V1 After sorting done, this field will be updated.
GTimer: B_AX_SORTING_ROLE_SEL[1:0] = 2'b11
Sorting_role[3:0] = 4’b0000: GT0
Sorting_role[3:0] = 4’b0001: GT1
Sorting_role[3:0] = 4’b0010: GT2
Sorting_role[3:0] = 4’b0011: GT3
Sorting_role[3:0] = 4’b0100: GT4
Sorting_role[3:0] = 4’b0101: GT5
Sorting_role[3:0] = 4’b0110: GT6
Sorting_role[3:0] = 4’b0111: GT7

CMAC0: B_AX_SORTING_ROLE_SEL[1:0] = 2'b01
CMAC1: B_AX_SORTING_ROLE_SEL[1:0] = 2'b10
Sorting_role[3:0] = 0 : port 0 tbtt
Sorting_role[3:0] = 1 : port 1 tbtt
Sorting_role[3:0] = 2 : port 2 tbtt
Sorting_role[3:0] = 3 : port 3 tbtt
Sorting_role[3:0] = 4 : port 4 tbtt
Sorting_role[3:0] = 5~7 : RSVD
Sorting_role[3:0] = 8 : ps timer 0
Sorting_role[3:0] = 9 : ps timer 1
Sorting_role[3:0] = 10 : ps timer 2
Sorting_role[3:0] = 11 : ps timer 3
Sorting_role[3:0] = 12 : ps timer 4
Sorting_role[3:0] = 13 : ps timer 5
Sorting_role[3:0] = 14~15 : RSVD

RSVD: B_AX_SORTING_ROLE_SEL[1:0] = 2'b11
[7] RO 0 B_AX_TM_WAKE_IND LPS Wake Timer wake indication
1: Wake by LPS Wake Timer
0: Not wake by LPS Wake Timer
[6] RO 0 B_AX_TM_BKP_EN_STS Timer backup enable status
1: If PRE_CHK_DONE=1 & PRE_CHK_VALID, BKP_EN=1, this bit is set ti 1
0: After TM_RES_EN=0, this bit is clear to 0
[5] RW 0 RSVD
[4] RW 0 B_AX_SCH_RES_EN Scheduler restore enable.
[3] RW 0 B_AX_PRE_CHK_DONE LPS Wake Timer Pre-check Done
After Pre-check finished, this bit is set to 1. This bit is cleared by CPU/HIOE before set TM_BKP_EN=1.
[2] RW 0 B_AX_PRE_CHK_VALID LPS Wake Timer Pre-check valid bit
If PRE_CHK_DONE =1 & this bit = 1, Pre-check success. HW stops counting all timers. WTM start to count with dmac-clk.
If PRE_CHK_DONE =1 & this bit = 0, Pre-check fail. HW clears TM_BKP_EN to 0.
[1] RW 0 B_AX_TM_RES_EN Timer restore enable
Set this bit to 1:
- Make “off domain timer except TSFT” start counting
- Compensate counter while (HIOE/DDMA/CPU) write value to counter.
Set this bit to 0:
- HW Clear B_AX_TM_BKP_EN_STS
- LPS_WTM_CNT stop counting.
- All circuit start to work
[0] RW 0 B_AX_TM_BKP_EN_TRIGGER Timer backup enable trigger
CPU/HIOE set this bit to 1 and try to enter LPS,, then HW do pre_check (save the sorted value of to LPS_WTM_SC, and compare the value with PRE_CHK_THD),
After PRE_CHK_DONE=1, then
- If PRE_CHK_VALID =0, stop backup flow.
- If PRE_CHK_VALID =1, start backup flow and stop all off domain timer. LPS_WTM_CNT start to count.

0 -> 1:
 HW Clear PRE_CHK_DONE, PRE_CHK_VALID, B_AX_TM_WAKE_IND
 LPS_WTM_CNT will reset to 0.
HW do pre_check flow.
1->0:
 This bit is cleared by HW after PRE_CHK_DONE=1.
18607814h R_AX_PRE_CHK_CTRL_V1
[31:16] R/W 0 B_AX_PRE_CHK_THD LPS Wake Timer Pre-check threshold
Sort the minimum value of off domain timer. If the value >= PRE_CHK_THD, LPS_PRE_CHK_VALID will be set to 1. Else, LPS_PRE_CHK_VALID reset to 0
Unit: 1us
[15:0] R/W 0 B_AX_PRE_WAKE_TIME LPS Wake Timer Pre-wakeup time.
When (TM_BKP_EN =1) & (PRE_CHK_VALID =1), LPS_WTM_CNT start counting. TIMERS_TO_EVENT will be issued when
LPS_WTM_CNT >= (LPS_WTM_SC - PRE_WAKE_TIME)
Unit: 1us
18607818h R_AX_LPS_WTM_SC_V1
[31:0] RW 0 B_AX_LPS_WTM_SC LPS Wake Timer Sorting Counter
When LPS_WTM_CNT >= (LPS_WTM_SC - PRE_WAKE_TIME) , issue TIMER_TO_EVENT_INT to FSM.
This counter is the min sorting timer value. Timers need to be sorting including
- GTimer,
- PSTimer,
- BCNERLY
1860781Ch R_AX_LPS_WTM_CNT_V1
[31:0] RO 0 B_AX_LPS_WTM_CNT LPS Wake Timer Counter.
When TM_BKP_EN=1, this counter will reset to 0.
When TM_BKP_EN=1 & PRE_CHK_VALID=1, this counter will start to count.
Unit: 1us
18607820h R_AX_TSF_32K_SEL_V1
[31:18] R/W 0 rsvd
[17] RO 0 B_AX_TSF_CLK_STABLE Indicate that TSF 32k/dmac_clk is stable
1’b1= TSF is stable in 32K,
1’b0 = TFS is stable in dmac_clk
[16] R/W 0 B_AX_CKSL_WLTSF TSF clock select:
1’b1 = TSF is 32K,
1’b0 = TSF is dmac_clk.
[15:9] R/W 0 rsvd
[8] R/W 0 B_AX_32K_SRC_SEL 32k clock source select:
1’b1 = 32.768K,
1’b0 = 32K.
[7:0] R/W 0xA0 B_AX_US_TIME_VALUE cycle counts of dmac_clk for 1us.
18607840h R_AX_HIOE_END_ADDR_V1
[31:0] R/W 0 B_AX_HIOE_END_ADDR FSM load this register to B_PL_INST_END_ADDR in below case:
LPS resume & (B_AX_LPSROP_CPU = 0) & (B_AX_LPSROP_HIOE = 1)
(B_AX_FSM_RP_END_EVENT_IMR = 1) & (B_AX_FSM_RP_END_EVENT_ISR = 1) & (B_AX_LPSROP_HIOE = 1)
18607844h R_AX_HIOE_STR_ADDR_V1
[31:0] R/W 0 B_AX_HIOE_STR_ADDR FSM load this register to B_PL_INST_STR_ADDR in below case:
- LPS resume & (B_AX_LPSROP_CPU = 0) & (B_AX_LPSROP_HIOE = 1)
- (B_AX_FSM_RP_END_EVENT_IMR = 1) & (B_AX_FSM_RP_END_EVENT_ISR = 1) & (B_AX_LPSROP_HIOE = 1)
18607848h R_AX_BKP_HIOE_CTRL_V1
[31:16] R/W 0 B_AX_BKP_HIOE_CTRL FSM load this register to R_PL_HIOE_CTRL in below case:
(B_AX_FSM_RP_END_EVENT_IMR = 1) & (B_AX_FSM_RP_END_EVENT_ISR = 1) & (B_AX_LPSROP_HIOE = 1)
[15:0] R/W 0 rsvd
1860784Ch R_AX_RES_HIOE_CTRL_V1
[31:16] R/W 0 B_AX_RES_HIOE_CTRL FSM load this register to R_PL_HIOE_CTRL in below case:
LPS resume & (B_AX_LPSROP_CPU = 0) & (B_AX_LPSROP_HIOE = 1)
[15:0] R/W 0 rsvd
18607880h R_AX_HCI_FUNC_EN_V1
[31] R/W 0 B_AX_HCI_CR_PROTECT
[30:3] R/W 0 rsvd
[2] R/W 1 B_AX_HCI_TRXBUF_EN
[1] R/W 0 B_AX_HCI_RXDMA_EN
[0] R/W 0 B_AX_HCI_TXDMA_EN
18607894h R_AX_OSC_32K_CTRL_V1

[30:4] R/W 0 RSVD RSVD
[3] R/W 0 B_AX_CAL_32K_DBG_SEL Selection for debug output

18607898h R_AX_32K_CAL_REG0_V1

[30:23] R/W 0 RSVD RSVD

[15:0] R/W 0 B_AX_CAL_32K_REG_DATA Register read/write data of 32K calibration
1860789Ch R_AX_OSC32K_RCAL_V1
[31:15] R/W 0 RSVD
[14:0] RO 0x10 B_AX_OSC32K_RCAL
186078F0h R_AX_BOOT_DBG_V1
[31:16] R/W 0 B_AX_BOOT_STATUS Boot status record register
[15:0] R/W 0 B_AX_SECUREBOOT_STATUS Secure boot status record register
18608400h R_AX_DMAC_FUNC_EN
[31] RW 1'b0 B_AX_DMAC_CRPRT
[30] RW 1'b0 B_AX_MAC_FUNC_EN
[29] RW 1'b0 B_AX_DMAC_FUNC_EN

18608410h R_AX_LTR_CTRL_0
[31:19] R/W 0 RSVD RSVD
[18] W1C 0 B_AX_LTR_REQ_FW Write 1 to request update LTR FW index
[17:16] R/W 0 B_AX_LTR_IDX_FW LTR FW index value
[15:11] R/W 0 RSVD RSVD
[10:8] R/W 0x5 B_AX_LTR_IDLE_TIMER_IDX LTR IDLE timer index. If TX DMA is idle and RX DMA is idle and RX packet buffer is empty, a timer gets started. After time set by the index expired, LTR idle message will be sent.
0: 100T; 1: 1000T; 2: 10000T; 3: 20000T; 4: 40000T; 5: 80000T; 6: 160000T; 7: 320000T. Unit: DMAC clock cycle.
[7:2] R/W 0 RSVD RSVD
[1] R/W 1 B_AX_LTR_WD_NOEMP_CHK_V1 1: Enable WD non-empty check condition for LTR idle -> active.
[0] R/W 0 B_AX_LTR_HW_EN 1: Enable hardware LTR state machine. If you want to disable HW LTR, use 0x1600[2] to disable HW LTR decision.
18608414h R_AX_LTR_CTRL_1
[31:28] R/W 0 RSVD RSVD
[27:16] R/W 0x28 B_AX_LTR_RX1_TH RX threshold for CMAC 1. Unit: page. Send LTR active msg if CMAC 1 RX used page is over this threshold.
[15:12] R/W 0 RSVD RSVD
[11:0] R/W 0x28 B_AX_LTR_RX0_TH RX threshold for CMAC 0. Unit: page. Send LTR active msg if CMAC 0 RX used page is over this threshold.
18608418h R_AX_LTR_IDLE_LATENCY
[31:2] R/W 0 RSVD RSVD
[1:0] R/W 0x3 B_AX_LTR_IDLE_IDX LTR idle idx for HW LTR.
1860841Ch R_AX_LTR_ACTIVE_LATENCY
[31:2] R/W 0 RSVD RSVD
[1:0] R/W 0x0 B_AX_LTR_ACT_IDX LTR act idx for HW LTR.

[31] R/W 0x0 B_AX_HWAMSDU_PADDING_MODE 0: The last AMSDU doesn’t add padding for 4 bytes alignment.(default follow spec)
1: The last AMSDU adds padding for 4 bytes alignment.

[5] R/W 0 B_AX_FS_TXPKTIN_INT_EN Enable the interrupt source that when host sends a packet into PKTIN
[4] R/W 0 B_AX_FS_WWLAN_INT_EN Enable HW detect WWLAN event interrupt source
[3] R/W 0 B_AX_FS_PLD_CPU_IO_PORT_Q1_INT_EN Indicate the packet has transmitted to CPU IO port queue1 of PLD_ENG.
[2] R/W 0 B_AX_FS_PLD_CPU_IO_PORT_Q0_INT_EN Indicate the packet has transmitted to CPU IO port queue0 of PLD_ENG.
[1] R/W 0 B_AX_FS_WD_CPU_IO_PORT_Q1_INT_EN Indicate the packet has transmitted to CPU IO port queue1 of WD_ENG.
[0] R/W 0 B_AX_FS_WD_CPU_IO_PORT_Q0_INT_EN Indicate the packet has transmitted to CPU IO port queue0 of WD_ENG.

[5] R/W1C 0 B_AX_FS_TXPKTIN_INT The interrupt is issued when host sends a packet into PKTIN, and match the enable case of R_AX_TXPKTIN_CTRL, PKTIN issues this interrupt to inform MCU.
[4] R/W1C 0 B_AX_FS_WWLAN_INT The interrupt is issued while HW detect WWLAN event.
[3] R/W1C 0 B_AX_FS_PLD_CPU_IO_PORT_Q1_INT The interrupt is issued while the packet has transmitted to CPU IO port queue1 of PLD_ENG.
[2] R/W1C 0 B_AX_FS_PLD_CPU_IO_PORT_Q0_INT The interrupt is issued while the packet has transmitted to CPU IO port queue0 of PLD_ENG.
[1] R/W1C 0 B_AX_FS_WD_CPU_IO_PORT_Q1_INT The interrupt is issued while the packet has transmitted to CPU IO port queue1 of WD_ENG.
[0] R/W1C 0 B_AX_FS_WD_CPU_IO_PORT_Q0_INT The interrupt is issued while the packet has transmitted to CPU IO port queue0 of WD_ENG.

[18] RO 1'd0 B_AX_cr_rxpkt_process P_AXIDMA RX process signal, when P_AXIDMA RX BUSY this signal is 1, until receive P_AXIDMA send packet_end, this signal will set to 0.
0: idle, 1: busy
[17] RO 1'd0 B_AX_cr_hci_last_dma
[16] RO 1'd0 B_AX_cr_hci_empty
[15] RO 1'd0 RSVD

[7:0] RO 8'hEE RSVD
18608908h R_AX_RXDMA_SETTING
[31:25] R 0 RSVD RSVD
[24] R/W 1 B_AX_PLE_BURST_READ 1: Set HDR read PLE always by 64 byte burst. 0: HDR will read PLE by fifo available size
[23:18] R 0 RSVD RSVD
[17:16] R/W 0 B_AX_REQ_DEPTH RX request to HCI when burst fifo full or empty. 2'b0:4/16 full, 2'01:8/16 full, 2'b10:12/16 full, 2'b11:14/16 full
[15:11] R 0 RSVD RSVD
[10] R/W 0 B_AX_BULK_TH_OPT Set HDR start RXAGG packet when meet the bulk count
[9:8] R/W 0 B_AX_BURST_CNT Bulk count number to start RXAGG packet
[7:2] R 0 RSVD RSVD
[1:0] R/W 1 B_AX_BULK_SIZE Every burst pkt len 2’b00 : 1KB ; 2’b01 : 512B ; 2’b10 : 64B ; Others : 64B
(P.S. USB 3.0 must set : 0x00 USB 2.0 HighRSVDspeed must set : 0x01 USB 2.0 FullRSVDspeed must set : 0x10)
1860890Ch R_AX_FWDL_CHECKSUM
[31:16] R 0 B_AX_FWDL_CHKSUM 16bit XOR checksum result
[15:3] R 0 RSVD RSVD
[2] R 0 B_AX_FWDL_CHKSUM_VALID Indicate Checksum result is valid( HW state is Idle )
[1] R/W1C 0 B_AX_FWDL_CHKSUM_RESULT Success of Fail of FWDL checksum result. Write 1 to clear r_FWDL_CHKSUM & r_FWDL_CHKSUM_Result.
[0] R/W 0 B_AX_FWDL_CHKSUM_EN Enable FWDL checksum function. Set this bit to 1 or 0 won't reset B_AX_FWDL_CHKSUM_RESULT.
18608910h R_AX_H2C_SETTING
[31:9] RO 23'd0 RSVD
[8] R/W 1'b0 B_AX_rx_cpu_port_sel
[7:1] RO 7'd0 RSVD
[0] R/W 1'd0 B_AX_remove_h2c_rxd
18608914h R_AX_RX_PPDU_STATUS_FW_MODE
[31] R/W 0 B_AX_HDR_PPDU_ENQ_WLCPU_EN Enable HDR enq PPDU status to WLCPU.que4 after transfer to Host RX. ( 0:Not enq to WLCPU )
[30:14] RO 0 RSVD RSVD
[13:0] R/W 0 B_AX_CDR_PPDU_2_WLCPU_LEN Set the length from WLCPU.que4 to AXIDMA_RX. (don't including RX descriptor)
0: transfer full packet
18608918h R_AX_RX_TCPIP_CHECKSUM_FUNCTION
[31:1] R 0 RSVD Rsvd
[0] R/W 0 B_AX_HDR_TCPIP_CHKSUM_EN Host dispatcher RX offload tcp/ip checksum function enable
1860891Ch R_AX_RX_FC_BD_VALID_MASK
[31:11] R 0 RSVD RSVD
[10:8] R/W 0 B_AX_CDR_BD_MASK_V1 Set 1 to mask CPU dispatcher RX BD Valid from AXI_DMA
[7:2] R 0 RSVD RSVD
[1:0] R/W 0 B_AX_HDR_BD_MASK Set 1 to mask Host dispatcher RX BD Valid from PCIE
18608920h R_AX_RX_FUNCTION_STOP
[31:19] R 0 RSVD RSVD
[18] R/W 0 B_AX_CDR_DISABLE_CH2 disable CPU AXIDMA RX CH2
[17] R/W 0 B_AX_CDR_DISABLE_CH1 disable CPU AXIDMA RX CH1
[16] R/W 0 B_AX_CDR_DISABLE_CH0 disable CPU AXIDMA RX CH0
[15:12] R 0 RSVD RSVD
[11] R/W 0 B_AX_HDR_DISABLE_RPQ disable host RX RPQ packet
[10] R/W 0 B_AX_HDR_DISABLE_C2H disable host RX C2H packet
[9] R/W 0 B_AX_HDR_DISABLE_REPORT disable host RX report packet
[8] R/W 0 B_AX_HDR_DISABLE_NORMAL disable host RX normal packet, including AXIDMA TX CH2, WIFI packet, PPDU
[7:2] R 0 RSVD RSVD
[1] R/W 0 B_AX_CDR_RX_STOP Stop CDR RX at IDLE
[0] R/W 0 B_AX_HDR_RX_STOP Stop HDR RX at IDLE

[8] R/W 1 B_AX_WDE_NULLPG_WRPORT NULL page write protection.
If this bit is set to 1, then NULL page can't be written, but can be read. If this bit is set to 0, then NULL page can be written and read

[6] R/W 0 B_AX_WDE_LOCKEN_DLEPIF06 DLE port interface 06 debug error mode function enable. If it is set to 1 and there is error flag from DLE port interface 06, then debug error lock is active to stop FSM of datchn

18608C34h R_AX_WDE_ERR_FLAG_CFG_NUM1
[31] W1C 0 B_AX_WDE_ERR_FLAG_NUM1_VLD Indicate B_AX_WDE_ERR_FLAG_NUM1_MSTIDX and B_AX_ERR_FLAG_NUM1_ISRIDX are valid
[30:28] R 0 RSVD Reserved
[27:24] R 0 B_AX_WDE_ERR_FLAG_NUM1_MSTIDX Bit index for first error flag
[23:21] R 0 RSVD Reserved
[20:16] R 0 B_AX_WDE_ERR_FLAG_NUM1_ISRIDX Master index for first error flag. See sheet "1st_errlfag_idx" for detail

[2] R/W 0 B_AX_WDE_DATCHN_FRZTMR_MODE datchn FSM freeze timer mode selction
0: FSM state is unchanged, 1: FSM is in busy state
[1] R/W 0 B_AX_WDE_QUEMGN_FRZTMR_MODE quemgn FSM freeze timer mode selection
0: FSM state is unchanged, 1: FSM is in busy state
[0] R/W 0 B_AX_WDE_BUFMGN_FRZTMR_MODE bufmgn FSM freeze timer mode selection
0: FSM state is unchanged, 1: FSM is in busy state
18608C38h R_AX_WDE_ERR_IMR
[31:30] R/W 0x3 DUMMY DUMMY
[29] R/W 1 B_AX_WDE_DATCHN_CAMREQ_ERR_INT_EN Error flag mask bit for dle datchn page index cam receoves CAM entry request, but packet ID is existed in CAM
[28] R/W 1 B_AX_WDE_DATCHN_ADRERR_ERR_INT_EN Error flag mask bit for dle datchn d_addr is larger than max page offset supported by HW
[27] R/W 1 B_AX_WDE_DATCHN_RRDY_ERR_INT_EN Error flag mask bit for dle datchn unsupporting read wait state detects d_rvalid=1 & d_rready=0 during read transfer
[26] R/W 1 B_AX_WDE_DATCHN_FRZTO_ERR_INT_EN Error flag mask bit for datchn FSM is frozen over 32us
[25] R/W 1 B_AX_WDE_DATCHN_NULLPG_ERR_INT_EN Error flag mask bit for accessing NULL page
[24] R/W 1 B_AX_WDE_DATCHN_ARBT_ERR_INT_EN Error flag mask bit for d_req is deasserted before transfer is completed
[23:20] R/W 0xF DUMMY DUMMY
[19] R/W 1 B_AX_WDE_QUEMGN_FRZTO_ERR_INT_EN Error flag mask bit for quemgn FSM is frozen over 32us
[18] R/W 1 B_AX_WDE_NXTPKTLL_AD_ERR_INT_EN Error flag mask bit for nxtpktllt access address is error
[17] R/W 1 B_AX_WDE_PREPKTLLT_AD_ERR_INT_EN Error flag mask bit for prepktllt access address is error
[16] R/W 1 B_AX_WDE_ENQ_PKTCNT_NVAL_ERR_INT_EN Error flag mask bit for packet count is negative value during dequeue operation
[15] R/W 1 B_AX_WDE_ENQ_PKTCNT_OVRF_ERR_INT_EN Error flag mask bit for packet count is overflow during enqueue operation
[14] R/W 1 B_AX_WDE_QUE_SRCQUEID_ERR_INT_EN Error flag mask bit for error q_srcprtid, q_srcqueid or q_macid
[13] R/W 1 B_AX_WDE_QUE_DSTQUEID_ERR_INT_EN Error flag mask bit for error q_dstprtid, q_dstqueid or q_macid
[12] R/W 1 B_AX_WDE_QUE_CMDTYPE_ERR_INT_EN Error flag mask bit for error q_cmdtype
[11:10] R/W 0x3 DUMMY DUMMY
[9] R/W 1 B_AX_WDE_BUFMGN_FRZTO_ERR_INT_EN_V1 Error flag mask bit for bufmgn FSM is frozen over 32us
[8] R/W 1 B_AX_WDE_GETNPG_PGOFST_ERR_INT_EN_V1 Error flag mask bit for next page offset is 0 during data pgidxcam do next page index search
[7] R/W 1 B_AX_WDE_GETNPG_STRPG_ERR_INT_EN_V1 Error flag mask bit for start page indx is NULL during data pgidxcam do next page index search
[6] R/W 1 B_AX_WDE_BUFREQ_SRCHTAILPG_ERR_INT_EN_V1 Error flag mask bit for searching tail page index to NULL during buffer request
[5] R/W 1 B_AX_WDE_BUFRTN_SIZE_ERR_INT_EN_V1 Error flag mask bit for return buffer size > current used page number
[4] R/W 1 B_AX_WDE_BUFRTN_INVLD_PKTID_ERR_INT_EN_V1 Error flag mask bit for return buffer packet ID is invalid in pktinfo table
[3] R/W 1 B_AX_WDE_BUFREQ_UNAVAL_ERR_INT_EN_V1 Error flag mask bit for request buffer size > available page number
[2] R/W 1 B_AX_WDE_BUFREQ_SIZELMT_INT_EN Error flag mask bit for bufmgn detects buffer size of buffer request is over HW limitation
[1] R/W 1 B_AX_WDE_BUFREQ_SIZE0_INT_EN Error flag mask bit for bufmgn detects buffer size of buffer request is 0
[0] R/W 1 B_AX_WDE_BUFREQ_QTAID_ERR_INT_EN Error flag mask bit for quota ID of buffer request is error

18608D10h R_AX_WDE_DBG_FUN_INTF_CTL
[31] $W1S 0 B_AX_WDE_DFI_ACTIVE Write 1 to enable debug function interface, and the HW clear it as action is completed.
This bit is 1 to indicate action is not completed, and B_AX_WDE_DFI_DATA is invalid.
[30:20] R/W 0 DUMMY Dummy
[19:16] R/W 0 B_AX_WDE_DFI_TRGSEL Debug function selection.
See sheet "DBG_FUN_INTF" for detail information.
[15:0] R/W 0 B_AX_WDE_DFI_ADDR Debug function input index. E.g. page index, packet ID… etc.
See sheet "DBG_FUN_INTF" for detail information.
18608D14h R_AX_WDE_DBG_FUN_INTF_DATA
[31:0] R/W 0 B_AX_WDE_DFI_DATA Debug function output data.
See sheet "DBG_FUN_INTF" for detail information.

18608D80h R_AX_WDE_Q_STATUS_CFG
[31:4] RO 0 RSVD Reserved
[3] R/W 0 DUMMY Dummy
[2:0] R/W 0 B_AX_WDE_Q_STATUS_SEL Determine B_AX_WDE_Q_STATUS_VAL will show empty status of which MACID
0: MACID31~0 queue empty
1: MACID63~32 queue empty
2: MACID95~64 queue empty
3:MACID127~96 queue empty
else: RSVD
18608D84h R_AX_WDE_Q_STATUS_VAL
[31:0] RO 0xFFFFFFFF B_AX_WDE_Q_STATUS_VAL Indicate the queue of MACID is empty.
1: empty
0: non empty

18609018h R_AX_PLE_DATCHN_CTL
[31:9] R/W 0 DUMMY Dummy
[8] R/W 1 B_AX_PLE_NULLPG_WRPORT NULL page write protection.
If this bit is set to 1, then NULL page can't be written, but can be read. If this bit is set to 0, then NULL page can be written and read
[7:0] R/W 0 DUMMY Dummy
1860901Ch R_AX_PLE_FUNC_RSTCTL
[31:8] R/W 0 DUMMY Dummy
[7] $W1S 0 B_AX_PLE_GEN_PIF07 DLE port interface 07 function reset.
Write 1 to generate 1T sync reset pulse to reset datchn of DLE port interface 07
[6] $W1S 0 B_AX_PLE_GEN_PIF06 DLE port interface 06 function reset.
Write 1 to generate 1T sync reset pulse to reset datchn of DLE port interface 06

[0] $W1S 0 B_AX_PLE_GEN_PIF00 DLE port interface 00 function reset.

18609020h R_AX_PLE_DBGERR_LOCKEN
[31:8] R/W 0 DUMMY Dummy
[7] R/W 0 B_AX_PLE_LOCKEN_DLEPIF07 DLE port interface 07 debug error mode function enable. If it is set to 1 and there is error flag from DLE port interface 07, then debug error lock is active to stop FSM of datchn
[6] R/W 0 B_AX_PLE_LOCKEN_DLEPIF06 DLE port interface 06 debug error mode function enable. If it is set to 1 and there is error flag from DLE port interface 06, then debug error lock is active to stop FSM of datchn
[5] R/W 0 B_AX_PLE_LOCKEN_DLEPIF05 DLE port interface 05 debug error mode function enable. If it is set to 1 and there is error flag from DLE port interface 05, then debug error lock is active to stop FSM of datchn
[4] R/W 0 B_AX_PLE_LOCKEN_DLEPIF04 DLE port interface 04 debug error mode function enable. If it is set to 1 and there is error flag from DLE port interface 04, then debug error lock is active to stop FSM of datchn
[3] R/W 0 B_AX_PLE_LOCKEN_DLEPIF03 DLE port interface 03 debug error mode function enable. If it is set to 1 and there is error flag from DLE port interface 03, then debug error lock is active to stop FSM of datchn
[2] R/W 0 B_AX_PLE_LOCKEN_DLEPIF02 DLE port interface 02 debug error mode function enable. If it is set to 1 and there is error flag from DLE port interface 02, then debug error lock is active to stop FSM of datchn
[1] R/W 0 B_AX_PLE_LOCKEN_DLEPIF01 DLE port interface 01 debug error mode function enable. If it is set to 1 and there is error flag from DLE port interface 01, then debug error lock is active to stop FSM of datchn
[0] R/W 0 B_AX_PLE_LOCKEN_DLEPIF00 DLE port interface 00 debug error mode function enable. If it is set to 1 and there is error flag from DLE port interface 00, then debug error lock is active to stop FSM of datchn

[31:8] R/W 0 DUMMY Dummy

[5] W1C 0 B_AX_PLE_LOCKON_DLEPIF05 Indicate DLE port interface 05 debug error lock status

[3] W1C 0 B_AX_PLE_LOCKON_DLEPIF03 Indicate DLE port interface 03 debug error lock status
[2] W1C 0 B_AX_PLE_LOCKON_DLEPIF02 Indicate DLE port interface 02 debug error lock status

[31:0] R/W 0 DUMMY Dummy
1860902Ch R_AX_PLE_WORD02C
[31:0] R/W 0 DUMMY Dummy
18609030h R_AX_PLE_ERRFLAG_MSG
[31:0] RO 0 B_AX_PLE_ERR_FLAG_MSG Error flag message
18609034h R_AX_PLE_ERR_FLAG_CFG_NUM1
[31] W1C 0 B_AX_PLE_ERR_FLAG_NUM1_VLD Indicate B_AX_PLE_ERR_FLAG_NUM1_MSTIDX and B_AX_ERR_FLAG_NUM1_ISRIDX are valid
[30:28] R 0 RSVD Reserved
[27:24] R 0 B_AX_PLE_ERR_FLAG_NUM1_MSTIDX Bit index for first error flag
[23:21] R 0 RSVD Reserved
[20:16] R 0 B_AX_PLE_ERR_FLAG_NUM1_ISRIDX Master index for first error flag. See sheet "1st_errlfag_idx" for detail
[15:3] R/W 0 DUMMY Dummy
[2] R/W 0 B_AX_PLE_DATCHN_FRZTMR_MODE datchn FSM freeze timer mode selction
0: FSM state is unchanged, 1: FSM is in busy state
[1] R/W 0 B_AX_PLE_QUEMGN_FRZTMR_MODE quemgn FSM freeze timer mode selection
0: FSM state is unchanged, 1: FSM is in busy state
[0] R/W 0 B_AX_PLE_BUFMGN_FRZTMR_MODE bufmgn FSM freeze timer mode selection
0: FSM state is unchanged, 1: FSM is in busy state
18609038h R_AX_PLE_ERR_IMR
[31:30] R/W 0x3 DUMMY Dummy
[29] R/W 1 B_AX_PLE_DATCHN_CAMREQ_ERR_INT_EN Error flag mask bit for dle datchn page index cam receoves CAM entry request, but packet ID is existed in CAM
[28] R/W 1 B_AX_PLE_DATCHN_ADRERR_ERR_INT_EN Error flag mask bit for dle datchn d_addr is larger than max page offset supported by HW
[27] R/W 1 B_AX_PLE_DATCHN_RRDY_ERR_INT_EN Error flag mask bit for dle datchn unsupporting read wait state detects d_rvalid=1 & d_rready=0 during read transfer
[26] R/W 1 B_AX_PLE_DATCHN_FRZTO_ERR_INT_EN Error flag mask bit for datchn FSM is frozen over 32us
[25] R/W 1 B_AX_PLE_DATCHN_NULLPG_ERR_INT_EN Error flag mask bit for accessing NULL page
[24] R/W 1 B_AX_PLE_DATCHN_ARBT_ERR_INT_EN Error flag mask bit for d_req is deasserted before transfer is completed
[23:20] R/W 0xF DUMMY Dummy
[19] R/W 1 B_AX_PLE_QUEMGN_FRZTO_ERR_INT_EN Error flag mask bit for quemgn FSM is frozen over 32us
[18] R/W 1 B_AX_PLE_NXTPKTLL_AD_ERR_INT_EN Error flag mask bit for nxtpktllt access address is error
[17] R/W 1 B_AX_PLE_PREPKTLLT_AD_ERR_INT_EN Error flag mask bit for prepktllt access address is error

18609110h R_AX_PLE_DBG_FUN_INTF_CTL
[31] $W1S 0 B_AX_PLE_DFI_ACTIVE Write 1 to enable debug function interface, and the HW clear it as action is completed.
This bit is 1 to indicate action is not completed, and B_AX_PLE_DFI_DATA is invalid.
[30:20] R/W 0 DUMMY Dummy
[19:16] R/W 0 B_AX_PLE_DFI_TRGSEL Debug function selection.
See sheet "DBG_FUN_INTF" for detail information.
[15:0] R/W 0 B_AX_PLE_DFI_ADDR Debug function input index. E.g. page index, packet ID… etc.
See sheet "DBG_FUN_INTF" for detail information.

18609408h R_AX_WDRLS_CFG
[31:16] R/W 0 RSVD
[15:8] R/W 0x40 B_AX_RLSRPT_BUFREQ_TO PLE_DLE buffer request wait time limit, unit in 32us. If buffer request waiting time is large then this threshold, then HW will assert error interrupt to 0x1860_8504[5]. If this value is set to 0, then wait timer is disabled.
[7:6] R/W 0 B_AX_RLSRPT_BUFREQ_TO_SEL PLE DLE buffer request wait timer unit selection
2'h0: 32us, 2'h1: 256us, 2'h2~2'h3: 1024us
[5:2] R/W 0 RSVD
[1:0] R/W 0 B_AX_WDRLS_MODE 0: PCIE release report + WLAN CPU release report
1: USB/SDIO release report + WLAN CPU release report
2: Only WLAN CPU release report
3: RSVD
1860940Ch R_AX_WDRLS_WORD00C
[31:0] R/W 0 DUMMY Dummy
18609410h R_AX_RLSRPT0_CFG0
[31:28] R/W 0 RSVD
[27:24] R/W 0xF B_AX_RLSRPT0_FLTR_MAP Report generation control for each WDE_DLE release queue.
If bit is set to 1 for report generation, else bypass report generation
[0]: WDE_DLE release queue - TXOK
[1]: WDE_DLE release queue - Drop for retry limit
[2]: WDE_DLE release queue - Drop for lifetime timeout
[3]: WDE_DLE release queue - Drop for MACID drop
[23:20] R/W 0 RSVD
[19:16] R/W 0x7 B_AX_RLSRPT0_PKTTYPE Packet type of Rx descriptor (offset 0 [27:24]) for report type 0
[15:11] R/W 0 RSVD
[10:8] R/W 0 B_AX_RLSRPT0_PID PLE_DLE port ID for report type 0
[7:6] R/W 0 RSVD
[5:0] R/W 0x1 B_AX_RLSRPT0_QID PLE_DLE queue ID for report type 0

[0]: WDE_DLE release queue - TXOK
[1]: WDE_DLE release queue - Drop for retry limit
[2]: WDE_DLE release queue - Drop for lifetime timeout
[3]: WDE_DLE release queue - Drop for MACID drop

[22:20] R/W 0 B_AX_CH_INFO_PRTID The ch info destination queue ID
port id 0 & queue id 2: host
port id 0 & queue id 0xB: wlcpu
[19:18] R/W 1 B_AX_CH_INFO_REQ Clock cycle number for bb_ch_info_req, unit in DMAC clock cycle
2'h0: 1 T
2'h1: 2 T
2’h2: 3 T
[17:16] R/W 0 B_AX_CH_INFO_SEG The channel info MAC segment size.
(including RX descriptor)
0: 128 bytes
1: 256 bytes
2: 512 bytes
3: 1024 bytes
[15:12] R/W 0 B_AX_CH_INFO_INTVL Shortest interval between consequent channel info. request, unit in 1us.
4'h0: disable
4'h1: 1 us
4’h2: 2 us
4’h3: 3 us
4’h4: 4 us
4'h5: 5 us
4'h6: 6 us
4'h7: 7 us
4'h8: 8 us
4'h9: 9 us
4'ha: 10 us
4'hb: 11 us
4'hc: 12 us
4'hd: 13 us
4'he: 14 us
4'hf: 15 us
[11:9] R/W 0 B_AX_GET_CH_INFO_TO Wait time limit selection for bb channel info. data, unit in DMAC clock cycles.
3'h0: disable
3'h1: 4 cycles
3'h2: 8 cycles
3'h3: 12 cycles
3'h4: 16 cycles
3'h5: 20 cycles
3'h6: 24 cycles
3'h7: 28 cycles
[8] R/W 0 B_AX_CH_INFO_PHY 1: The get-channel-info request from PHY1(CMAC1) would trigger get-channel-info operation
0: The get-channel-info request from PHY0(CMAC0) would trigger get-channel-info operation
[7:6] R/W 0 B_AX_CH_INFO_BUF Buffer size selection
2'h0: 128 bytes
2'h1: 256 bytes
2'h2: 512 bytes
2'h3: 1024 bytes
[5] $RO 0 B_AX_CH_INFO_STOP Indicate the channel info module is in the stop state
[4] $R/W 0 B_AX_CH_INFO_STOP_REQ Write 1 to stop channel info module
[3] RO 0 B_AX_CH_INFO_ON Indicate DMAC is in the get-ch-info progress.
[2:1] R/W 0 RSVD
[0] R/W 0 B_AX_CH_INFO_EN Enable ch info function.

1860962Ch R_AX_BBRPT_CHINFO_ERR_ISR
[31:8] RO 0 RSVD Reserved
[7] W1C 0 B_AX_BBPRT_CHIF_TO_ERR_V1 The ch info module does not act after 32 us
[6] W1C 0 B_AX_BBPRT_CHIF_NULL_ERR_V1 The segment is valid but the len = 0
[5] W1c 0 B_AX_BBPRT_CHIF_LEFT2_ERR_V1 Ch info buffer is not empty after action end
[4] W1C 0 B_AX_BBPRT_CHIF_LEFT1_ERR_V1 Ch info buffer is not empty before action start
[3] W1C 0 B_AX_BBPRT_CHIF_HDRL_ERR_V1 Ch info > header segment length
[2] W1C 0 B_AX_BBPRT_CHIF_BOVF_ERR_V1 Data loss due to buffer full. After the event occurs, the following ch info would be dropped.
[1] W1C 0 B_AX_BBPRT_CHIF_OVF_ERR_V1 Ch info segment length is larger than requested report buffer size. After the event occurs, the following ch info would be dropped.
[0] W1C 0 B_AX_BBPRT_CHIF_BB_TO_ERR_V1 Ch info wait timeout. After the event occurs, the following ch info would be dropped.
This error detection enable condition:
R_AX_GET_CH_INFO_TO > 0x0

where TU is defined by B_AX_LA_TRIG_TU_SEL

TU (time unit) = 2^ B_AX_LA_TRIG_TU_SEL

4’h0: 64KB

4’h2: 192KB
4’h3: 256KB
4’h4: 320KB
Misc: RSVD
[15:14] R/W 0 DUMMY dummy
[13:8] R/W 0 B_AX_LA_BUF_BNDY Start boundary of PKTBUFFER for LA ring buffer, unit in 8Kbytes
[7:6] R/W 0 B_AX_LA_TO_VAL Timeout value for 1st la_stop wait timer
2'h0: 1s, 2'h1: 2s, 2'h2: 4s, 2'h3: 8s
[5] R/W 0 B_AX_LA_TO_EN Set 1 to enable wait timer for 1st la_stop
1: enable, 0: disable
[4] R/W 0 B_AX_LA_RESTART_EN Set 1 to allow la_trig to re-strart LA data dump
1: enable, 0: disable
Note: this function is disabled as B_AX_LA_TO_EN=1
[3] $W1S 0 B_AX_LA_TRIG_START Set 1 to trigger LA data dump start
1: Wait to dump start, 0: Start dump
[2:1] R/W 0 DUMMY dummy
[0] R/W 0 B_AX_LA_FEN LA function enable
0: disable, 1: enable

18609A00h R_AX_PKTIN_SETTING
[31:6] RSVD RSVD RSVD Reseved
[5] R/W 0x1 B_AX_ALLQ_TXPKTIN_EN
[4:2] R/W 0x1 B_AX_AMSDU_MAX_FS_NUM The max searching number of AMSDU forward search:
0x0: 0
0x1: 5
0x2: 15
0x3: 25
0x4: 35
0x5: 45
[1] RSVD RSVD RSVD RSVD
[0] R/W 0x0 B_AX_PKTIN_CLK_GATING_DIS Control PKTIN module clk gating disable
0:clok gating
1:disable clock gating
18609A04h R_AX_HWAMSDU_CTRL
[31:5] R/W RSVD RSVD Reseved
[4:3] R/W 0x3 B_AX_MAX_AMSDU_NUM Break condition of max HW AMSDU number
0x0 : 1 msdu
0x1 : 2 msdus
0x2 : 3 msdus
0x3 : 4 msdus
[2] R/W 0x0 B_AX_SINGLE_AMSDU When BIT_SINGLE_AMSDU = 1 and AMSDU field =1 of tx descriptor, the packets will use AMSDU format at packet and more packets. When BIT_SINGLE_AMSDU = 0, hardware will update WD.HWAMSDU to 0, and not generate Single AMSDU packet.
0: Disable
1: Enable
[1] R/W 0x0 B_AX_AMSDU_FS_ENABLE 0: disable AMSDU forward search
1: ebable AMSDU forward search
[0] R/W 0x0 B_AX_HWAMSDU_EN 0: Disable HWAMSDU function
1: Enable HWAMSDU function
18609A08h R_AX_HWAMSDU_STATUS
[31] R/W1C 0x0 B_AX_AMSDU_PKT_SIZE_ERR packet size of AMSDU packet > amsdu_pkt_size_th
[30] R/W1C 0x0 B_AX_AMSDU_EN_ERR Assert when r__hwamsdu_en is disable and WD.AMSDU is enabled in packet (WD Body)
[29] R/W1C 0x0 B_AX_AMSDU_ADDR_INFO_ERR Per pkt last addr info (MSDU_LS , LS)  !=  (1,0)
[28:0] RSVD RSVD RSVD Reserved
18609A14h R_AX_TXPKTIN_CTRL
[31:26] RO 0 rsvd
[25] R/W1C 0 B_AX_C1P4_TXPKTIN_STS TxPktIn interrupt issued for CMAC1 & Port4
[24] R/W1C 0 B_AX_C1P3_TXPKTIN_STS TxPktIn interrupt issued for CMAC1 & Port3
[23] R/W1C 0 B_AX_C1P2_TXPKTIN_STS TxPktIn interrupt issued for CMAC1 & Port2
[22] R/W1C 0 B_AX_C1P1_TXPKTIN_STS TxPktIn interrupt issued for CMAC1 & Port1
[21] R/W1C 0 B_AX_C1P0_TXPKTIN_STS TxPktIn interrupt issued for CMAC1 & Port0
[20] R/W1C 0 B_AX_C0P4_TXPKTIN_STS TxPktIn interrupt issued for CMAC0 & Port4
[19] R/W1C 0 B_AX_C0P3_TXPKTIN_STS TxPktIn interrupt issued for CMAC0 & Port3
[18] R/W1C 0 B_AX_C0P2_TXPKTIN_STS TxPktIn interrupt issued for CMAC0 & Port2
[17] R/W1C 0 B_AX_C0P1_TXPKTIN_STS TxPktIn interrupt issued for CMAC0 & Port1
[16] R/W1C 0 B_AX_C0P0_TXPKTIN_STS TxPktIn interrupt issued for CMAC0 & Port0
[15] R/W 0 B_AX_TXPKTIN_CTRL_EN 1: Enable TXPKTIN generate interrupt
When host sends a packet into PKTIN, PKTIN module check if WDinfo’s QSEL & MULTIPORT_ID match the B_AX_C(m)P(n)_TXPKTIN_En, PKTIN issues interrupt to inform MCU.
0: Disable TXPKTIN generate interrupt
[14:10] RO 0 rsvd
[9] R/W 0 B_AX_C1P4_TXPKTIN_En Enable TxPktIn interrupt source for CMAC1 & Port4
[8] R/W 0 B_AX_C1P3_TXPKTIN_En Enable TxPktIn interrupt source for CMAC1 & Port3
[7] R/W 0 B_AX_C1P2_TXPKTIN_En Enable TxPktIn interrupt source for CMAC1 & Port2
[6] R/W 0 B_AX_C1P1_TXPKTIN_En Enable TxPktIn interrupt source for CMAC1 & Port1
[5] R/W 0 B_AX_C1P0_TXPKTIN_En Enable TxPktIn interrupt source for CMAC1 & Port0
[4] R/W 0 B_AX_C0P4_TXPKTIN_En Enable TxPktIn interrupt source for CMAC0 & Port4
[3] R/W 0 B_AX_C0P3_TXPKTIN_En Enable TxPktIn interrupt source for CMAC0 & Port3
[2] R/W 0 B_AX_C0P2_TXPKTIN_En Enable TxPktIn interrupt source for CMAC0 & Port2
[1] R/W 0 B_AX_C0P1_TXPKTIN_En Enable TxPktIn interrupt source for CMAC0 & Port1
[0] R/W 0 B_AX_C0P0_TXPKTIN_En Enable TxPktIn interrupt source for CMAC0 & Port0

[31:8] RO 0 RSVD Reserved
[7:1] R/W 0 DUMMY
[0] R/W 0 B_AX_PKTIN_GETPKTID_ERR_INT Pktin port has pkt but get pktid =0xfff event
18609B00h R_AX_HDR_SHCUT_SETTING
[31:4] RSVD RSVD RSVD RSVD
[3] RSVD RSVD RSVD 1: emable HW security header
0: disable HW security header
enable header convertor to insert security header if securitry type == CCMP\GCMP\TKIP
[2] R/W 1 B_AX_TX_MAC_MPDU_PROC_EN 1: enable TX MAC header convert
0: disable TX MAC header convert
[1] R/W 1 B_AX_TX_HW_ACK_POLICY_EN 1: enable HW update ACK policy
0: disable HW update ACK policy
[0] R/W 1 B_AX_TX_HW_SEQ_EN 1: enable HW update sequence number
0: disable HW update sequence number
18609B04h R_AX_SHCUT_LLC_ETH_TYPE0
[31:16] R/W 0x3781 B_AX_SHUT_ETH_TYPE1 r_eth_type1, default: 16'h3781
[15:0] R/W 0xF380 B_AX_SHUT_ETH_TYPE0 r_eth_type0, default: 16'hF380
18609B08h R_AX_SHCUT_LLC_ETH_TYPE1
[31:16] R/W RSVD RSVD Reserved
[15:0] R/W 0x0608 B_AX_SHUT_ETH_TYPE2 r_eth_type2, user define, default:0x0608
18609B0Ch R_AX_SHCUT_LLC_OUI0
[31:25] RSVD RSVD RSVD Reserved
[24] R/W 1 B_AX_SHUT_ENABLE_OUI0 r_enable_oui0, default: 1'b1
[23:0] R/W 0xF80000 B_AX_SHUT_ETH_OUI0 r_eth_oui0, default: 24'hF80000
18609B10h R_AX_SHCUT_LLC_OUI1
[31:25] RSVD RSVD RSVD Reserved
[24] R/W 1 B_AX_SHUT_ENABLE_OUI1 r_enable_oui1, default: 1'b1
[23:0] R/W 0xF80000 B_AX_SHUT_ETH_OUI1 r_eth_oui1, default: 24'hF80000
18609B14h R_AX_SHCUT_LLC_OUI2
[31:25] RSVD RSVD RSVD Reserved
[24] R/W 1 B_AX_SHUT_ENABLE_OUI2 r_enable_oui2, default: 1'b1
[23:0] R/W 0xF8 B_AX_SHUT_ETH_OUI2 r_eth_oui2, default: 24'h0000F8
18609B18h R_AX_SHCUT_LLC_OUI3
[31:25] RSVD RSVD RSVD Reserved
[24] R/W 1 B_AX_SHUT_ENABLE_OUI3 r_enable_oui3, default: 1'b1
[23:0] R/W 0 B_AX_SHUT_ETH_OUI3 r_eth_oui3, default: 24'h000000
18609BECh R_AX_TX_PTK_CNT
[31:16] R 0x0 B_AX_TX_PTKOUT_CNT Count value of packet forwarding from MPDU TX Processor
[15:0] R 0x0 B_AX_TX_PKTIN_CNT Count value of packet forwarding to MPDU TX Processor
18609BF0h R_AX_MPDU_TX_ERR_ISR
[31:10] RSVD RSVD RSVD Reserved
[9] W1C 0 B_AX_TX_KSRCH_ERR
[8] W1C 0 B_AX_TX_NW_TYPE_ERR
[7] W1C 0 B_AX_TX_LLC_PRE_ERR
[6] W1C 0 B_AX_TX_ETH_TYPE_ERR
[5] W1C 0 B_AX_TX_HDR3_SIZE_ERR MPDU processor detect a tx packet in 802.3 format with a header length which is less than 14 bytes
[4] W1C 0 B_AX_TX_OFFSET_ERR Offset value for Security Engine is underflow (negative)
[3] W1C 0 B_AX_TX_MPDU_SIZE_ZERO_ERR Get a packet with zero length mpdu (MPDU_desc.MPDU-SIZE == 0) in TX descriptor
[2] W1C 0 B_AX_TX_NXT_ERRPKTID_ERR In USB/SDIO mode get next packet ID from TX descriptor is zero
[1] W1C 0 B_AX_TX_GET_ERRPKTID_ERR Get null packet ID from MPDU TX Queue
[0] RSVD RSVD RSVD Reserved
18609BF4h R_AX_MPDU_TX_ERR_IMR
[31:10] RSVD RSVD RSVD Reserved
[9] R/W 0 B_AX_TX_KSRCH_ERR_EN tx key search fail
[8] R/W 0 B_AX_TX_NW_TYPE_ERR_EN network type is no link
[7] R/W 0 B_AX_TX_LLC_PRE_ERR_EN llc[23:0] != 24’h03AAAA
[6] R/W 0 B_AX_TX_ETH_TYPE_ERR_EN when packet is eth2, type field is less than 1536; when packet is 802.3, length field is larger than 1536
[5] R/W 0 B_AX_TX_HDR3_SIZE_ERR_INT_EN B_AX_TX_HDR3_SIZE_ERR mask bit, set 1 to enable interrupt mask
[4] R/W 0 B_AX_TX_OFFSET_ERR_INT_EN B_AX_TX_OFFSET_ERR mask bit, set 1 to enable interrupt mask
[3] R/W 0 B_AX_TX_MPDU_SIZE_ZERO_INT_EN B_AX_TX_MPDU_SIZE_ZERO_ERR mask bit, set 1 to enable interrupt mask
[2] R/W 0 B_AX_TX_NXT_ERRPKTID_INT_EN B_AX_TX_NXT_ERRPKTID_ERR mask bit, set 1 to enable interrupt mask
[1] R/W 0 B_AX_TX_GET_ERRPKTID_INT_EN B_AX_TX_GET_ERRPKTID_ERR mask bit, set 1 to enable interrupt mask
[0] RSVD RSVD RSVD Reserved
18609BF8h R_AX_MPDU_TX_DBG_V1
[31:9] RSVD RSVD RSVD RSVD
[8] R/W 0 B_AX_MPDU_TX_DBGEN_V1 Enable debug MPDU TX Processor port output
[7:2] RSVD RSVD RSVD
[1] R/W 0 B_AX_MPDU_TX_CKEN_DIS disable mpdu tx processor clock gating
[0] R/W 1 B_AX_MPDU_TX_DLAST_V1 Enable MPDU TX processor to toggle d_last else output low
18609C00h R_AX_MPDU_PROC
[31:30] RO 0 RSVD
[29:24] R/W 0xA B_AX_RPKT_CQID report packet(PPDU status) to WLCPU queue id
[23:22] RO 0 RSVD RSVD
[21:16] R/W 9 B_AX_WPKT_CQID wifi pakcet to WLCPU queue ID
[15] RO 0 RSVD RSVD
[14:12] R/W 0 B_AX_RPKT_CPID report packet(PPDU status) to WLCPU port id
[11] RO 1 RSVD RSVD
[10:8] R/W 0 B_AX_WPKT_CPID wifi pakcet to WLCPU port ID
[7:6] RO 0 RSVD RSVD
[5:4] R/W 0 B_AX_RXFWD_PRIO MPDU process rewrite RX.FWD, 0: host, 1: WLCPU, 2/3:RSVD(default host)
Rx filter and Rx forwarding overwrite issue patch.
[3:2] RO 0 RSVD RSVD
[1] R/W 0 B_AX_A_ICV_ERR Accept ICV Error packets.
1: Accept
0: Reject
[0] R/W 0 B_AX_APPEND_FCS Append FCS. 1: Append, 0: Not append.
18609C04h R_AX_ACTION_FWD0
[31:26] RO 0 RSVD
[25:24] R/W 0 B_AX_FWD_VHT_CBFM When set, VHT compressed beamforming frame will be received.
0: Don't care.
1: To host driver.
2: To wlan CPU.
3: RSVD.

0: Don't care.
1: To host driver.

1: To host driver.

0: Don't care.
1: To host driver.

0: Don't care.
1: To host driver.

0: Don't care.
1: To host driver.
2: To wlan CPU.

0: Don't care.

0: Don't care.

0: Don't care.

[17:16] R/W 0 B_AX_FWD_TF8 When set, frame with Trigger Type=8 will be received.
0: Don't care.
1: To host driver.

[15:14] R/W 0 B_AX_FWD_TF7 When set, frame with Trigger Type=7 will be received.
(NFRP)
0: Don't care.
1: To host driver.
2: To wlan CPU.
3: RSVD.
[13:12] R/W 0 B_AX_FWD_TF6 When set, frame with Trigger Type=6 will be received.
(BQRP)
0: Don't care.
1: To host driver.

0: Don't care.
